Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Looking for an exciting challenge?

Join us on our mission to become a global champion in commerce, advertising and performance marketing.

mrge, a leading Commerce Advertising platform, connects over 5,500 publishers, 55,000 advertisers, and 100 networks in 160+ countries. It combines the expertise of five market leaders: digidip, specializing in premium publishers; MaxBounty, focused on direct partnerships; shopping24, offering product recommendations; SourceKnowledge, a CPC platform; and Yieldkit, delivering high reach and performance. Backed by Waterland private equity, mrge employs over 160 professionals with offices in Hamburg, Berlin, Montreal, and Ottawa.

Our culture runs on trust, friendliness, and collegiality, fostering a positive work atmosphere.

Working with us is easy and fun – just ask our clients, who we help to reach their goals in an uncomplicated, reliable way.

In close cooperation with our partners, we provide advertising formats that offer genuine relevance for our users and, as a result, lead to higher sales.

We would be delighted if you joined us as a Junior Data Analyst!

Your Tasks:

We are seeking a skilled and motivated Jr. Data Analyst to join our Billing & Payments Finance team. In this role, you will analyze data, provide actionable insights, and take hands-on ownership of resolving data discrepancies by directly coordinating with our external partners.


Analyze complex financial data sets using SQL and spreadsheets to support daily billing operations, with a strong focus on transaction matching and payment reconciliation.
Cross-reference and reconcile data across different platforms, actively identifying inconsistencies and driving their resolution by communicating directly with external partners.
Design and maintain clear, informative reports and dashboards that empower stakeholders to understand the data and make actionable business decisions.
Translate technical findings into clear business insights, communicating effectively with both technical teams and non-technical finance stakeholders.


Your Profile:


Strong knowledge of SQL for independent data extraction and querying.
Advanced skills in Excel and Google Sheets for ad-hoc analysis, data cleaning, and reporting.
Basic knowledge of a programming language (such as Python or R).
Familiarity with, or a strong willingness to learn, modern data environments like Databricks.
Finance Knowledge: Basic understanding of financial operations, particularly accounts receivable (AR), payment reconciliation, and managing open items will be a plus.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to think critically, ensure high data accuracy and make data-driven decisions.
Strong communication and teamwork skills, with the ability to collaborate and communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.


Your Opportunity:


Take an active role in shaping an innovative, growth-orientated international corporate group from its early stages; seize the opportunity to become irreplaceable!
Take on responsibility as you become an important part of a young, ambitious, and friendly team with a shared desire to develop its skills
Enjoy opportunities to work independently, with flexible hours
Take your skills, talents, and career to the next level as you grow with us


About Us

A global champion for Commerce Advertising with the vibrant energy of a start-up? A high-tech platform that’s fun to work with – for both employees and clients? A multi-location based company with flat hierarchies, run by an international, multilingual team? Yes, that’s us! At mrge, we connect publishers, advertisers, smart tools and formats to drive additional revenue. Now we are excited about connecting with you.

Are you ready to grow? Then join us on our mission to expand mrge and its entities digidip, shopping24 and Yieldkit through fresh ideas, great talent and true commitment. We’re looking forward to getting to know you!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
This isn’t your regular job. Almedia is a place where those who want to push harder can accelerate their careers faster than anywhere else. We’re aiming to become Germany’s second bootstrapped unicorn. Almedia is already Europe’s #3 fastest-growing company in 2025 (FT1000).

We are building the future of marketing by rewarding our community of over 70 million users for engaging with our advertisers’ products. We are offering a new way to acquire users for the biggest companies in the world.

We are seeking a Data Analyst with a passion for uncovering insights that drive growth. This pivotal role is for a highly analytical individual who will directly influence our product, rewards, marketing, and user acquisition strategies through deep dives into user behaviour and advanced predictive modelling.

Join us and become a central force in optimising our user acquisition and incentivisation, for maximising Lifetime Value (LTV) for millions of users.

What You'll Do 🎯


Focus on Causal Analysis: Quantify the causal impact of monetary incentives and product features on user retention. Identify the key behavioural drivers that lead to long-term LTV growth across Freecash and Link.
Design, Run and Analyse A/B tests: Own the end-to-end experimentation lifecycle (design, power analysis, execution, and interpretation). Use A/B and multivariate testing to validate causal hypotheses and drive measurable incremental growth, across different domains (product, rewards, UA) to drive incremental outcomes for business top line and bottom line.
Drive Predictive Analytics: Focus on analysing user behaviour and marketing activities to predict key metrics like Return on Ad Spend, identifying high-value user segments and opportunities for growth.
Deep User Segmentation: Lead in developing and applying advanced user segmentation models, providing actionable insights that inform product development, marketing strategies, and personalised user experiences.
Performance Optimisation & Strategy: Partner closely with Engineering, Product, and User Acquisition teams to interpret complex performance data, translate findings into strategic recommendations, and directly influence optimisation efforts.
Advanced Data Analysis & Modelling: Utilise strong SQL and Python skills to conduct sophisticated data analysis, build robust analytical models, and maintain relevant data structures that support predictive insights.
Insights Communication & Enablement: Transform complex analytical findings into clear, compelling narratives and actionable recommendations for stakeholders. Mentor and train teams on how to leverage these insights effectively, moving beyond basic reporting.


What You'll Bring 💡


Extensive experience (typically 4+ years) in a data analysis role with a strong focus on business performance, user behaviour, and growth metrics.
Proven experience with technical data analysis, statistics, A/B testing, data modelling and evaluation of algorithmic data solutions.
Exceptional proficiency in SQL and Python for complex data manipulation, analysis, and statistical modelling.
Strong understanding of data warehousing concepts and data modelling principles relevant to analytical outcomes.
Experience in collaborating closely with marketing, product, and data science teams to drive measurable business impact.
Ability to communicate complex analytical findings clearly and concisely to diverse audiences, influencing data-driven decisions.


What Makes You a Great Fit 🌟


A Predictive Mindset: You are passionate about uncovering future trends and user behaviour through data, using insights to drive proactive strategies.
Impact-Driven & Strategic: You're not just reporting numbers; you're using them to influence key business decisions and directly contribute to revenue and user growth.
Collaborative Analytical Partner: You excel at translating complex data into actionable strategies for business stakeholders, thriving in a cross-functional environment.


Why Almedia? 🎁


Own Our Growth: We offer all Berlin-based employees equity in Almedia to truly be a part of our success.
Scale With Almedia: Grow alongside a startup that has been profitable from day one.
Central Berlin Office: Work from a fully-stocked modern office built for collaboration, accessible from all around Berlin.
Other Benefits: Transport subsidy, breakfasts and lunches, language learning, Urban Sports Club, and more.
We Listen: We regularly add to our benefits through rigorous employee feedback.


We believe in fostering talent, evaluating all skill levels during the hiring process, and providing a clear path for growth. Almedia is an equal opportunity employer. We embrace and celebrate diversity, and encourage individuals from all backgrounds to apply.

Title: (Junior) Data Analyst (m/w/d) Data Analytics
Description:
Unser Angebot


Freuen Sie sich auf eine leistungsorientierte Vergütung, flexible Arbeitszeiten und die Möglichkeit, im Homeoffice mit aktuellen Technologien virtuell zusammenzuarbeiten.
Wir wünschen uns, dass Sie mit uns wachsen. Dabei unterstützen und begleiten wir Ihre fachliche und persönliche Entwicklung zielgerichtet und gestalten mit Ihnen zusammen Ihren persönlichen Karriereweg.
Es erwarten Sie ein kollegiales Betriebsklima, zahlreiche Annehmlichkeiten für den beruflichen Alltag sowie verschiedene Rahmenbedingungen, mit denen wir die Vereinbarkeit von Familie und Beruf unterstützen.


Ihre Aufgaben


Sie arbeiten flexibel in interdisziplinären Projektteams aus Kunden- und Syncwork-Mitarbeitenden, direkt bei unseren Kunden vor Ort, von unserem Office bzw. Homeoffice aus.
Sie managen und analysieren große Datenmengen und entwickeln intelligente Algorithmen.
Sie unterstützen bei der Analyse, dem Design und Prototyping sowie bei der Realisierung und dem Rollout innovativer Reporting- und Planungslösungen zur Unternehmenssteuerung mithilfe von Business Intelligence.
Sie konzipieren und realisieren innovative Lösungen im Bereich Industrie 4.0, Advanced Analytics, Machine Learning, Big Data oder Artificial Intelligence.
Sie analysieren Strategien, Organisations- und Kostenstrukturen sowie Prozesse.


Ihr Profil


Sie haben Ihr Studium der Wirtschaftswissenschaften (BWL, VWL), (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, des (Wirtschafts-)Ingenieurwesens, der Mathematik, ein vergleichbares Studium oder Ihre Ausbildung mit bankfachlichem Hintergrund erfolgreich abgeschlossen.
Sie punkten idealerweise mit praktischen Erfahrungen im Bereich Big Data, Advanced und Data Analytics und haben eine Leidenschaft für neue Technologien und digitale Trends.
Sie besitzen vorteilhafterweise Kenntnisse in Programmiersprachen (z. B. R, Python), Datenbanksystemen (z. B. SQL) oder Cloud-Technologien (MS Azure, IBM Bluemix).
Sie denken analytisch und gehen Aufgaben systematisch und strukturiert an.
Sie verfügen über gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse.
Sie wollen durch Ihren Einsatz zum Projekterfolg beitragen und verkörpern unsere durch Teamgeist, Flexibilität und Erfolgswillen geprägte Unternehmenskultur.
Kommunikationsstärke und Eigeninitiative runden Ihr Profil ab.

Title: Data Analyst ( M/F/D )
Description:
As Data Analyst, you play a key role in driving data-informed decision-making across one of LYNX’s most strategic product and business domains.

We are looking for four Data Analysts to join one of our core squads: Onboarding, Demand Capture, Client Experience, or Client & Product Integrity.


Onboarding Squad: Analyze and improve the customer onboarding journey to make it faster, smoother, and more efficient.
Demand Capture Squad: Use data to understand customer interest and help turn prospects into qualified leads and customers.
Client Experience Squad: Analyze client behavior, feedback, and platform usage to improve the trading experience, increase loyalty, and reduce churn.
Client and Product Integrity Squad: Support smarter, data-driven compliance and due diligence processes tailored to client needs and risk profiles.


In this role, you will help turn data into actionable insights that improve client journeys, optimize commercial performance, strengthen compliance processes, and support better business decisions.

You will own the full lifecycle of data-driven discovery, from connecting and exploring data to modelling, visualizing, and deploying solutions. Your work will directly influence how we grow, serve, and protect our clients.

What You’ll Do


End-to-End Analytics Ownership: Own the full process of data-driven discovery, from sourcing and exploring data to modelling, dashboarding, and delivering scalable solutions.
Business Problem Solving: Translate complex business challenges into practical, data-based solutions that create measurable impact.
Dashboard & KPI Development: Build dashboards, reporting frameworks, and tracking models that support decision-making and performance management over time.
Stakeholder Partnership: Work closely with Product, Marketing, Operations, Compliance, and Commercial teams to understand priorities and turn questions into insights.
Data Exploration & Insight Generation: Analyze structured and unstructured datasets to identify patterns, risks, opportunities, and growth levers.
Experimentation & Optimization: Support testing, experimentation, and continuous improvement initiatives with strong analytical input.
Data Quality & Governance: Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and reliability across reporting and analytics outputs.
Squad Impact Contribution: Depending on your squad, contribute to improving onboarding flows, lead conversion, client loyalty, churn reduction, or smarter compliance processes.﻿


Who You Are

You are a curious and proactive analyst who enjoys solving business problems with data. You combine technical skills with commercial awareness and communicate insights in a clear and practical way.


You have strong analytical skills and confidence working with structured and unstructured data
You have solid Python and SQL skills, including advanced querying
You have a proactive, self-driven mindset and take ownership of your work
You communicate clearly and can explain complex topics in a simple way
You have a high standard for quality and strong attention to detail
You are comfortable working with cross-functional stakeholders
You are motivated by continuous improvement and measurable impact


Nice to have


Knowledge of financial markets and products such as options and futures
Experience in fintech, brokerage, or other data-rich environments
Experience with BI tools and dashboarding platforms
Experience working in agile product squads


What Do We Offer


A competitive salary based on your educational background and professional experience
Free lunch prepared by our in-house cook, plus fresh juices and fruit
Plenty of room to grow, including a yearly training budget of €2,000
Team trips and social activities with colleagues
A relaxed and informal working environment
Discounted membership with Urban Sports Club
An additional contribution to your company pension scheme
In-house German and English courses with a native speaker, with 50% of the cost covered


Who We Are

“LYNX helps you invest in yourself.”

“Working at LYNX means joining the LYNX family.”

“The development opportunities make LYNX the best place to work.”

This is what employees say about LYNX — and it reflects exactly what we aim to offer: an environment where you can get the best out of yourself.

From our offices in Amsterdam, Ghent, Berlin, and Prague, we work every day to provide private and professional clients with the best trading opportunities through a fast, user-friendly, and reliable platform.

LYNX is an international FinTech company where around 300 colleagues contribute to client success across Europe in an informal, collaborative, and growth-oriented environment.

We're an equal-opportunity employer. We welcome applications from all suitably qualified and eligible candidates without attention to gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation/identity, disability or neurodiversity.

Title: Data Analyst - (Mid or Senior level )(F/M/X)
Description:
About us

At ablefy, we believe passion can turn into a career for anyone. With ablefy.io, we give coaches, consultants, creators, and entrepreneurs everything they need to build and sell digital products online — simply and efficiently. 70,000+ entrepreneurs already trust us to power their business.




Born in 2015, ablefy has grown into a true all-in-one solution — courses, memberships, payments, e-ticketing, and more. We take care of the hard stuff, so our users can focus on what truly matters: growing their business.




Today we're the #1 platform for coaches, consultants, speakers, and service providers in the DACH region — and we're not slowing down. That's where you come in.




Our mission; to build software that empowers everyone to start, run and grow a successful digital business - borderless, is supported by our high performance principles that are crucial to our success:

Radical Collaboration
Constructive confrontation
Adaptive execution
Collective ambition
Evolving expertise
Empathetic leadership




About the role

Data is at the core of how we build products at ablefy — not only to guide our own decisions, but also as a value we deliver to our customers. As a Data Analyst, you will play a key role in shaping both our internal analytics and the customer-facing data products we bring to market. This includes delivering business-critical insights and reports, working on our AI-powered self-service analytics agent and the intelligence layer that powers agentic, data-driven product recommendations for our customers.




You will work closely with cross-functional stakeholders to define priorities and deliver end-to-end data projects. You will also stay close to customer needs and feedback to shape impactful data products — ensuring that data and AI-driven insights deliver maximum impact.




Responsibilities

Take responsibility for key analytics initiatives across the business, owning projects end-to-end and ensuring insights are translated into decisions.
Work closely with internal teams to understand business and customer workflows, challenges & goals, and translate these into meaningful analytics solutions.
Design and build customer-facing data products — from embedded dashboards and reports to AI-based self-service analytics tools and our intelligence layer — empowering internal stakeholders and our customers to make smarter, data-driven decisions more efficiently.
Collaborate with senior leadership and stakeholders to define and track KPIs, assess opportunities, and prioritize projects with the largest impact.
Drive experimentation and deep quantitative analysis — from A/B testing to customer behavior deep dives — to support product strategy and feature prioritization.
Contribute to the development of our data warehouse and ensure scalable, consistent metrics across the company.




Qualifications

Strong quantitative background and 3-5 years of relevant experience as a Data Analyst.
Proven experience with product analytics and experimentation.
Exposure to AI-driven analytics products or LLM applications (e.g. self-service analytics agents, chatbots and dashboarding tools). Hands-on experience is a plus, but curiosity and the ability to learn are essential.
Experience with product and business event tracking — including defining business events, understanding product flows, and identifying the signals that drive meaningful analytics.
Familiarity with AI-assisted development tools (e.g. Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, or similar) and an interest in applying them to build and iterate on data pipelines, models, and analytics products more efficiently.
Great knowledge of SQL, data modeling, and data warehouse concepts.
Proficiency in Python for analytics and statistics.
Strong communication skills and commercial awareness — able to simplify complex insights, prioritize based on customer and business impact, and manage diverse stakeholders across Product, Engineering and Finance.
Experience building or contributing to customer-facing data products is a plus (embedded analytics, data APIs, or AI-based self-service tools).

HTML:
About us

At ablefy, we believe passion can turn into a career for anyone. With ablefy.io, we give coaches, consultants, creators, and entrepreneurs everything they need to build and sell digital products online — simply and efficiently. 70,000+ entrepreneurs already trust us to power their business.


Born in 2015, ablefy has grown into a true all-in-one solution — courses, memberships, payments, e-ticketing, and more. We take care of the hard stuff, so our users can focus on what truly matters: growing their business.


Today we're the #1 platform for coaches, consultants, speakers, and service providers in the DACH region — and we're not slowing down. That's where you come in.


Our mission; to build software that empowers everyone to start, run and grow a successful digital business - borderless, is supported by our high performance principles that are crucial to our success:

  • Radical Collaboration
  • Constructive confrontation
  • Adaptive execution
  • Collective ambition
  • Evolving expertise
  • Empathetic leadership


About the role

Data is at the core of how we build products at ablefy — not only to guide our own decisions, but also as a value we deliver to our customers. As a Data Analyst, you will play a key role in shaping both our internal analytics and the customer-facing data products we bring to market. This includes delivering business-critical insights and reports, working on our AI-powered self-service analytics agent and the intelligence layer that powers agentic, data-driven product recommendations for our customers.


You will work closely with cross-functional stakeholders to define priorities and deliver end-to-end data projects. You will also stay close to customer needs and feedback to shape impactful data products — ensuring that data and AI-driven insights deliver maximum impact.


Responsibilities

  • Take responsibility for key analytics initiatives across the business, owning projects end-to-end and ensuring insights are translated into decisions.
  • Work closely with internal teams to understand business and customer workflows, challenges & goals, and translate these into meaningful analytics solutions.
  • Design and build customer-facing data products — from embedded dashboards and reports to AI-based self-service analytics tools and our intelligence layer — empowering internal stakeholders and our customers to make smarter, data-driven decisions more efficiently.
  • Collaborate with senior leadership and stakeholders to define and track KPIs, assess opportunities, and prioritize projects with the largest impact.
  • Drive experimentation and deep quantitative analysis — from A/B testing to customer behavior deep dives — to support product strategy and feature prioritization.
  • Contribute to the development of our data warehouse and ensure scalable, consistent metrics across the company.


Qualifications

  • Strong quantitative background and 3-5 years of relevant experience as a Data Analyst.
  • Proven experience with product analytics and experimentation.
  • Exposure to AI-driven analytics products or LLM applications (e.g. self-service analytics agents, chatbots and dashboarding tools). Hands-on experience is a plus, but curiosity and the ability to learn are essential.
  • Experience with product and business event tracking — including defining business events, understanding product flows, and identifying the signals that drive meaningful analytics.
  • Familiarity with AI-assisted development tools (e.g. Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, or similar) and an interest in applying them to build and iterate on data pipelines, models, and analytics products more efficiently.
  • Great knowledge of SQL, data modeling, and data warehouse concepts.
  • Proficiency in Python for analytics and statistics.
  • Strong communication skills and commercial awareness — able to simplify complex insights, prioritize based on customer and business impact, and manage diverse stakeholders across Product, Engineering and Finance.
  • Experience building or contributing to customer-facing data products is a plus (embedded analytics, data APIs, or AI-based self-service tools).
  • Experience in fintech domains — such as payments, PSPs and checkout is a plus.
  • Fluent in English; German is a plus.


Benefits

  • Paid time off – 30 vacation days.
  • A competitive salary.
  • A personal training and development budget of €1,500 per year.
  • Responsibility and trust from day one.
  • Individual coaching and further training to support you in your development.
  • A free BVG ticket.
  • Employee discounts at numerous companies (e.g. at the gym or in online stores).
  • Pension program with company match up to 30% of employee contributions.
  • A culture of equity and inclusion where you are appreciated for the person you are.

Title: Product Data Analyst
Description:
Location: We are flexible! Come and join us either in Hamburg, Berlin or Barcelona.

Freenow empowers smarter mobility decisions helping people to move freely and cities to thrive.

We are looking for a Product Data Analyst (m/f/d) to collaborate with our product squads, using data to build a flawless product experience. You will join the Product Data Team and work closely with PMs, designers, UX researchers, developers, and other data functions to discover new product opportunities, validate hypotheses, and launch valuable features to our users.

Be ready to work in a multinational, diverse, highly motivated and collaborative team of passionate analysts who strive for excellence and like to have fun. Are you ready for your next ride?

YOUR DAILY ADVENTURES WILL INCLUDE:


Providing expertise and collaborating with stakeholders to generate new product feature ideas and improvements, and also estimating the potential impact of planned projects.
Continuously engaging with the product squads to gain an understanding of the problems that need to be solved, gathering requirements, and defining the best deliverables to match the business needs.
Having full ownership of your analysis, from ideation to communication of results and making recommendations to all levels of the company including senior management and prioritization committees.
Developing and evaluating A/B test experiments or other analyses to measure and report the performance of released features, ensuring that we are launching the correct changes and features in our apps.
Working closely with our developers and data engineers to create and capture data that is aligned with the business logic and allows modeling and analysis.
Being involved throughout the complete product lifecycle in order to guarantee a data-driven process.
Bringing new insights and championing them using your analyses, dashboards, and good storytelling! We value ideas over hierarchy, and there's always a new path to follow if your idea is convincing and well-backed with data.
You will have the opportunity to steer our products, which kind of features should be developed, and which changes should be made to solve hundreds of thousands of users' daily needs.


TO BE SUCCESSFUL IN THIS ROLE:


You have curiosity in your essence, which motivates you to answer WHY things happened, not only scratching the surface, but aiming for well-founded actionable results
Degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, Computer Science, Engineering, or other fields with a strong mathematical-statistical background
3+ years experience in working as a Data Analyst in a data-driven analytics environment, preferably within tech product organizations, having direct contact with stakeholders.
Exceptional analytical skills, lateral thinking, and proven experience in solving highly challenging problems.
Very good knowledge of SQL, databases, and statistical programming tools (e.g. Python, R) so that exploratory analyses can be performed, reports created and ETLs maintained
Good understanding and hands-on experience in analyzing and interpreting A/B test results, backed by statistical knowledge.
Experience in working with data visualisation tools (e.g.Tableau, Qlikview) and event tracking (e.g. Mixpanel, GA, Firebase)
You are able to communicate findings in a concise and clear manner to a wide variety of stakeholders to support data-driven decision making by using data visualisation and storytelling and have excellent English communication skills.


BENEFITS & PERKS IN A NUTSHELL:


Flexible working arrangements
LinkedIn Learning
Sabbatical & special leave policies
WeRoad partnership
Birthday, 24th + 31st December off
Short term EU work policy
Mobility Credit
Health Insurance
Employee assistance program


Plus more local benefits depending on your work location!

DIVERSITY, EQUITY & INCLUSION:

Freenow is an equal opportunity employer and we consider qualified applicants regardless of race, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability or age.

We want you to grow and evolve, bring your true self to work.

ABOUT US:

Freenow by Lyft empowers smarter mobility decisions, helping people to move freely and cities to thrive. Through our multi-mobility app, we feature broad options for everyone across 9 European markets and over 180 cities. Millions of passengers can access services including taxis, private hire vehicles, carsharing, car rental, e-scooters, e-bikes, e-mopeds, and public transport within a single app.

In July 2025 Freenow was acquired by Lyft, a global mobility platform, which connects riders and drivers for billions of rides across North America and Europe. Together, Freenow and Lyft are creating a more caring and connected world, with transportation for everyone. We are a global, diverse, highly motivated, and collaborative team that strives for excellence and likes to have fun. Ready for your next ride?

Title: BI / Data Analyst (m/w/d)
Description:
BI / Data Analyst (m/w/d) bei Legal Hero GmbH

Anforderungen

Bitte beachte, dass für diese Stelle eine Arbeitserlaubnis für Deutschland erforderlich ist.

Sprache: Deutsch - Fließend


Mehrjährige Erfahrung in BI, Analytics Engineering oder Data Analytics
Sehr gute SQL-Kenntnisse und produktive Erfahrung mit dbt
Erfahrung mit Databricks, Snowflake oder BigQuery
Sicherer Umgang mit modernen BI-Tools
Kommunikationsstärke im Austausch mit Business- und Management-Stakeholdern
Sehr gutes Englisch sowie Deutschkenntnisse auf C1-Niveau oder Bereitschaft, dieses Niveau zu erreichen


Tätigkeiten


Aufbau und Pflege von dbt-Modellen in Databricks
Etablierung einer Single Source of Truth für Finance- und Operations-Kennzahlen
Aufbau einer KPI-Governance inklusive Definitionen, Verantwortlichkeiten und Data Lineage
Entwicklung von Metabase-Dashboards für Management, Finance sowie Strategy & Operations
Unterstützung und Steuerung der Migration von Tableau zu Metabase
Sicherstellung der Datenqualität durch Alerts, Schema-Checks und Data-Quality-Tests
Regelmäßige Abstimmungen mit Finance, StratOps und Management
Übersetzung von Business-Fragen in belastbare Analysen und KPIs
Unterstützung bei Compliance-Themen wie DSAR, Löschkonzept und VVZ-Mapping


Über das Unternehmen

Legalhero ist die moderne Plattform für Rechtsschutzversicherungen und Anwälte. Wir verfolgen das ehrgeizige Ziel, die Schadensfallabwicklung von Rechtsschutzversicherungen zu revolutionieren. Dazu integrieren wir uns direkt in die Systeme der Versicherer. Unsere Partneranwälte wickeln die Fälle hocheffizient und kundenorientiert auf unserer Tech-Plattform mit Hilfe von KI ab. Legalhero wurde 2017 gegründet und hat bis heute hunderttausenden von Versicherungsnehmern geholfen. Unsere ca. 90 Kollegen sitzen in Berlin, nahe dem Gleisdreieck.

Ein inklusives, gleichberechtigtes und diskriminierungsfreies Arbeitsumfeld ist für uns selbstverständlich. Wenn du eine Beeinträchtigung oder spezielle Bedürfnisse haben solltest und während des Bewerbungsprozesses spezielle Unterstützung benötigst, teile es uns gerne mit.

Klicke auf "Bewerben" für Details zu den Benefits, dem Team und dem Bewerbungsprozess.

Title: Data Analyst (m/w/d)
HTML:
Starte in unserer Bio-Molkerei!

Bist du bereit für einen Neuanfang? Wir bieten dir die Chance, in der spannenden Welt der Bio-Molkerei Fuß zu fassen. Werde Teil unseres Teams und gestalte deine Zukunft mit uns!

Zur Weiterentwicklung unseres Controlling Teams suchen wir eine/n Data Analyst (m/w/d)

Deine spannenden Aufgabenfelder:

  • Aktive digitale Weiterentwicklung des Controllings unter Einbringung moderner Technologien wie Data Analytics und Künstliche Intelligenz in die Prozesse;
  • Mitwirkung bei der Digitalisierung und Automatisierung von Prozessen;
  • Aufbau datengetriebener Entscheidungsgrundlagen mittels Advanced Analytics;
  • Unterstützung und Leitung von Digitalisierungs- und Optimierungsprojekten;
  • Erstellen und Analysieren von Kennzahlen zur Steuerung und Optimierung der Fertigung;
  • Durchführung von Soll-Ist-Vergleichen und Abweichungsanalysen, inkl. Ableitung konkreter Handlungsempfehlungen;
  • Pflege und Weiterentwicklung von Controlling-Tools und Dashboards (z. B. in Sopra, Power BI oder vergleichbaren Systemen);
  • Unterstützung bei der Budgetplanung, Forecasts und Investitionsrechnungen für Produktionsprojekte;
  • Fungieren als Bindeglied zwischen Controlling und Management.

Deine fachlichen und persönlichen Stärken:

  • Abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich (Wirtschafts)informatik, Data Science, Künstliche Intelligenz, Data Analytics, Mathematik, Statistik, Wirtschaftsingenieurwesen oder vergleichbare Qualifikation;
  • Grundverständnis von KI, Machine Learning und datengetriebenen Analysen;
  • Erfahrung in Digitalisierung von Geschäftsprozessen;
  • Kenntnisse im Projektmanagement;
  • Fähigkeit, Digitalisierungsinitiativen eigenständig voranzutreiben;
  • Kenntnisse in Kostenrechnung, Produktionsprozessen und Fertigungssteuerung;
  • Sicherer Umgang mit ERP-Systemen und Reporting-Tools wie Power BI;
  • Analytische Stärke, gepaart mit der Fähigkeit, komplexe Sachverhalte verständlich zu kommunizieren;
  • Proaktive, lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise und Freude an der Zusammenarbeit mit interdisziplinären Teams;
  • Strategisches Denken sowie eine unternehmerische Arbeitsweise;
  • Hohe Eigeninitiative und Freude daran, Themen voranzutreiben.

Was wir dir bieten:

  • Ein familiär geprägtes Umfeld mit flachen Hierarchien und kurzen Entscheidungswegen und WIR-Kultur;
  • Eine authentische, offene und wertschätzende Unternehmenskultur, mit regelmäßigen Feedback- und Entwicklungsgesprächen, während und nach der Probezeit, und Entwicklungspläne;
  • Ein unbefristetes Arbeitsverhältnis sowie abwechslungsreiche Aufgaben
  • Ein Arbeitsumfeld, in dem du gestalten, Verantwortung übernehmen und etwas bewegen kannst;
  • Mobiles Arbeiten (40 %) sowie Gleitzeit in der Verwaltung;
  • 29 Tage Urlaub, plus den 24.12. und 31.12. frei, steuerfreie Zuschläge im gewerblichen Bereich, Prämien für die Anwerbung neuer Kolleginnen und Kollegen;
  • Du erhältst einen monatlichen steuerfreien Zuschuss auf deine Sachbezugswertkarte in Höhe von 50,00 Euro netto zum Einkaufen, sowie eine jährliche Förderung für präventive Gesundheitsmaßnahmen;
  • Jobbike-Leasing, Corporate Benefits, BAV und eine quartalsweise Anwesenheitsprämien von bis zu 1.200 Euro brutto im Jahr;
  • Bei Krankheit des Kindes stocken wir das Krankengeld der gesetzlichen Krankenkasse (60 %) auf.

Deine Ansprechpartnerin bei Rückfragen:

Du hast Lust, das Team der Gläsernen Molkerei zu verstärken? Wir freuen uns auf deine Bewerbung mit Angabe deines Gehaltswunschs und deines frühestmöglichen Eintrittsdatums.

Wenn du Fragen vorab hast steht dir Nadine Reimann (Leitung Personal & Recht) unter +49 38873 3379-24 als Ansprechpartnerin zur Verfügung.

Title: AI Fact-Checking Assistant
Description:
About The Role
The AI Fact-Checking Assistant identifies and corrects inaccuracies within our advisory reports. You will work alongside our senior advisors to ensure that every sentence in our written counsel is supported by verifiable evidence. This position exists to bridge the gap between AI-assisted research and the rigorous standards required for board-level decision making.
This is a full-time, permanent position designed for someone early in their career. You will spend your time auditing technical summaries, verifying citations, and checking the logic of drafts. In a firm that relies entirely on the written word, your role is to protect the integrity of the record.

About JHC Consulting
JHC Consulting is a private advisory practice. we provide counsel to chief executives and board committees regarding structural reorganizations and governance transitions. Our firm operates on a specific philosophy: we do not use slide decks or facilitate consensus sessions. We produce authored memoranda that remain on file as a permanent record of a decision. These documents are usually seven to ten pages long and are signed by a single individual. This approach ensures analytical honesty and accountability. Because our clients rely on these reports for high-stakes decisions, our writing must be precise and every fact must be absolute.

What You Will Do
Verify each data point and statistic mentioned in a drafted memorandum against primary sources.
Audit citations to ensure that every quote and reference is attributed correctly and used in context.
Review AI-generated research summaries to identify hallucinations or subtle misinterpretations of source material.
Examine public filings, corporate records, and regulatory documents to confirm timelines of executive actions.
Draft brief, evidence-based notes for senior advisors when a drafted claim cannot be verified.
Maintain a logical filing system for all source materials used to support a specific memorandum.
Check the formatting of footnotes and references to ensure they meet our internal standard for clarity.


What we Need From You
We look for individuals with a skeptical mind and an eye for small details. You should find satisfaction in catching a misattributed quote or an incorrect date that others might miss. You must be able to read dense material and summarize it with perfect accuracy. We value people who prefer working independently and focusing on a single document for several hours at a time. This role requires the ability to explain why a fact is incorrect without using emotional language. High performance in this role is measured by the clarity and accuracy of the final document. You do not need years of corporate experience, but you must be able to demonstrate a high standard for your own research and writing.

Working At JHC Consulting
Work at our firm is conducted entirely in a remote environment. We prioritize deep work and do not fill your schedule with regular video calls or meetings. You will have a clear set of tasks for the day and the autonomy to complete them. Senior advisors provide direct feedback on your research and help you develop your analytical skills. We maintain a quiet and disciplined atmosphere where the quality of the thought is the primary metric for success. Intellectual honesty is expected in every communication.

What We Offer
Full-time salary and permanent employment status.
A fully remote work schedule with no requirement for travel.
Direct mentorship from senior advisors in a specialized field.
Exposure to complex governance and strategic questions at the highest level.
An environment that protects your time for focused, deep work.
Access to a wide range of professional research databases and tools.


JHC Consulting remains committed to the written record as the highest form of professional counsel. We believe that analytical honesty is the only way to help leaders make consequential decisions. Your work ensures that our documents provide a stable foundation for the future of the organizations we serve.

Title: Freelance Data Science Engineer (Python & SQL)
Description:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:


Design original computational data science problems that simulate real-world analytical workflows across industries (telecom, finance, government, e-commerce, healthcare)
Create problems requiring Python programming to solve (using Pandas, Numpy, Scipy, Sklearn, Statsmodels, Matplotlib, Seaborn)
Ensure problems are computationally intensive and cannot be solved manually within reasonable timeframes (days/weeks)
Develop problems requiring non-trivial reasoning chains in data processing, statistical analysis, feature engineering, predictive modeling, and insight extraction
Create deterministic problems with reproducible answers: avoid stochastic elements or require fixed random seeds for exact reproducibility
Base problems on real business challenges: customer analytics, risk assessment, fraud detection, forecasting, optimization, and operational efficiency
Design end-to-end problems spanning the complete data science pipeline (data ingestion → cleaning → EDA → modeling → validation → deployment considerations)
Incorporate big data processing scenarios requiring scalable computational approaches
Verify solutions using Python with standard data science libraries and statistical methods
Document problem statements clearly with realistic business contexts and provide verified correct answers


What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit for Data Science specialists with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:


5+ years of hands-on data science experience with proven business impact
Portfolio of completed projects and publications showcasing real-world problem-solving
Expert Python programming for data science (pandas, numpy, scipy, scikit-learn, statsmodels)
Expert statistical analysis and machine learning - deep understanding of algorithms, methods, and their practical applications
Expert with SQL and database operations for data manipulation and analysis
Experience with GenAI technologies (LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering, vector databases)
Understanding of MLOps practices and model deployment workflows
Knowledge of modern frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, LangChain)
Strong written English (C1+)


How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $58 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Title: (Senior) Data Analyst
Description:
As a (Senior) Data Analyst at Nelly, you'll transform fragmented health and finance data into scalable, trustworthy models, creating a single source of truth that powers company-wide strategic decisions. Your role goes beyond answering business questions: you'll own the core analytics logic, challenge requirements, and define how we measure business health.

About Nelly

At Nelly, we’re revolutionizing medical practice management with AI-driven automation and seamless digital workflows. Our innovative solutions - from best-in-class patient journeys, AI enabled patient relationship management and billing to state of the art payment solutions - eliminate painful workflows and improve financial outcomes making healthcare administration smarter, faster, and 100% digital.

To fuel our mission, we just raised €50M in our Series B, backed by top-tier investors like Cathay, Notion, Lakestar, GFC, b2venture, and Motive Partners. With this momentum, we’re on a mission to build Europe’s largest fintech in healthcare - bringing best-in-class financial and operational solutions to over 1 million medical practices serving millions of patients daily.

Join us now if you’re driven by impact, excited by fast-paced innovation, and ready to shape the future of healthcare!

Joining us, you will


Design, build, and maintain scalable data models using dbt and strong SQL, with a focus on robust data marts and a clean semantic layer.
Own the BI layer (Looker/LookML) end-to-end, creating self-service Explores that give the entire company a single source of truth for analysis.
Translate complex business logic into structured data models, including topics such as Customer Health Scores, NRR (cohort-based), CAC Payback, product unit economics, churn, provisions, and commercial KPIs.
Unify and harmonize data across systems such as Salesforce, Product Data, HubSpot, Jira, Intercom, Personio, and internal booking/factoring systems.
Develop end-to-end reporting solutions from requirement gathering and data validation to Looker dashboards and stakeholder-ready reports.


Build advanced analyses such as:


Sales funnel and GTM performance tracking
Commercial KPI automation
Cohort-based NRR, CAC payback and churn analytics
Revenue reconciliation (Signed vs. Actual Revenue)
Customer and usage benchmarks (including regional usage / booking patterns)


Ensuring data quality and cross-functional alignment:


Identify gaps in data collection and instrumentation before modeling begins, ensuring quality, consistency, and long-term scalability
Establish tests and validation logic in dbt to guarantee reliability and transparency across data models
Collaborate closely with Product, Commercial and Finance to deeply understand the processes generating the data
Support system alignment between tools to ensure consistent reporting logic across the organization


What You'll Bring


Strong hands-on experience with SQL, modular query design and dimensional data modeling (star schemas, fact/dimension design).
Production experience designing and maintaining scalable data models using modern transformation tools (e.g. dbt, SQLMesh, Metabase), including testing and documentation.
Experience developing SQL-based data marts and self-service explores BI-tool with modeling capabilities (e.g. Looker)
Solid understanding of revenue metrics (MRR, NRR, churn, cohort logic), SaaS KPIs, and GTM performance metrics.
Experience integrating and modeling data from tools such as Salesforce, HubSpot, CRM systems, and financial systems.
Strong stakeholder management skills and the ability to translate ambiguous business questions into structured analytical frameworks.
A proactive mindset - you question definitions, challenge inconsistencies, and improve processes instead of just reporting on them.
Fluency in SQL; Experience in dbt and Looker/LookML is a plus
Professional proficiency in English; German is a plus.


What We Offer


Competitive salary
Work in a great team of talented and highly motivated people
Dynamic environment, steep learning curve, and extensive development opportunities
Part of an exciting AI enabled FinTech startup in the German healthcare market
Responsibility, independent work, and high flexibility
Generous budget for learning and development
First-class work equipment and flexible working hours
Deutschlandticket or Swapfiets rental bike
Urban Sports Club membership


We are looking forward to receiving your application and welcoming you to the team.

Title: Data Engineer, Fraud
Description:
Who Are We?

Xcelirate develops technologically-advanced platforms which are accessed by thousands of users every minute! We are proud to offer a workplace where the sharpest developers come together to strategically plan and swiftly execute practices which see us maintain our existing market dominance and attain global expansion. We owe our success to our customers who have seen us grow across a decade, and our talented team who have made that growth possible.

Who are we looking for?

The Data Engineer will focus on designing, developing, and maintaining robust data infrastructure to support use cases such as fraud detection but also general data engineering. The position emphasizes building scalable, high-performing data pipelines and storage systems for fraud use cases. Although this role involves light integration with machine learning models, its primary responsibility is creating the technical foundation that powers such use cases of fraud detection, analytics and reporting.

What will you be doing?


Develop and Maintain Pipelines: build and maintain efficient, scalable data pipelines for use cases such as fraud detection
Support Fraud Analytics: enable analysts and product teams to identify and address emerging fraud patterns through engineered datasets
Integrate Detection Models: collaborate with teams to operationalise external fraud detection models and integrate them into the data infrastructure
Data Storage Optimisation: design and optimise data storage solutions for analysing fraud signals and managing historical data
Feature Engineering: create fraud-specific datasets and features to enhance detection accuracy while supporting business and analytics teams
Pipeline Monitoring and Optimisation: monitor fraud data pipelines to ensure system reliability and troubleshoot performance issues
Best Practices Documentation: establish and document best practices for fraud-related data engineering
Cross-Team Collaboration: partner with data, product, and engineering teams to proactively address fraud trends


Requirements

What will you bring along?


3+ years of experience as a data engineer with some expertise in fraud detection systems or similar
Proficiency in Python and SQL, with knowledge of orchestration and transformation tools (e.g., Apache Airflow, DBT)
Strong knowledge of database design, query optimisation, and ETL/ELT workflows, ideally including relational databases such as MySQL and columnar databases such as ClickHouse
Experience building and maintaining production data pipelines
Familiarity with leveraging machine learning models, primarily as a component of the broader data pipeline, including model registries such as MLflow
Understanding of CI/CD processes for data pipelines
Hands-on experience with data visualization platforms for trend reporting (e.g., Tableau, Superset, Metabase)
Ability to support with model serving components, such as Flask or FastAPI
Familiarity with statistical techniques for fraud trend analysis and reporting
Experience with Git and version control in collaborative workflows


We are always looking for the best candidates, so if you think you would be a good fit even if you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we would love to hear from you!

Benefits

How We Support Our Contractors:


An annualised rate of €98,000 + retention bonus scheme: Competitive contractor rate with a generous bonus paid every 6 months based on your time with us
Top-Notch Workstation: We provide the latest MacBook, branded merchandise, and everything you need for an optimal work environment.
Global Co-Working Access: Work from a global network of co-working spaces to keep your work-life dynamic and flexible.
Flexibility: Enjoy full flexibility in work location and hours, supporting a work-life balance tailored to your needs.
Events and Gatherings: Participate in exciting events throughout the year, including team gatherings, cultural events, and other fun activities


At Xcelirate, we're committed to equal employment opportunities regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender, gender identity or expression, or veteran status. We pride ourselves in being an equal opportunity workplace.

Title: Data Operations Analyst (m/f/d)
Description:
About Adsquare




Adsquare is a global leader in Location Intelligence for programmatic advertising.




Our mission is built on:




Passion: solving complex challenges with exceptional people, technology, and data
Niche: specializing in location intelligence for programmatic advertisers




We hire people who embody our core values:




Drive: turning ambition into tangible outcomes
Resilience: adapting and growing through challenges
No BS: clear, honest, transparent communication
Humble: letting results speak for themselves
Moral Compass: acting with integrity and fairness




Your Mission




Own and scale Adsquare’s data operations by ensuring seamless data ingestion, processing, automation, and delivery across all partner platforms.




What You Will Do




Data Execution & Automation




Own and deliver internal data projects end-to-end
Translate operational goals into clear work packages
Build automation workflows (incl. AI tools) to reduce manual work
Ensure smooth data delivery across all partner channels
Monitor and improve data quality and reliability
Identify bottlenecks and proactively solve them




Data Distribution & Integrations




Manage data syndication across DSPs, DMPs, and platforms
Support taxonomy mapping and partner integrations
Implement monitoring and alerting systems




Operational Efficiency & Scalability




Analyse partner usage and optimize distribution
Improve efficiency in taxonomy and package management
Collaborate with Product and Commercial teams on pricing and availability




Your Profile




3–5 years experience in Data Ops, Data Engineering, or AdTech
Strong SQL and Python skills
Experience with ETL workflows (dbt is a plus)
Familiar with cloud environments (AWS, GCP, Snowflake)
Experience with data quality monitoring
Understanding of AdTech ecosystem (DSPs, segmentation, taxonomy)
Experience with dashboards (Tableau, Sigma, etc.)
Strong communicator (technical + business stakeholders)
Proactive, autonomous, and solution-oriented
Interest in AI/automation with practical examples




Our Stack




SQL, Python
dbt / ETL pipelines
Cloud: AWS / GCP / Snowflake
BI tools: Tableau, Sigma




Why Join Us




Join at a key moment as Adsquare evolves into an AI-powered platform
Build impactful automation, not just maintain systems
Work closely with Product, Engineering, and Leadership
Fast growth in a high-trust, high-ownership environment




Recruiting Process




HR Interview (30 min)
Mission / Competencies Interview (45 min)
Case Study (90 min)
Team Fit Interview (30 min)
Offer




Yearly compensation: 50,000 - 65,000 € OTE




What We Offer




Hybrid work model
€1,200 yearly learning budget
30 vacation days
Urban Sports Club membership
Company pension scheme
Regular team & company events
Latest hardware and tools




Apply Now




If you’re passionate about data, automation, and building scalable systems we’d love to hear from you!

Title: Data Analyst - Teilportfolio AIDA
Description:
Data Analyst - Teilportfolio AIDA (m/w/d)

DKB AG

Standort: Berlin

unbefristet

Teil- & Vollzeit möglich

ab sofort

Deine Aufgaben


Du arbeitest im Teilportfolio AIDA an der Schnittstelle von Business, Data & AI, Data Engineering und Plattformentwicklung und machst Daten für Analytics-, Reporting-, KI- und Automatisierungs-Use-Cases nutzbar
Du wirkst am Aufbau einer produktorientierten Datenplattform mit und hilfst, Daten als Data Products verständlich, qualitätsgesichert und wiederverwendbar bereitzustellen
Du übersetzt fachliche Anforderungen in Datenanforderungen, Data-Product-Spezifikationen, Transformationslogiken und Abnahmekriterien und bist Sparringspartnerin für Product Owner*innen, Data Product Owner*innen und Fachbereiche
Du analysierst DKB-interne und externe Datenquellen, definierst fachliche Regeln für Integration, Harmonisierung und Qualitätssicherung und arbeitest an Data Products wie Person, Konto, Einkommen oder weiteren bankfachlichen Datenprodukten
Du schlägst die Brücke zwischen fachlichem Datenverständnis und technischer Implementierung, begleitest die Umsetzung in enger Zusammenarbeit mit Data Engineers und testest neue Datenprodukte, Datenlogiken und Plattformartefakte fachlich auf Herz und Nieren


Dein Profil


Du hast einen Abschluss in (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, Statistik, Mathematik, Wirtschaftswissenschaften oder einem vergleichbaren quantitativen Studiengang und bringst relevante Berufserfahrung als Data Analyst*in oder Analytics Engineer mit
Du verstehst fachliche Anforderungen schnell, kannst sie strukturieren, priorisieren und in belastbare Datenanforderungen, Analysekonzepte und (technische) Umsetzungsschritte übersetzen
Du bringst sehr gute SQL-Kenntnisse mit, kannst Datenmodelle, Datenflüsse und Transformationslogiken sicher analysieren, validieren und fachlich bewerten und so analytische Fragestellungen von der Datenexploration bis zur Ergebnisaufbereitung begleiten
Begriffe wie Data Product, Data Contract, Data Product Factory und Data Marketplace sind für dich idealerweise keine Fremdwörter - oder du hast Lust, diese Themen aktiv mitzugestalten
Du arbeitest sicher mit Git, Jira und Confluence und bewegst dich gern in einem agilen, interdisziplinären Umfeld


Die DKB steht für Vielfalt und Chancengerechtigkeit. Egal, woher du kommst, wie alt du bist, welches Geschlecht, welche Genderidentität, sexuelle Orientierung oder Religion du hast, ob mit oder ohne Behinderung - wir freuen uns auf deine Bewerbung!

Deine Benefits und mehr


Attraktive Vergütung (tariflich oder außertariflich)
Flexibles und mobiles Arbeiten – temporär auch im EU-Ausland
Du hast die Wahl: Benefits Pass, Jobticket oder Jobrad
Moderne Technik- ausstattung und Tool Stack
Individuelle Qualifi- zierungsmöglichkeiten
Duz-Kultur und kein Dresscode
Externe Mitarbeitenden- beratung
Angebote zum Gesundbleiben
Weitere Extras wie z.B. betriebliche Altersvorsorge und Versicherungsschutz


Immer für dich ansprechbar

Axel Zimmermann

Chapter Lead

axel.zimmermann@dkb.de

Deutsche Kreditbank AG

Du möchtest mehr zur DKB erfahren?

Dann schau auf unserer Karriereseite vorbei.

Title: Junior Tracking Specialist (all genders)
Description:
Your role

You will support the measurement of user behavior and E-Commerce data on our websites and possible future app(s). For this, you will help continuously evolve the client- and server-side Google Tag Manager setup, including the usage of accompanying features in Google Cloud, as well as improving the GA4 setup.

The position centers around supporting to set up the best possible tracking to provide high-quality data to be used in our marketing channels, for website optimization, and in BI reporting to facilitate further business growth.

You will be an important contributor to the company's website data setup for our 10+ country domains together with our Senior Tracking Specialist.


Continuously improve the quality and quantity of measured data in GTM and GA 4
Support implementing data quality monitoring measures and creating suitable reports with Looker Studio
Participate in shaping our measurement strategy to meet marketing and business needs
Take over parts of the maintenance of Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics
Educate stakeholders about the features and limitations of our tracking setup
Continuously monitor new developments in the digital analytics ecosphere
Collaborate closely with our marketing and data teams to solve business questions, create synergies, and drive business growth through data
Support marketing teams with requests


Your profile


At least 1 year of in-depth experience with Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics 4; App tracking is a bonus
Demonstrated success in the development of tracking setups
Experience with setting up Server-Side GTM in a multi-domain setup
You are accustomed to the tracking needs of paid channels such as Google Ads, Meta Ads, and others
Basic knowledge in SQL, CSS, and JS is a plus
Strong focus on structured approaches, documentation, and organizing maintenance and monitoring of data quality
High interest in closely collaborating with Data and Marketing Teams
Talent to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences
An interest in how AI will augment work in data measurement and digital analytics is a plus


What we offer


An appealing discount on all of our products – from essential oils to vitamins & nutrients
We offer 28 days of paid time off per year plus up to 5 days off between Christmas and New Year's (pro rata), provided Sunday Natural is closed for company holidays during this time
The Urban Sports Club membership and either Swapfiets bike rental or a subsidy for the Deutschlandticket
We believe great things happen when we meet in person, which is why we embrace a hybrid model with one home office day per week – up to two depending on the area you are working in – and up to two weeks per year working from abroad
Receive various discounts of up to more than 50 percent on attractive brands such as Flaconi, Bosch, Apple or Expedia
You will be given real ownership and plenty of room for new initiatives, as well as the opportunity to contribute your expertise and implement your great ideas
Inviting office spaces with access to plenty of organic teas and superfoods from around the world
We like to celebrate each other! Regular tea & office parties, Christmas and summer feasts and various influencer events such as Women's Day


Apply with your CV and cover letter.

Apply for this job

About Us

Sunday Natural emerged from a long-standing passion and research in the fields of health, healing, and personal growth. The lack of natural, high-quality products on the market was the original motivation for founding Sunday Natural in 2013. We aim to produce the products we ourselves would love to buy – as natural as possible, absolutely pure, free from any additives, and of the highest quality. In a strategic partnership with CVC, Sunday Natural is now one of Germany's most renowned quality manufacturers, with its own research and development department in Berlin. For the continuous improvement of our products, we collaborate with selected research institutes, scientists, therapists, farmers, and professional associations.

We take our ecological responsibility very seriously: that’s why the majority of our product range is vegan. We also take an innovative approach to packaging: most of our products are packaged in glass, making them almost entirely recyclable.

Our diverse team in Berlin consists of over 350 employees from more than 40 nationalities and continues to grow – but at a sustainable pace. Our goal is to foster a collaborative, supportive, and inclusive work environment with a diverse group of motivated, talented, and visionary team members.

We highly value equality of opportunity and inclusivity, and we would like to particularly encourage women and candidates from under-represented backgrounds to apply, even if you don’t match with 100% of the requirements.

Title: Working Student Operations & Analytics (x/f/m)
Description:
Gemeinsam gestalten wir die Gesundheitsversorgung der Zukunft

Doctolib sucht Dich ab sofort als Werkstudent:in im Bereich Operations & Analytics für unseren Standort in Berlin.  

Das LOE-Team steht für operative Exzellenz quer durch unsere kundenseitigen Bereiche – von Deployment über Customer Onboarding und Customer Success bis hin zu Support Excellence. Wir stellen sicher, dass alle Teams mit sauberen Daten, verlässlichen KPI-Reportings und reibungslosen CRM-Prozessen arbeiten können. So tragen wir dazu bei, dass Tausende Gesundheitseinrichtungen in Deutschland täglich die bestmögliche Unterstützung von Doctolib erhalten – und leisten damit unseren direkten Beitrag zu unserer Mission: den Zugang zur Gesundheitsversorgung für alle zu verbessern.

Deine Aufgaben

Als Werkstudent:in unterstützt Du uns in den folgenden Aufgabenbereichen:

Wöchentliche Datenexporte aus Salesforce, Metabase & Google Sheets
Manuelle KPI-Updates in Qobra (unserem Tool zur Bonusberechnung)
Pflege von Abwesenheits- und Headcount-Tracking-Tabellen
Aktualisierung von KPI-Präsentationen für die Teams Customer Onboarding & Deployment
Erstellung wiederkehrender Salesforce-Cases (KYC, Preiserhöhung, Churn)
Unterstützung bei der Bonus-Validierung und der Vorbereitung von Performance Reviews
Formatierung & Re-Import von Daten zwischen verschiedenen Systemen (z. B. Typeform → Salesforce, Google Sheets → Salesforce)
Ad-hoc-Unterstützung bei Reporting-Anfragen und Analytics-Tickets
Beantwortung von Datenfragen quer durch alle Teams (KPI-Definitionen, Berechnungen)
Während Deiner Tätigkeit bei uns wirst Du lernen:
relevanten Business-Intelligence- und CRM-Tools (Salesforce, Metabase, Qobra) in einem schnell wachsenden HealthTech-Unternehmen
Ein tiefes Verständnis für datengetriebene Operations-Prozesse entwickeln und verstehen, wie KPI-Systeme in einer internationalen Organisation funktionieren
Cross-funktionale Zusammenarbeit in einem dynamischen Umfeld erleben und ein breites Netzwerk innerhalb von Doctolib aufbauen
Dein Profil

Du bist in Vollzeit an einer Hochschule immatrikuliert und studierst im Bereich BWL, Wirtschaftsinformatik, Data Science, Statistik oder einem vergleichbaren Studiengang (ab dem 3. Semester)

HTML:
Gemeinsam gestalten wir die Gesundheitsversorgung der Zukunft

Doctolib sucht Dich ab sofort als Werkstudent:in im Bereich Operations & Analytics für unseren Standort in Berlin.  

Das LOE-Team steht für operative Exzellenz quer durch unsere kundenseitigen Bereiche – von Deployment über Customer Onboarding und Customer Success bis hin zu Support Excellence. Wir stellen sicher, dass alle Teams mit sauberen Daten, verlässlichen KPI-Reportings und reibungslosen CRM-Prozessen arbeiten können. So tragen wir dazu bei, dass Tausende Gesundheitseinrichtungen in Deutschland täglich die bestmögliche Unterstützung von Doctolib erhalten – und leisten damit unseren direkten Beitrag zu unserer Mission: den Zugang zur Gesundheitsversorgung für alle zu verbessern.

Deine Aufgaben

Als Werkstudent:in unterstützt Du uns in den folgenden Aufgabenbereichen:

  • Wöchentliche Datenexporte aus Salesforce, Metabase & Google Sheets
  • Manuelle KPI-Updates in Qobra (unserem Tool zur Bonusberechnung)
  • Pflege von Abwesenheits- und Headcount-Tracking-Tabellen
  • Aktualisierung von KPI-Präsentationen für die Teams Customer Onboarding & Deployment
  • Erstellung wiederkehrender Salesforce-Cases (KYC, Preiserhöhung, Churn)
  • Unterstützung bei der Bonus-Validierung und der Vorbereitung von Performance Reviews
  • Formatierung & Re-Import von Daten zwischen verschiedenen Systemen (z. B. Typeform → Salesforce, Google Sheets → Salesforce)
  • Ad-hoc-Unterstützung bei Reporting-Anfragen und Analytics-Tickets
  • Beantwortung von Datenfragen quer durch alle Teams (KPI-Definitionen, Berechnungen)
Während Deiner Tätigkeit bei uns wirst Du lernen:
  • relevanten Business-Intelligence- und CRM-Tools (Salesforce, Metabase, Qobra) in einem schnell wachsenden HealthTech-Unternehmen
  • Ein tiefes Verständnis für datengetriebene Operations-Prozesse entwickeln und verstehen, wie KPI-Systeme in einer internationalen Organisation funktionieren
  • Cross-funktionale Zusammenarbeit in einem dynamischen Umfeld erleben und ein breites Netzwerk innerhalb von Doctolib aufbauen

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
Location: We are flexible! Come and join us either in Hamburg, Berlin or Barcelona.

FREENOW empowers smarter mobility decisions helping people to move freely and cities to thrive.

We're seeking a talented Analytics Engineer (m/f/d) to join our Data Team and collaborate with Data Analysts, Developers, and Data Scientists, in implementing a Data Mesh architecture that will enable our domains to access trusted data to unlock business opportunities easily.

Imagine empowering teams with the data they need to make game-changing decisions. Data puzzles excite you? Do you thrive in a collaborative environment where you can shape the future of data infrastructure? If so, we want you!

Be ready to work in a multinational, diverse, highly motivated, and collaborative team of passionate data professionals who strive for excellence and like to have fun. Are you ready for your next ride?

YOUR DAILY ADVENTURES WILL INCLUDE:


Providing expertise and collaborating with stakeholders to develop new data products and improve on existing ones in a Data Mesh environment
Continuously engaging with data analysts, data scientists, and software engineers to gain an understanding of the problems that need to be solved, gathering requirements, and defining the best deliverables to match the business needs
Working hand-in-hand with developers to create and capture data that aligns with business logic, enabling powerful modeling and analysis
Own the quality, availability, and trustworthiness of data by applying data quality checks and data contracts
Performing data analysis to troubleshoot data-related issues and assist in resolving them
Designing, building and maintaining the definitive source of truth for KPIs, empowering strategic decisions across all domains
Working towards the creation of an environment where self-service capabilities are available and data can be accessible by everyone


Our Tech Tools: SQL | Python | Databricks | Tableau | Mixpanel | Airflow | AWS | Kafka | Debezium

TO BE SUCCESSFUL IN THIS ROLE:


You have curiosity in your essence, aligned with attention to detail
Previous experience in working in a data-driven analytics environment, preferably within tech product organizations
Expert level of SQL (especially SparkSQL) and experience in building data pipelines is a core requirement.
You have good knowledge of at least one programming language and are happy to learn more. Our core language is Python.
Exceptional analytical skills, lateral thinking, and proven experience in solving highly challenging problems
Experience in working with data visualization tools (e.g.Tableau, Qlikview) and event tracking (e.g. Mixpanel, Google Analytics)
Excellent English communication skills
Hands-on experience with AWS, Hive, Spark, Trino, Databricks, and Airflow is helpful


BENEFITS & PERKS IN A NUTSHELL:


Flexible working arrangements
LinkedIn Learning
Sabbatical & special leave policies
WeRoad partnership
Birthday, 24th + 31st December off
Short term EU work policy
Mobility Credit
Health insurance
Employee assistance program


Plus more local benefits depending on your work location!

DIVERSITY, EQUITY & INCLUSION:

FREENOW is an equal opportunity employer and we consider qualified applicants regardless of race, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability or age.

We want you to grow and evolve, bring your true self to work.

ABOUT US:

Freenow by Lyft empowers smarter mobility decisions, helping people to move freely and cities to thrive. Through our multi-mobility app, we feature broad options for everyone across 9 European markets and over 180 cities. Millions of passengers can access services including taxis, private hire vehicles, carsharing, car rental, e-scooters, e-bikes, e-mopeds, and public transport within a single app.

In July 2025 Freenow was acquired by Lyft, a global mobility platform, which connects riders and drivers for billions of rides across North America and Europe. Together, Freenow and Lyft are creating a more caring and connected world, with transportation for everyone. We are a global, diverse, highly motivated, and collaborative team that strives for excellence and likes to have fun. Ready for your next ride?

Title: Senior Data Analyst, Real Estate & Motors (d/f/m)
Description:
Job Description

Kleinanzeigen is the leading online classifieds market in Germany. Users buy and sell on Germany's No. 1 for classifieds mainly second-hand. In this way they make an active contribution to more sustainability. On average, more than 55 million ads are available in numerous categories – from children's supplies to electronics and real estate. Kleinanzeigen also offers small and medium-sized businesses the opportunity to present their services online. Around 35 million users per month make Kleinanzeigen one of the most widely used websites in Germany. Kleinanzeigen was launched in September 2009 as eBay Kleinanzeigen. Since June 2021, the company has been part of Adevinta, a global provider of online classified ads. In May 2023, the name was changed to Kleinanzeigen.

The Job

As a Data Analyst in the Real Estate & Motors Analytics team, you help shape data-driven decisions across Product, Business, and Sales functions. You turn complex data into actionable insights, support experimentation and forecasting initiatives, and contribute to improving our products and user experience. Working closely with cross-functional stakeholders in a collaborative Data and Analytics setup, you help teams identify impactful opportunities and drive informed decision-making through analytics and clear communication. This is a hybrid role, our office is in Berlin-Charlottenburg. You report to the Analytics Lead, Real Estate.

We're looking for someone to join us on a limited 1-year contract.

Job requirements

Who You Are


You bring strong SQL skills and hands-on experience in product and business analytics, experimentation, and KPI analysis.
You communicate effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders and build strong cross-functional relationships.
You demonstrate an ownership mindset, strong prioritization skills, and adaptability in dynamic environments.
You have experience with BI and visualization tools such as Tableau and understand tracking concepts and data quality validation.
You bring skills to adapt automation and AI where it creates value.
Nice to have: exposure to forecasting, Python, and modern analytics tooling such as DBT.


Job responsibilities

What You’ll Do


Partner with Product, Business, and Sales stakeholders to deliver actionable insights and support data-driven decision making.
Own analytics topics, including tracking definition, funnel analysis, KPI monitoring, and performance reporting for product, business and sales.
Support A/B testing initiatives through experiment setup, analysis, and measurement of feature performance and business impact.
Build and maintain dashboards, reports, and analytical deep dives for the Real Estate & Motors domain. To “get the data ready”, you will contribute to the semantic layer and data model.
Collaborate closely with Analysts, Data Engineers, and Product Managers in a cross-functional DnA environment.
Contribute to forecasting, planning, and operational analytics initiatives to support business growth and prioritization.
Utilize state-of-the-art AI tools to perform your work efficiently by balancing manual effort with automation.


Job benefits

At Kleinanzeigen, we care about our people — their wellbeing, growth, and life beyond work. Our benefits are designed to support you holistically, giving you flexibility and opportunities to thrive.

✨ Flexibility


Flexible working time (hybrid)
20 days “work from anywhere”
Paid additional parental leave for both parents
Extra paid days off for special occasions (e.g. your wedding or a move)


❤️‍🩹 Wellbeing & Support


Confidential support & coaching for you and your loved ones
Annual company sports events
Pension and life insurance


📚 Growth & Development


Personal development budget
Learning opportunities to grow your skills and career


🛍️ Perks & Everyday benefits


Corporate benefits and exclusive discounts
An attractive referral program
Transportation allowance
Free shipping on Kleinanzeigen Direktkauf


Kleinanzeigen is an equal opportunity employer that values diversity and inclusion. We welcome people of all backgrounds, races, religions, colours, national origins, gender identities, sexual orientation, ages, marital status, or disability.

If this role sparks your interest but you’re not sure you meet every single requirement, we’d still love to hear from you anyway.

Title: Werkstudent:in Data Science
Description:
Über nerou




Die Datenanalyse-Software von nerou unterstützt Betreiber von Kläranlagen bei täglichen Entscheidungen in der Anlagensteuerung. In Kläranlagen müssen komplexe Entscheidungen getroffen werden, um die Anlage auf sich täglich ändernde Einflüsse einzustellen. Alle relevanten Daten werden von Machine Learning Algorithmen analysiert und ausgewertet. Die gewonnen Erkenntnisse werden in Form von Entscheidungshilfen an den Kunden weitergeleitet. Dies reduziert nicht nur Kosten für die Betreiber, sondern schont so auch Ressourcen und Umwelt. Zur Verstärkung unseres Data Science-Bereichs suchen wir nun Dich!







Deine Tätigkeiten



Du extrahierst und validierst große Datenbestände auf Grundlage verschiedener Fragestellungen
Du analysierst Datensätze mit geeigneten statistischen Methoden
Du ergänzt unser motiviertes Team mit Machine Learning- und Data Science-Expertise






Deine Erfahrung



Du studierst (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, Mathematik oder etwas vergleichbares
Du hast Erfahrung im Umgang mit Data Science und Machine Learning
Du hast grundliegende Kenntnisse mit Python
Du bist in der Lage, deine Arbeit selbstständig zu strukturieren






Was wir dir bieten




Eine Menge Spaß, versprochen! Du wirst als Werkstudent Teil eines jungen Start-ups und kannst dich mit deinen eigenen Ideen austoben. Du sammelst wertvolle Erfahrungen und hast dabei die Möglichkeit, eigene Projekte zu verwirklichen. Hierbei erhältst du zudem wertvolle Einblicke in das Thema Unternehmensgründung und Start-up. Bei uns kannst du dir deine Arbeitszeit frei einteilen und hast die Wahl zwischen Büro und Home Office - somit bist du auch neben deinem Studium flexibel.




Wenn es dir bei uns gefällt, freuen wir uns natürlich, wenn du lange bei uns bleibst - auch nach deinem Studium in einer Festanstellung! Interessiert? Dann ran an die Tasten! Sende uns deine Mail mit Anschreiben, Zeugnissen und Lebenslauf an jobs@nerou.de Wir freuen uns von dir zu hören und hoffen, dich schon bald in unserem Team begrüßen zu dürfen!
