Title: Business Systems Analyst
Description:
Drive the future of cybersecurity and AI-powered innovation across financial services.

The Role

Capco is seeking an experienced Business Analyst with deep expertise in cybersecurity, vulnerability management, and emerging AI technologies to support strategic security initiatives for leading financial institutions. This role combines strong business analysis capabilities with technical cybersecurity knowledge, enabling you to bridge the gap between business stakeholders and technology teams while helping organizations strengthen their security posture and explore AI-driven opportunities.

What You’ll Do


Gather, analyze, and document business, functional, and technical requirements for cybersecurity initiatives.
Partner with security, technology, and business stakeholders to enhance vulnerability management processes and controls.
Assess security risks, identify gaps, and support remediation planning and execution.
Identify and evaluate opportunities to leverage AI capabilities within cybersecurity workflows and operations.
Facilitate stakeholder workshops, maintain project documentation, and support successful delivery of strategic initiatives.


What We’re Looking For


Advanced experience in business analysis within cybersecurity-focused environments.
Strong knowledge of cybersecurity principles, vulnerability management, and security risk assessment methodologies.
Understanding of AI applications and emerging technologies within cybersecurity operations.
Excellent stakeholder management, requirements gathering, communication, and facilitation skills.
Experience working within financial services, banking, or highly regulated environments.


Bonus Points For


Professional certifications such as CBAP, CCBA, CISSP, CISM, Security+, or equivalent.
Experience supporting enterprise cybersecurity transformation programs.
Knowledge of cloud security, threat management, or security operations practices.
Familiarity with Agile delivery methodologies and project frameworks.
Experience working with AI-driven security tooling or automation platforms.


Why Join Capco


Deliver high-impact technology solutions for Tier 1 financial institutions.
Work in a collaborative, flat, and entrepreneurial consulting culture.
Access continuous learning, training, and industry certifications.
Be part of a team shaping the future of digital financial services.
Help shape the future of digital transformation across Financial Services.


Benefits


Comprehensive Benefits Coverage – 100% company-paid health, life, and disability insurance effective from Day 1 of employment.
Retirement Savings Program – Registered Retirement Savings Plan (RRSP) with the option of receiving a matching employer contribution.
Virtual Health Care – Company-paid program available to all employees and their dependents/partners.
Employee & Family Assistance Program (EFAP) – Company-paid support services for employees and their dependents/partners.
Wellness Support and Career Growth – Fitness reimbursement benefit plus a dedicated Business Coach from Day 1 to support your professional development.


Why This Role Is Open

We are currently hiring for this role due to an existing or newly created vacancy within our organization. This position is open as part of our ongoing business needs, and we are actively reviewing applications to fill this role with a qualified candidate who can make an immediate impact.

Inclusion at Capco

We’re committed to making our recruitment process accessible and straightforward for everyone. If you need any adjustments at any stage, just let us know – we’ll be happy to help. We value each person’s unique perspective and contribution. At Capco, we believe that being yourself is your greatest strength. Our #BeYourselfAtWork culture encourages individuality and collaboration – a mindset that shapes how we work with clients and each other every day.

Use of Artificial Intelligence in Talent Acquisition

At Capco, we use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support and enhance several parts of talent acquisition. This includes using AI-enabled features within LinkedIn to help source potential candidates, speeding up routine recruitment communications such as emails and creating compelling and brand-aligned job postings that accurately reflect role requirements, and AI-scheduling applications to improve the efficiency of interview coordination.

AI is used as a support tool only. All hiring decisions are made by talent acquisition and hiring teams.

Pay Transparency

The salary range for this position is listed below. Additionally, this position may also be eligible to receive an annual discretionary/variable bonus payment.

Capco is committed to providing fair and equitable compensation to our people. Our compensation policies and salary ranges are designed to allow our people to progress through the salary range as they demonstrate strong performance and develop in their role over time. The base pay offered to selected candidates will be within the salary range and the placement will vary based upon a variety of factors, including, but not limited to job-related knowledge, skills, experience and internal equity.

Canada Pay Transparency

$92,000—$118,000 CAD

Title: Data Developer
Description:
About The Company

Farm Credit Canada (FCC) is a federally owned Crown corporation dedicated to supporting Canadian agriculture and food sectors. With a mission to build a strong, sustainable, and innovative agricultural industry, FCC provides essential financing, knowledge resources, and business management software to over 103,000 customers across the country. As a key player in the agricultural finance landscape, FCC is committed to fostering growth, innovation, and resilience within Canada's agricultural communities. The organization prides itself on its purpose-driven approach, emphasizing relationship building, knowledge sharing, and supporting the people who feed the nation.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly skilled Senior Developer to join our dynamic team at Farm Credit Canada. In this pivotal role, you will leverage your expertise in cloud-native development and data analytics to design, develop, and implement innovative solutions that empower our business operations. The Senior Developer will work extensively with AWS tools and platforms, utilizing their technical acumen to create scalable, efficient, and maintainable code. This position offers an excellent opportunity for a seasoned professional to lead technical initiatives, mentor peers, and contribute to strategic projects that directly impact the organization’s mission to support Canadian agriculture. The ideal candidate will thrive in a collaborative environment, demonstrating strong problem-solving skills and a passion for delivering high-quality technology solutions.

Qualifications

The successful candidate will possess a combination of education and experience that aligns with the following qualifications:


Bachelor's degree in computer science, engineering, or a related field.
Minimum of seven years of software development experience, or an equivalent combination of education and experience.
Expert-level proficiency in AWS technologies such as AWS Glue, Step Functions, S3, Redshift, Quicksight, Lambda, Eventbridge, and AppFlow.
Strong programming skills in Python, PySpark, Typescript, and SQL.
In-depth knowledge of common design patterns and their applications, including object-oriented programming (OOP) and functional programming (FP).
Experience in optimizing system design to enable business value creation through analytics.
Proven experience in code reviews, application design, and system integration.
Exceptional troubleshooting and problem-solving skills.


Preferred qualifications include expertise in data modeling methodologies such as Data Vault 2.0 and Dimensional (Star Schema), experience in coaching and mentoring peers, and a background in Canadian agricultural business knowledge.

Responsibilities

The Senior Developer will be responsible for a broad range of technical and collaborative tasks, including:


Analyzing and developing new code, with a focus on automation, integration, and unit testing to ensure robust solutions.
Utilizing AWS frameworks and patterns, primarily infrastructure as code with CDK, to design and implement scalable solutions.
Collaborating on architectural design decisions, ensuring solutions meet business requirements and align with organizational standards.
Maintaining and enhancing technical solutions and system integrations for efficiency and effectiveness.
Implementing code fixes, supporting user acceptance testing, and ensuring high-quality deliverables.
Leading or participating in group design and coding sessions to promote best practices and knowledge sharing.
Designing data processing applications, data models, and analytics solutions to support business insights.
Contributing to code reviews by providing constructive feedback and ensuring adherence to coding standards.
Explaining complex technical concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders to facilitate understanding and collaboration.
Working closely with analytics users, data scientists, and ML specialists to develop cloud-native solutions that drive data-driven decision-making.


Benefits

Joining FCC offers a comprehensive benefits package designed to support your professional growth and personal well-being. You can expect:


A competitive total rewards package, including a market-aligned salary, performance-based incentives, and flexible benefit plans.
Well-being programs that promote health, wellness, and work-life balance.
Opportunities for continuous learning and professional development to help you thrive in your career.
Hybrid work options that provide flexibility in managing your work environment.
A purpose-driven organization committed to making a positive impact on Canadian agriculture and food sectors.


Equal Opportunity

Farm Credit Canada is committed to fostering an inclusive, equitable, and accessible workplace. We believe diversity enhances our organization and strengthens our ability to serve our communities. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds, including Indigenous Peoples, visible minorities, persons with disabilities, and women. If you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please contact us via email. We are dedicated to providing a fair and respectful hiring experience and will work with you to meet your needs. Our commitment is to ensure that everyone has an equal opportunity to succeed and contribute to our mission.

Title: Lab Assistant
Description:
Description

Position at Dynacare

Job Posting: Laboratory Assistant

Where YOU work, makes a difference.

Life is precious and every moment matters. Dynacare is helping Canadians achieve a healthy future with care and wellness solutions that are convenient, understandable, and accessible. When you join our team, every day is an opportunity to get better and be inspired. It’s an environment where you can grow your career in any direction you choose. We’re evolving our technology and optimizing our processes, so you can do your best work, make a meaningful impact, and thrive.

Are YOU our next Laboratory Assistant?

As a Laboratory Assistant (float), you are responsible for processing blood samples and other laboratory specimens, entering requisitions and results accurately – all in a timely manner. You also play an important role in providing customer service to create the best possible patient experience. These can be stressful situations for patients, and you need to display empathy, care, and kindness. Testing results are often critical for patients, and you are there to help and make an impact on their lives.

Status: Contract, Part-Time, (10 months) Guaranteed 15 hours a week

Shift: Day Shifts, 6:30 am – 5:30 pm; Monday – Sunday

Float Locations: North York, GTA, Etobicoke, Woodbridge, Ontario areas

Discipline: Laboratory and Health Services Centers

Number of positions: 1

Vacancy Type: Existing

Start Date: As soon as possible

Internal Posting Deadline: October 21, 2025

Why Dynacare Is An Amazing Place For YOU


Join an award-winning "Top Employer" with meaningful and impactful career opportunities
Access a health and wellness benefits program that supports you and your loved ones
Grow and thrive with a dynamic, successful company through internal mobility opportunities
Invest in your future through RRSP match benefits and an employee stock purchase program
Experience a collaborative, diverse workforce that prioritizes dignity and respect for all


How YOU Will Make a Difference


Create an ideal customer experience that is best in class and engages with individuals around our products and services
Accurately enter data, patient, and test information for Ministry of Health requisitions
Obtain quality blood samples and other specimens from patients, using skillful venipuncture techniques, performed in keeping with Dynacare’s mission and values
Sort and steer specimens, perform ECG’s, Holter Hookups, paternity, urine, drug and breath alcohol collections
Ensure specimens and supporting documentation are completed as required in SOPs and ready for data entry and transportation to the laboratory, following established protocols for the handling of suitable and unsuitable samples
Ensure all confidential information is maintained in a secure fashion. Contribute to meeting the departmental/corporate quality metrics, regulatory compliance and quality indicators


What YOU Bring To The Role


You have completed Secondary School and a Laboratory Assistant training program or a Medical Lab Assistance course with specialized training in phlebotomy
You are customer-focused with a genuine care for patient’s health
Demonstrate skill in phlebotomy, with the ability to manage anxious patients through the sample obtaining process in a pleasant, calming and reassuring manner
Prioritize and self-manage within a deadline-oriented environment
Be extremely detail oriented
Proficiency in English, both written and verbal, at a level that enables you to communicate instructions, clearly and understandably, to patients
MLPAO/CSMLS Certification is strongly recommended


Pay rate: $20.00 -$23.75 per hour

The hiring pay range has been established; however, the final pay offer will be determined by taking into consideration various factors including but not limited to, level of experience and education requirements.

At Dynacare, we may leverage AI-powered tools in our recruitment process. AI will never reach out to our candidates directly.

Passionate about supporting healthy lives with commitment and care? Please apply today and let us open the door to your extended career journey.

Dynacare has been a “Top Employer” for many years and there is a reason why. We are a great place to work. At Dynacare, we’re proud to hire the best people. If you are looking for a meaningful career where you can support healthy lives with commitment and care – we would like to meet you. We hope you will join us in our journey to become Canada’s health and wellness solutions leader.

Dynacare is proud to be an equal opportunity employer committed to the attraction, selection, advancement, and fair treatment of all individuals. We believe that our diversity is our strength, so we employ a diverse workforce and respect the needs of all our employees. In accordance with the Ontario Human Rights Code, Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act and the Dynacare Accommodation Policy a request for accommodation will be accepted as part of the Dynacare hiring process.

Description:
Business Analyst - IT – 3

Location: 40 King Street W 13th Floor

Description:
Date limite pour postuler : 3 Juillet 2026

EDC Analyste de recherche diplômé - Centre d'intelligence économique et politique

À propos d'EDC : À Exportation et développement Canada (EDC), nous aidons les entreprises canadiennes à réussir à l'étranger. En tant que société d'État à vocation financière, EDC propose des solutions financières innovantes et des conseils d'experts pour aider les entreprises à explorer de nouveaux marchés, à atténuer leurs risques et à se développer.

Pourquoi vous joindre à EDC?


Collaborez avec une équipe diversifiée et expérimentée
Épanouissez-vous dans un environnement axé sur le soutien et le travail d'équipe
Influencez les communautés à l'échelle mondiale en stimulant la croissance durable des marchés émergents
Travaillez dans une entreprise reconnue comme l'un des 100 meilleurs employeurs du Canada, l'un des meilleurs employeurs pour la diversité au Canada et l'un des employeurs canadiens favorisant le plus la conciliation travail-famille
Profitez d'un programme d'avantages globaux comprenant des avantages sociaux, des congés, des programmes de bien-être et des occasions de perfectionnement professionnel


Offres d'emploi pour les personnes étudiantes et nouvellement diplômées

Nos offres d'emploi pour les personnes étudiantes et nouvellement diplômées comprennent des stages et des programmes d'alternance travail-études, tous conçus pour donner un bon départ à votre carrière. Un stage supplémentaire de quatre mois ou plus peut s'ajouter au stage de quatre mois, selon les besoins opérationnels. Il s'agit pour la plupart de postes à temps plein, mais le travail à temps partiel pourrait être envisagé.

Notre programme est idéal pour les personnes étudiantes et pour les professionnelles et les professionnels en début de carrière qui ont récemment obtenu leur diplôme et ont terminé leurs cours l'année dernière. Les stages effectués dans le cadre de notre programme peuvent faire partie d'un programme coopératif (COOP) universitaire ou collégial accrédité.

Profil de la personne étudiante ou nouvellement diplômée idéale


Diplôme d'un programme de premier ou de deuxième cycle (jusqu'à un an après la date d'obtention du diplôme) ou diplôme en voie d'obtention
Disponibilité pour une période de 8 mois ou plus - bien que les stages aient une durée de 4 mois, la préférence sera accordée aux candidates et candidats disponibles au-delà de cette période
Expérience professionnelle antérieure liée au domaine d'études
Aptitudes marquées pour la communication orale et écrite
Motivation et sens de l'initiative, de l'organisation et de l'analyse
Capacité de travailler de façon autonome dans une équipe
Enthousiasme dans l'exécution des tâches simples comme complexes
La connaissance des deux langues officielles est un atout, mais peut être exigée pour certains postes


Aperçu

Le Centre d'information économique et politique d'Exportation et développement Canada (EDC) est à la recherche d'un(e) étudiant(e) à la maîtrise ou au doctorat très motivé(e) qui se joindra à son équipe dans le cadre d'un projet de recherche axé sur le commerce d'intégration et la productivité au Canada qui comportera les volets suivants : l'analyse des exportations de biens et de services du Canada, l'investissement direct canadien à l'étranger (IDCE), les infrastructures de facilitation du commerce et les investissements directs étrangers (IDE) et leur corrélation avec la croissance économique et la productivité.

Le Centre d'information économique et politique interne d'EDC réunit des économistes, des chercheurs et des experts en recherche quantitative qui effectuent des analyses actuelles sur les enjeux façonnant les résultats commerciaux et la compétitivité mondiale des entreprises canadiennes. Nos travaux éclairent à la fois le processus décisionnel interne et la capacité de leadership éclairé d'acteurs externes.

Il s'agit d'un poste idéal pour une personne en mesure de travailler à titre de chercheur indépendant en sciences appliquées et de transposer une question dans le domaine de la recherche du concept au résultat final avec une supervision limitée, le tout en s'appuyant sur les conseils d'économistes chevronnés et en gérant le projet dans son intégralité.

À propos du projet

La personne choisie contribuera à un projet de recherche portant sur le commerce d'intégration, l'engagement économique et la productivité du Canada. En fonction du profil du candidat et de l'évolution des travaux, le projet peut être axé sur un ou plusieurs des domaines suivants :


rendement des exportations canadiennes de biens
commerce des services et croissance des exportations de services du Canada
investissement direct canadien à l'étranger (IDCE)
investissements directs étrangers (IDE) et leur relation avec le commerce, la compétitivité, la productivité et la capacité de production
Infrastructures de facilitation du commerce et la façon dont elles contribuent à la diversification et à une intégration accrue aux chaînes de valeur mondiales pour les entreprises canadiennes


Le projet exigera non seulement de solides compétences empiriques, mais aussi la capacité de relier les données et les résultats économétriques à des enjeux économiques plus larges liés à la diversification du commerce, à la compétitivité internationale, à la création de valeur et à la croissance.

La capacité à travailler avec des données recueillies auprès d'entreprises et une compréhension approfondie des ensembles de données sur le commerce mondial seront essentielles pour le poste.

Principales responsabilités

Nota - La personne choisie relèvera directement de la ou du gestionnaire, Recherche et retombées économiques.


Diriger un projet de recherche indépendant du début à la fin de concert avec des économistes chevronnés et des leaders de la recherche
Contribuer à affiner le sujet de recherche, le cadre analytique et la stratégie empirique
Réaliser des analyses documentaires ciblées sur le commerce d'intégration, les liens entre le commerce et l'investissement, le commerce des services, les exportations de biens, l'IDCE, l'IDE et les sujets macroéconomiques connexes
Relever, sélectionner, nettoyer, fusionner et valider des données provenant de sources statistiques canadiennes et internationales telles que Statistique Canada, l'OCDE, l'ONU Commerce et développement (CNUCED), l'OMC, le FMI, la Banque mondiale et d'autres bases de données pertinentes
Concevoir et gérer un ensemble de données analytiques « nettoyées » appropriées pour l'analyse descriptive et économétrique
Concevoir et exécuter des modèles économétriques adaptés à la question de recherche
Interpréter les résultats d'une manière qui soit économiquement significative, pertinente et clairement communiquée
Préparer des graphiques, des tableaux, des analyses écrites et des présentations pour étayer les discussions internes et les livrables finaux
Produire un rapport final bien structuré qui expose clairement les motivations, la méthodologie, les résultats et la portée des travaux


Exigences de base


Actuellement inscrit à un programme de maîtrise ou de doctorat en économie, en économie internationale, en politique publique, en finance ou dans un domaine connexe, ou l'ayant récemment achevé
Compréhension avérée des concepts de commerce et d'investissement internationaux, en particulier dans les domaines suivants :
exportations de biens
exportations de services
investissement direct étranger (IDE)
investissement direct canadien à l'étranger (IDCE)
infrastructures de facilitation du commerce
compétitivité commerciale, productivité ou chaînes de valeur mondiales
Expérience préalable requise en matière de recherche universitaire, de thèse ou de recherche appliquée sur le commerce d'intégration ou sur des sujets étroitement connexes
Grande aisance à travailler de façon autonome avec un minimum d'aide, tout en accueillant des conseils à des étapes clés
Expérience avérée en gestion d'un projet de recherche du début à la fin, y compris sur les plans suivants :
plan de recherche
collecte et nettoyage de données
analyses empiriques
modélisation économétrique
interprétation des résultats
rédaction des résultats finaux
Solides compétences quantitatives et analytiques, y compris une expérience des méthodes économétriques
Maîtrise d'au moins un outil de programmation statistique ou analytique tel que Stata, R, Python ou équivalent
Bonne capacité à travailler avec des données dans Excel, y compris d'organiser, de nettoyer, de structurer et de présenter clairement des données
Excellentes compétences rédactionnelles et aptitude à communiquer des résultats techniques de manière claire, concise et accessible


Atouts


Expérience directe de l'utilisation d'ensembles de données canadiennes ou internationales relatives au commerce, aux services, aux IDE, aux IDCE, à la productivité ou au rendement macroéconomique
Capacité à aller au-delà de l'exécution technique et à illustrer clairement la pertinence de l'analyse
Intérêt manifeste pour les enjeux liés à la diversification commerciale, à la croissance et à la capacité de production du Canada
Expérience de production de résultats de recherche, de mémoires ou de travaux écrits de thèse susceptibles d'être publiés
Solides compétences en matière de visualisation des données et capacité à traduire les résultats empiriques en présentations et en tableaux convaincants
Maîtrise des deux langues officielles


Rémunération

Les salaires des personnes étudiantes et nouvellement diplômées et des titulaires d'une maîtrise varient de 42 900 $ à 56 550 $ par année, en fonction de l'année d'études. Vous trouverez ci-dessous la ventilation des salaires par année d'études :


1re année : 42 900 $ (22,00 $/heure)
2e année : 44 850 $ (23,00 $/heure)
3e année : 46 800 $ (24,00 $/heure)
4e année et au-delà : 48 750 $ (25,00 $/heure)
Diplôme de premier cycle : 54 600 $ (28,00 $/heure)
Maîtrise ou doctorat : 56 550 $ (29,00 $/heure)


Lieu de travail


Exportation et développement Canada évolue dans un environnement de travail hybride, avec une exigence actuelle de présence au bureau de deux jours par semaine, laquelle passera à trois jours par semaine à compter de septembre 2026 (sous réserve de modifications).
La personne choisie peut travailler à partir du siège social d'EDC à Ottawa ou à partir du Carrefour communautaire à Toronto, à Mississauga, ou à Montréal


Veuillez noter que nous communiquerons avec les candidates et les candidats de façon continue de juin à septembre 2026 pour des entretiens, en fonction de nos besoins opérationnels.

Aperçu d'un stage étudiant à EDC : C'est l'occasion de tisser des relations avec des collègues provenant de secteurs divers et variés, mais aussi de déceler et de concrétiser des occasions d'amélioration et d'innovation.C'est profiter d'un programme d'encadrement et de mentorat favorisant l'apprentissage et le développement professionnel. C'est évoluer dans un environnement ultraproductif, souple et dynamique et misant sur les technologies depuis notre superbe siège social du centre-ville d'Ottawa.

À quoi s'attendre de la part d'EDC


Vous recevrez un avis lorsque nous aurons reçu votre demande
Si votre profil correspond aux besoins d'une équipe, nous communiquerons avec vous pour fixer un entretien avec celle‑ci. Vous recevrez une description de l'équipe pour laquelle votre candidature a été sélectionnée en préparation de votre entretien


Veuillez noter que nous communiquerons avec les candidates et les candidats de façon continue de juin à août 2026 pour des entretiens, en fonction de nos besoins opérationnels.

Engagement d'EDC envers l'équité en matière d'emploi : Exportation et développement Canada (EDC) s'engage à favoriser l'équité en matière d'emploi et à constituer une main-d'œuvre diversifiée. Elle invite les femmes, les Autochtones, les personnes en situation de handicap, les membres d'une minorité visible et les membres de la communauté 2ELGBTQI+ à poser leur candidature. Si vous avez besoin de mesures d'adaptation pendant le processus de recrutement et de sélection, veuillez en informer notre équipe de recrutement.

Les personnes intéressées doivent postuler directement en ligne pour que leur candidature soit prise en considération pour ce poste. Nous remercions l'ensemble des postulantes et postulants de leur intérêt dans une carrière à EDC. Toutefois, nous communiquerons uniquement avec les personnes retenues pour une entrevue de sélection. 

Veuillez noter que nous communiquerons avec les candidates et les candidats de façon continue de juin à septembre 2026 pour des entretiens, en fonction de nos besoins opérationnels.

Programme étudiants :


Éligibilité: EDC s'engage à respecter des pratiques d'emploi équitables et la préférence sera accordée aux candidats qui sont en mesure de travailler légalement au Canada au moment de la présentation de leur candidature (citoyens canadiens, résidents permanents ou détenteur d'un permis de travail). Le poste est ouvert aux personnes qui répondent à toutes les exigences essentielles énoncées ci-dessus et dont la candidature est reçue avant la date limite.

Saisissez l'occasion d'intégrer une équipe dynamique en pleine croissance et de laisser votre marque dans notre organisation, le domaine du financement du développement et le monde.

  • Compréhension avérée des concepts de commerce et d'investissement internationaux, en particulier dans les domaines suivants :
    • exportations de biens
    • exportations de services
    • investissement direct étranger (IDE)
    • investissement direct canadien à l'étranger (IDCE)
    • infrastructures de facilitation du commerce
    • compétitivité commerciale, productivité ou chaînes de valeur mondiales
  • Expérience préalable requise en matière de recherche universitaire, de thèse ou de recherche appliquée sur le commerce d'intégration ou sur des sujets étroitement connexes
  • Grande aisance à travailler de façon autonome avec un minimum d'aide, tout en accueillant des conseils à des étapes clés
  • Expérience avérée en gestion d'un projet de recherche du début à la fin, y compris sur les plans suivants :
    • plan de recherche
    • collecte et nettoyage de données
    • analyses empiriques
    • modélisation économétrique
    • interprétation des résultats
    • rédaction des résultats finaux
  • Solides compétences quantitatives et analytiques, y compris une expérience des méthodes économétriques
  • Maîtrise d'au moins un outil de programmation statistique ou analytique tel que Stata, R, Python ou équivalent
  • Bonne capacité à travailler avec des données dans Excel, y compris d'organiser, de nettoyer, de structurer et de présenter clairement des données
  • Excellentes compétences rédactionnelles et aptitude à communiquer des résultats techniques de manière claire, concise et accessible

Atouts

  • Expérience directe de l'utilisation d'ensembles de données canadiennes ou internationales relatives au commerce, aux services, aux IDE, aux IDCE, à la productivité ou au rendement macroéconomique
  • Capacité à aller au-delà de l'exécution technique et à illustrer clairement la pertinence de l'analyse
  • Intérêt manifeste pour les enjeux liés à la diversification commerciale, à la croissance et à la capacité de production du Canada
  • Expérience de production de résultats de recherche, de mémoires ou de travaux écrits de thèse susceptibles d'être publiés
  • Solides compétences en matière de visualisation des données et capacité à traduire les résultats empiriques en présentations et en tableaux convaincants
  • Maîtrise des deux langues officielles

Rémunération

Les salaires des personnes étudiantes et nouvellement diplômées et des titulaires d'une maîtrise varient de 42 900 $ à 56 550 $ par année, en fonction de l'année d'études. Vous trouverez ci-dessous la ventilation des salaires par année d'études :

  • 1re année : 42 900 $ (22,00 $/heure)
  • 2e année : 44 850 $ (23,00 $/heure)
  • 3e année : 46 800 $ (24,00 $/heure)
  • 4e année et au-delà : 48 750 $ (25,00 $/heure)
  • Diplôme de premier cycle : 54 600 $ (28,00 $/heure)
  • Maîtrise ou doctorat : 56 550 $ (29,00 $/heure)

Lieu de travail

  • Exportation et développement Canada évolue dans un environnement de travail hybride, avec une exigence actuelle de présence au bureau de deux jours par semaine, laquelle passera à trois jours par semaine à compter de septembre 2026 (sous réserve de modifications).
  • La personne choisie peut travailler à partir du siège social d'EDC à Ottawa ou à partir du Carrefour communautaire à Toronto, à Mississauga, ou à Montréal

Veuillez noter que nous communiquerons avec les candidates et les candidats de façon continue de juin à septembre 2026 pour des entretiens, en fonction de nos besoins opérationnels.

C'est évoluer dans un environnement ultraproductif, souple et dynamique et misant sur les technologies depuis notre superbe siège social du centre-ville d'Ottawa.

Title: Data Annotator (Remote)
Description:
Role: Data Annotator (Remote)
Job Type: Part-Time Contract
Location: Remote (Work From Home)
Compensation: Up to $20 USD/hr, paid weekly




About the Role

We are looking for fluent language professionals to help train and evaluate AI language models. This is a flexible, part-time contract role you can do entirely from home, on your own schedule. If you work with language professionally - as a writer, teacher, translator, tutor, linguist, or language specialist - this role is a strong fit.




What You'll Do

Read and evaluate AI-generated text for quality, accuracy, and fluency
Write short prompts and creative responses to help train AI systems
Rank and compare AI outputs based on clear evaluation criteria
Provide written feedback on language quality




What We're Looking For

Fluency in your language and English
Bachelor's degree or higher, preferably in a humanities, language, or writing-related field
Professional experience working with language - writing, teaching, translating, editing, annotating, or related fields
Strong attention to detail and ability to follow evaluation guidelines
Background in linguistics, academic writing, data annotation, or AI content evaluation is a plus




What You Get

Up to $20 USD/hr, paid weekly
Fully remote - work from anywhere
Flexible hours - you set your own schedule
Part-time contract - ideal as a primary or supplemental income source




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.




Apply Now!

Title: Administrative Assistant II
Description:
Company Description

UHN is Canada’s #1 hospital and the world’s #1 publicly funded hospital. With 10 sites and more than 44,000 TeamUHN members, UHN consists of Toronto General Hospital, Toronto Western Hospital, Princess Margaret Cancer Centre, Toronto Rehabilitation Institute, The Michener Institute of Education and West Park Healthcare Centre. As Canada's top research hospital, the scope of biomedical research and complexity of cases at UHN have made it a national and international source for discovery, education and patient care. UHN has the largest hospital-based research program in Canada, with major research in neurosciences, cardiology, transplantation, oncology, surgical innovation, infectious diseases, genomic medicine and rehabilitation medicine. UHN is a research hospital affiliated with the University of Toronto.

UHN’s vision is to build A Healthier World and it’s only because of the talented and dedicated people who work here that we are continually bringing that vision closer to reality.

www.uhn.ca

Job Description

Union: Non-Union

Number of Vacancies: 1

New or Replacement Position: Replacement

Site: Toronto General Hospital/Toronto Western Hospital

Department:  General Internal Medicine Acute Inpatient Medicine Service

Reports to: Physician

Salary: $25.56 - $31.95 per hour

Hours: 37.5 hours per week

Shifts: Weekdays; Monday - Friday

Status: Permanent Full-Time

Closing Date: June 23, 2026

Job Summary

The UHN General Internal Medicine Acute Inpatient Medicine Service (GIM-AIM) is seeking an Administrative Assistant (Program Assistant) to provide administrative support for this team of physicians. The AIM program is a group of attending General Internists and Family Medicine hospitalists who are responsible for the care of inpatients at TGH and TWH, and, as physicians are often cross-appointed at both UHN and Sinai Health, nocturnal coverage at Sinai Health. This role is essential to the operational success of the AIM Program and the newly integrated Mount Sinai Hospital (MSH) Nocturnist Program. The ideal candidate will have exceptional organizational and communication skills and will be able to prioritize and manage multiple tasks and projects concurrently. The successful candidate will be responsible for managing the day-to-day administrative duties of the AIM program, including coordinating schedules, credentialing new physicians, preparing reports and providing support to the Physician Team Leads, the Site Leads and the GIM Division Head for all aspects of this Program. Approximately 0.75 FTE of the work is anticipated to supporting the AIM and MSH programs, while the remaining 0.25 FTE will be acting as administrative support for one or more physician Faculty members who require scheduling and other organizational/administrative support. This position will largely be split between on-site at TGH (1-2 days/week) and TWH (2-3 days/week). In rare instances, there will be occasional mandatory in-person events or activities.

Duties

AIM Support


Provide administrative support to the AIM Team Co-Leads and Faculty
Assist with the creation of an annual schedule for the AIM program and keep this group of physicians up to date with regular email communication (for example, monthly reminders for operational announcements and schedule tracking)
Assist in recruitment, interviews and onboarding paperwork and credentialing for all physicians in the AIM program, both at the hospital and university level
Assisting with collection of annual 360 reviews for AIM physicians and all other documents that require central collection by the program
Prepare and distribute reports and internal communications as needed
Assist in the development and implementation of new policies and procedures for the AIM Program and keep communications and paperwork filed and organized
Perform data entry and data management tasks as needed
Assist in the editing, formatting, and updating of preparation of lists, documents, presentations and other materials using software, including Microsoft SharePoint, Microsoft Office 365,
Organize, attend and participate in regular team meetings, and others as needed, including setting up virtual meetings, taking minutes, keeping attendance and following up action items between meetings.
Perform other administrative duties to support the AIM program as assigned
As physicians are all cross-appointed to Sinai Health as well as UHN, there may be onboarding and credentialing administrative needs for physicians at both hospitals, including cross-scheduling for the Sinai nocturnist program



Other Faculty Support


Administrative support for one or more other physician Faculty members, including: schedule management (including organizing meetings but not usually attending them); organizing and keeping academic CV and other files up to date; managing/tracking assigned email, faxes and other communications requiring follow-up; and file organization.
There may be interaction with patients’ Personal Health Information and occasional patient contact incidental to the support role for the physician(s)



Qualifications


Completion of Grade XII or recognized equivalent
Completion of a recognized post-secondary medical office administration program at the diploma level or equivalent
Three (3) years of related medical administrative support experience
Working knowledge of telephone system, photocopier, fax machine, printer and various other office equipment; working knowledge of PC back-up procedures
Excellent time management and organizational skills; ability to work under pressure, effectively prioritize workload and manage competing priorities
Proficiency in Microsoft Office 365, including Excel, PowerPoint and Word, as well as other technology tools, including Microsoft Teams and Zoom
Ability to work independently and collaboratively as part of a team
Knowledge of medical terminology and hospital operations a plus, in particular an understanding of physician credentialing at the hospital and university level
Able to handle sensitive and confidential information with discretion and professionalism, including occasional confidential matters
Excellent attendance history – this position is an on-site position. Detail oriented, dependable, mature and flexible, with initiative and capacity to handle a variety of complex tasks simultaneously with demonstrated ability to function well independently and in a professional manner
Excellent customer service skills with a problem-solving approach
Excellent verbal, written and interpersonal communication skills



Additional Information

In addition to working alongside some of the most talented and inspiring healthcare professionals in the world, UHN offers a wide range of benefits, programs and perks. It is the comprehensiveness of these offerings that makes it a differentiating factor, allowing you to find value where it matters most to you, now and throughout your career at UHN.


Competitive offer packages
Government organization and a member of the Healthcare of Ontario Pension Plan (HOOPP https://hoopp.com/)
Close access to Transit and UHN shuttle service
A flexible work environment
Opportunities for development and promotions within a large organization
Additional perks (multiple corporate discounts including: travel, restaurants, parking, phone plans, auto insurance discounts, on-site gyms, etc.)



Current UHN employees must have successfully completed their probationary period, have a good employee record along with satisfactory attendance in accordance with UHN's attendance management program, to be eligible for consideration.

All applications must be submitted before the posting close date.

UHN uses email to communicate with selected candidates. Please ensure you check your email regularly.

Please be advised that a Criminal Record Check may be required of the successful candidate. Should it be determined that any information provided by a candidate be misleading, inaccurate or incorrect, UHN reserves the right to discontinue with the consideration of their application.

UHN is an equal opportunity employer committed to an inclusive recruitment process and workplace. Requests for accommodation can be made at any stage of the recruitment process. Applicants need to make their requirements known.

We thank all applicants for their interest, however, only those selected for further consideration will be contacted.

Title: Senior Business Systems Analyst
Description:
RAVL helps technologists accelerate their careers.

At RAVL, we connect strategy with execution, care deeply about the people we work with, and measure success by the lasting impact we leave behind. Our purpose is to build a team that puts real, sustainable business outcomes at the core of everything we do.

We’re here to leave our clients better than we found them, and to create a place where our people are proud to Build. Better.

As a Business Systems Analyst in our Delivery guild, you’ll play a critical role at the intersection of business and technology. This role is more technical in nature, working closely with engineering teams and directly with clients to translate complex business needs into requirements which lead to scalable system solutions.

You’ll bring a systems-thinking mindset, leveraging your technical background to ensure requirements are not only clear—but feasible, efficient, and aligned to architecture.

What does success look like in this role?


Partner directly with client stakeholders to elicit, analyze, and document business and system requirements
Translate business objectives into detailed functional and non-functional specifications with technical depth
Act as a bridge between business and engineering—ensuring shared understanding of requirements, constraints, and trade-offs
Facilitate discovery sessions, technical workshops, and stakeholder interviews to uncover system needs and dependencies
Create high-quality artifacts including user stories, process flows, data mappings, and acceptance criteria
Collaborate closely with developers, architects, QA, and product teams to support solution design and delivery
Contribute to backlog refinement, prioritization, and scope management with a strong understanding of technical implications
Identify risks, integration challenges, and system gaps early—proactively communicating impacts and mitigation strategies
Support testing efforts including UAT, validation, and stakeholder walkthroughs to ensure solutions meet business intent
Continuously improve analysis practices, documentation quality, and delivery efficiency within the team



Great, do my skills fit?

You’ll thrive in this role if you bring:


5+ years of experience as a Business Analyst or Business Systems Analyst in technology delivery environments
A strong technical foundation—previous experience as a developer or working closely with engineering teams is highly valued
Proven ability to translate complex business processes into clear, structured technical requirements
Hands-on experience working in Agile or hybrid delivery models
Strong experience writing user stories, acceptance criteria, and functional/system documentation
Familiarity with system design concepts, APIs, integrations, and data flows
Experience working with tools such as Jira, Confluence, Azure DevOps, or similar
Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and problem-solving capabilities
Consulting mindset: clear communication, stakeholder management, and outcome orientation
Comfort working directly with clients in fast-paced, evolving delivery environments



Mindset Traits


Systems thinker: you naturally connect business needs to technical solutions
Structured but adaptable: you bring clarity to ambiguity without slowing momentum
Technically curious: you enjoy understanding how things work under the hood
Collaborative: you build trust across business and engineering teams
Outcome-driven: you focus on delivering real, measurable value for clients



Why join RAVL?


Flexible, client-aligned work model — autonomy with accountability, adapting to client delivery needs
Variable bonus & RRSP contributions tied to performance and delivery impact
4 weeks paid time off (plus public holidays)
Paid professional development days and continuous learning opportunities
Comprehensive health & dental coverage, including mental health support
Commitment to lifelong learning — continuous improvement through training, mentorship, and certification



Compensation & Hiring Process

We are recruiting for a current opportunity. The salary range for this role is $85,000 - $125,000. Total compensation may also include additional pay such as bonuses or incentives, depending on the position, and final offers are based on experience, skills, and qualifications.

As part of our hiring process, we may use technology, including AI-based tools, to help summarize and assess applications; these tools assist our team and do not replace human review or decision-making.

Equal Opportunity & Accessibility

RAVL is an equal opportunity employer committed to building a diverse, inclusive, and accessible workplace. We welcome applications from all qualified individuals and provide accommodations throughout the hiring process upon request.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

We’re here to leave our clients better than we found them, and to create a place where our people are proud to Build. Better.

As a Business Systems Analyst in our Delivery guild, you’ll play a critical role at the intersection of business and technology. This role is more technical in nature, working closely with engineering teams and directly with clients to translate complex business needs into requirements which lead to scalable system solutions.

You’ll bring a systems-thinking mindset, leveraging your technical background to ensure requirements are not only clear—but feasible, efficient, and aligned to architecture.

What does success look like in this role?

  • Partner directly with client stakeholders to elicit, analyze, and document business and system requirements
  • Translate business objectives into detailed functional and non-functional specifications with technical depth
  • Act as a bridge between business and engineering—ensuring shared understanding of requirements, constraints, and trade-offs
  • Facilitate discovery sessions, technical workshops, and stakeholder interviews to uncover system needs and dependencies
  • Create high-quality artifacts including user stories, process flows, data mappings, and acceptance criteria
  • Collaborate closely with developers, architects, QA, and product teams to support solution design and delivery
  • Contribute to backlog refinement, prioritization, and scope management with a strong understanding of technical implications
  • Identify risks, integration challenges, and system gaps early—proactively communicating impacts and mitigation strategies
  • Support testing efforts including UAT, validation, and stakeholder walkthroughs to ensure solutions meet business intent
  • Continuously improve analysis practices, documentation quality, and delivery efficiency within the team


Great, do my skills fit?

You’ll thrive in this role if you bring:

  • 5+ years of experience as a Business Analyst or Business Systems Analyst in technology delivery environments
  • A strong technical foundation—previous experience as a developer or working closely with engineering teams is highly valued
  • Proven ability to translate complex business processes into clear, structured technical requirements
  • Hands-on experience working in Agile or hybrid delivery models
  • Strong experience writing user stories, acceptance criteria, and functional/system documentation
  • Familiarity with system design concepts, APIs, integrations, and data flows
  • Experience working with tools such as Jira, Confluence, Azure DevOps, or similar
  • Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and problem-solving capabilities
  • Consulting mindset: clear communication, stakeholder management, and outcome orientation
  • Comfort working directly with clients in fast-paced, evolving delivery environments


Mindset Traits

  • Systems thinker: you naturally connect business needs to technical solutions
  • Structured but adaptable: you bring clarity to ambiguity without slowing momentum
  • Technically curious: you enjoy understanding how things work under the hood
  • Collaborative: you build trust across business and engineering teams
  • Outcome-driven: you focus on delivering real, measurable value for clients


Why join RAVL?

  • Flexible, client-aligned work model — autonomy with accountability, adapting to client delivery needs
  • Variable bonus & RRSP contributions tied to performance and delivery impact
  • 4 weeks paid time off (plus public holidays)
  • Paid professional development days and continuous learning opportunities
  • Comprehensive health & dental coverage, including mental health support
  • Commitment to lifelong learning — continuous improvement through training, mentorship, and certification


Compensation & Hiring Process

We are recruiting for a current opportunity. The salary range for this role is $85,000 - $125,000. Total compensation may also include additional pay such as bonuses or incentives, depending on the position, and final offers are based on experience, skills, and qualifications.

As part of our hiring process, we may use technology, including AI-based tools, to help summarize and assess applications; these tools assist our team and do not replace human review or decision-making.

Equal Opportunity & Accessibility

RAVL is an equal opportunity employer committed to building a diverse, inclusive, and accessible workplace. We welcome applications from all qualified individuals and provide accommodations throughout the hiring process upon request.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Executive Assistant
Description:
Work Location:

Toronto, Ontario, Canada

Hours

37.5

Line Of Business

Administration

Pay Details

$65,000 - $85,000 CAD

This role is eligible for a discretionary variable compensation award that considers business and individual performance.

TD is committed to providing fair and equitable compensation opportunities to all colleagues. Growth opportunities and skill development are defining features of the colleague experience at TD. Our compensation policies and practices have been designed to allow colleagues to progress through the salary range over time as they progress in their role. The base pay actually offered may vary based upon the candidate's skills and experience, job-related knowledge, geographic location, and other specific business and organizational needs.

As a candidate, you are encouraged to ask compensation related questions and have an open dialogue with your recruiter who can provide you more specific details for this role.

Job Description

Department Overview

At TD Bank Group (TD), everything we do is driven by our passion to provide legendary customer experiences. In order to reach and exceed the commitments we make to our customers and greater communities, our executive team seeks out and relies on exceptional people for support. Alongside caring colleagues and supportive leaders, our administrative and executive assistants provide a broad range of organizational and general workplace support to executives within TD.

TD Securities offers a wide range of capital markets products and services to corporate, government, and institutional clients who choose us for our innovation, execution, and experience.

With more than 6,900 professionals operating out of 40 cities across the globe, we help clients meet their needs today and prepare for tomorrow. Our services include underwriting and distributing new issues, providing trusted advice and industry-leading insight, extending access to global markets, and delivering integrated transaction banking solutions. In 2023, we acquired Cowen Inc., offering our clients access to a premier U.S. equities business and highly-diverse equity research franchise, while growing our strong, diversified investment bank.

Job Details

If you're a dynamic administrative professional who can support executives and help maintain smooth business operations, we want you to join our team.

As an Executive Assistant, you'll provide a range of administrative and general workplace support for our senior leaders.

What You'll Do

No matter where you work at TD you’ll make a meaningful impact, grow your skills and thrive in our culture of care. As a valued member of our executive team your work will involve a range of complexity and may include the following accountabilities:

Make An Impact


Own and maintain calendar management for multiple leaders (Investment Banking Managing Directors; may include their respective teams) and troubleshoot conflicts with creative solutions
Coordinate travel for leaders, including supporting travel delay re-scheduling for national an international travel arrangements, in addition to rescheduling meetings with internal/external parties
Process expenses for teams and ensure timely completion
Assist in coordinating in-office or virtual meetings, townhalls or offsite events; may include booking meeting spaces, agenda development, technology setup, amalgamating/proofreading PowerPoint decks, event day troubleshooting and ordering catering with dietary restrictions
Maintain confidentiality: Possible access to leaders' emails to assist in prioritizing or performing important administrative tasks on their behalf
Apply appropriate due diligence ensuring accuracy in completing ad-hoc administrative tasks (e.g., reports, invoices, communications, tables, charts, files)
Inform immediate team regularly on project status, as well as relevant information related to day-to-day activities
Provide vacation/temporary coverage for colleagues, supporting additional leaders Experience Growth:
Provide training to new Administrative Assistants/Executive Assistants and be a Subject Matter Expert for common tasks performed
Work with complete autonomy alongside leader; may partner with Special Assistant or other leaders to finish unique tasks Cultivate our Culture of Care:
Provide support while ensuring quality service and professionalism at every interaction
Participate fully as a member of the team and contribute to a positive work environment


What You Need To Succeed


We’re proud to work with a group of diverse colleagues. If you have relevant experience that isn’t mentioned below, tell us about it in your resume or cover letter.
High school diploma with over 5 years of relevant experience
Extensive knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite and internet applications (Outlook, PowerPoint, Word, etc.)
Ability to work independently as well as part of a team with flexibility to coordinate processes an information across related teams
Ability to collaborate with other Executive Assistants/Administrative Assistants, in addition to internal and external partners
Ability to think strategically and multi-task while able to meet conflicting deadlines
Experience coordinating travel arrangements and comfortable managing frequent changes
Strong organizational skillset to manage accountabilities, while simultaneously managing leaders' administrative requirements
Excellent communication skills with the ability to listen actively, ask questions and communicate effectively
Can efficiently interpret TD policies and help executives apply these standards in a fast-changing environment
Strong judgment skills to support initiatives and apply discretion when working with confidential information


Colleague Development Through regular development conversations and training programs, we’re committed to providing our colleagues the support they need to thrive both at work and at home.

Applications must include:


Cover letter (one letter-sized page only)
Resume (two letter-sized pages only)


Interview Process We’ll reach out to candidates of interest to schedule an interview. We do our best to communicate outcomes to all applicants by email or phone call.

Who We Are

TD Securities offers a wide range of capital markets products and services to corporate, government, and institutional clients who choose us for our innovation, execution, and experience. With more than 6,500 professionals operating out of 40 cities across the globe, we strive to make every interaction, product and experience remarkably human and refreshingly simple. Our services include underwriting and distributing new issues, providing trusted advice and industry-leading insight, extending access to global markets, and delivering integrated transaction banking solutions. In 2023, we acquired Cowen Inc., offering our clients access to a premier U.S. equities business and highly-diverse equity research franchise, while growing our strong, diversified investment bank.

Together, we are reimagining what banking can be for our clients, colleagues and communities.

Our Total Rewards Package

Our Total Rewards package reflects the investments we make in our colleagues to help them and their families achieve their financial, physical, and mental well-being goals. Total Rewards at TD includes a base salary, variable compensation, and several other key plans such as health and well-being benefits, savings and retirement programs, paid time off, banking benefits and discounts, career development, and reward and recognition programs. Learn more

Additional Information

We’re delighted that you’re considering building a career with TD. Through regular development conversations, training programs, and a competitive benefits plan, we’re committed to providing the support our colleagues need to thrive both at work and at home.

Please be advised that this job opportunity is subject to provincial regulation for employment purposes. It is imperative to acknowledge that each province or territory within the jurisdiction of Canada may have its own set of regulations, requirements.

Colleague Development

If you’re interested in a specific career path or are looking to build certain skills, we want to help you succeed. You’ll have regular career, development, and performance conversations with your manager, as well as access to an online learning platform and a variety of mentoring programs to help you unlock future opportunities.

If you’re passionate about helping clients and building deep, lasting relationships, TD offers diverse career paths where you can grow your expertise and make a meaningful impact.

We're committed to your success and foster a respectful workplace where diverse perspectives are valued, everyone has fair opportunities to grow, and you can unlock your full potential to achieve your career goals. Here at TD, we hire and develop the best.

Training & Onboarding

We will provide training and onboarding sessions to ensure that you’ve got everything you need to succeed in your new role.

Interview Process

We’ll reach out to candidates of interest to schedule an interview. We do our best to communicate outcomes to all applicants by email or phone call.

Accommodation

Your accessibility is important to us. Please let us know if you’d like accommodations (including accessible meeting rooms, captioning for virtual interviews, etc.) to help us remove barriers so that you can participate throughout the interview process.

We look forward to hearing from you!

Language Requirement (Quebec Only)

Title: Office and Administrative Specialist (Remote)
Description:
Role: Office and Administrative Specialist (Remote)
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Payout: $20 - $40/hour




Role Overview:

We are hiring for one of our clients, seeking an Office and Administrative Domains Specialist to work on a contractor basis. This specialist will apply their expertise to help train next-generation AI systems, focusing on office and administrative domains. The goal is to provide high-quality, real-world input that shapes how models learn, reason, and perform, ultimately contributing to the development of reliable AI agents in various industries. The specialist will work closely with the client to review and analyze business documents, extract key information, and create clear, structured questions or insights.




Key Responsibilities:

• Review and analyze business documents, standard operating procedures, reports, and workflows to identify key information and create clear, structured questions or insights.

• Perform basic analytical and reasoning tasks to extract key information and identify inconsistencies or gaps in operational content.

• Deliver high-quality written work, including clear and concise reports, summaries, and analyses of business documents and workflows.

• Identify and document areas for process improvement, providing recommendations for increasing efficiency and effectiveness in office and administrative domains.

• Collaborate with the client to provide feedback and insights on the development of AI models, ensuring that they are accurate, reliable, and effective.




Required Skills & Qualifications:

• Strong office administration skills, including experience with executive assistance, written communication, and verbal communication.

• Proficiency in digital office tools, including document editing and spreadsheet software, with the ability to learn new tools and technologies quickly.

• Analytical thinking and attention to detail, with the ability to identify inconsistencies and gaps in operational content.

• Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively in a remote team environment.

• Strong organizational and time management skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.




More About the Opportunity:

This role offers a unique opportunity to work with a global leader in the technology and AI industry, contributing to the development of next-generation AI systems and models. The specialist will have the opportunity to work on a variety of projects, applying their expertise to real-world problems and making a meaningful impact on the development of reliable AI agents.




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.

Title: Analyst/Senior Analyst, Debt Investments
Description:
About the Company:

Founded in 2000, Timbercreek is one of Canada’s leading alternative asset class investment managers, focused on providing structured financing solutions to experienced real estate owners and investors across selectively identified urban centres primarily in Canada, Ireland and the United States. Through active and direct investment, we employ a thematic approach to deliver compelling risk-adjusted returns for our investors and partners, leveraging the diversified expertise and relationships of our highly experienced team to invest capital across a wide range of asset classes. Timbercreek’s team of 50 investment professionals have extensive domain expertise in these markets and combine an entrepreneurial growth focus with institutional risk management. Since 2000, the Timbercreek team has actively sourced, underwritten, funded and managed real estate debt or equity transactions that deployed over $18 billion of capital, financed by public and private Timbercreek investment vehicles, as well as many institutional partners.




About the Position:

Timbercreek is seeking an Analyst/Senior Analyst, Debt Investments to be part of its Global Debt Investments team. This individual will thrive in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment and will take part in the full Investment process from deal sizing/structuring to closing to ongoing asset management post-closing. The ideal candidate is a results-oriented real estate and finance professional, a problem solver and creative thinker, self-starter, and most importantly, a team player excited to grow with the company. This role is part of our hybrid work model.




Key Responsibilities:




Underwriting

Support the Investment Team on the evaluation of new mortgage investment opportunities on commercial real estate asset classes including, but not limited to, office, retail, industrial, residential, hotels, land, and development projects.
With support from Senior Management prepare detailed underwriting of commercial real estate assets including preliminary proformas to detailed financial models.
Conduct market research as required.
Correspond with clients, brokers, and other industry professionals to validate and further substantiate the investment assumptions and gain better understanding of particular markets and asset classes.
Conduct in-depth due diligence on the credit worthiness of sponsors/clients and other financial data relevant to transactions.
Identify risks and mitigants and provide feedback to the Investment team based on preliminary underwriting and due diligence.
Prepare term sheets and/or commitment letters consistent with preliminary underwriting and pre-approved deal structure.
Provide potential investment partners with underwriting and transaction materials to support syndication activities.




Preparation, Due Diligence, and Closing

Under the guidance of Senior Management draft Investment Committee Memos for submission and approval.
Ensure all required due diligence material is obtained and reviewed, and is consistent with preliminary underwriting, deal structure, and Investment Committee approval.
Where required, and in conjunction with the Origination team, perform site inspections and meet with clients, property managers, appraisers, and other related stakeholders.
Together with the Originator, the Analyst/Senior Analyst will be a point person in the due diligence process (both internal and external) and assist all relevant teams throughout the closing process.




Portfolio and Asset Management

Support the Investment team by providing proactive asset management of funded/closed investments (i.e. mortgage renewals, fundings, restructurings and annual reviews).
Perform active financial updates and reporting including updating financial models, client budgets and maintaining up to date documentation and information management.
Assist the syndication, funding and servicing teams with post-closing follow ups, as required.




Job Requirements:




Education and Experience:

Up to 4 years of experience in one of the following: Real Estate or Structured Finance, Private Equity, Private Debt, Investment Banking, Fund Management or a major financial institution.
Ability to perform financial modeling and run valuation analysis as part of the underwriting process.
Working knowledge of the commercial real estate sector and/or capital markets is considered an asset.
Experience with commercial real estate software (i.e. CoStar and Argus) is considered an asset.
Minimum undergraduate degree in Finance, Economics, Business/Commerce, a Real Estate program,  Engineering, Mathematics or a related discipline.
Completion/enrollment in a professional designation (e.g. CAIA, CFA, CPA) is considered an asset.




Qualifications:

Fluently bilingual in both French and English is considered an asset.
Strong analytical, strategic-thinking, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.
Inquisitive and curious to understand the full transaction cycle and related participants.
Superior attention to detail and accuracy.
Ability to work with, analyze and organize large datasets.
Excellent communication (both written and verbal) skills and the poise and professionalism to represent Timbercreek in front of both internal and external parties.
Strong organizational and time management skills and the ability to work under pressure on multiple transactions concurrently.
Professional and positive with a “can-do” deal and team focused attitude.
Ability to work independently prioritizing and establishing deadlines for own work, and when work is delegated, for the work of others.
Advanced computer skills (Excel, Power Point and Word).
Willingness to learn, take on new challenges and grow in the role.







Timbercreek Capital is an inclusive and equal opportunity employer. If you require a disability–related accommodation to participate in the recruitment process, please email us. We will accommodate your needs under the Ontario Human Rights Code. While we appreciate all applications, only those candidates selected for an interview will be contacted. We thank all applicants for their submissions.

  • Ability to perform financial modeling and run valuation analysis as part of the underwriting process.
  • Working knowledge of the commercial real estate sector and/or capital markets is considered an asset.
  • Experience with commercial real estate software (i.e. CoStar and Argus) is considered an asset.
  • Minimum undergraduate degree in Finance, Economics, Business/Commerce, a Real Estate program,  Engineering, Mathematics or a related discipline.
  • Completion/enrollment in a professional designation (e.g. CAIA, CFA, CPA) is considered an asset.


Qualifications:

  • Fluently bilingual in both French and English is considered an asset.
  • Strong analytical, strategic-thinking, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.
  • Inquisitive and curious to understand the full transaction cycle and related participants.
  • Superior attention to detail and accuracy.
  • Ability to work with, analyze and organize large datasets.
  • Excellent communication (both written and verbal) skills and the poise and professionalism to represent Timbercreek in front of both internal and external parties.
  • Strong organizational and time management skills and the ability to work under pressure on multiple transactions concurrently.
  • Professional and positive with a “can-do” deal and team focused attitude.
  • Ability to work independently prioritizing and establishing deadlines for own work, and when work is delegated, for the work of others.
  • Advanced computer skills (Excel, Power Point and Word).
  • Willingness to learn, take on new challenges and grow in the role.



Timbercreek Capital is an inclusive and equal opportunity employer. If you require a disability–related accommodation to participate in the recruitment process, please email us. We will accommodate your needs under the Ontario Human Rights Code. While we appreciate all applications, only those candidates selected for an interview will be contacted. We thank all applicants for their submissions.

Title: Clinical Systems Analyst
Description:
Clinical Systems Analyst (AI Training)

About The Role

Your healthcare IT expertise is more valuable than ever — not just in hospitals, but in shaping the next generation of AI. At Alignerr, we partner with the world's leading AI research labs to build and train cutting-edge models. We're looking for Clinical Systems Analysts to help ensure AI understands the complex, high-stakes world of clinical technology.

This is a fully remote, flexible contract role where your real-world knowledge of EHRs, clinical workflows, and healthcare infrastructure directly improves how AI reasons about these systems.


Organization: Alignerr
Type: Hourly Contract
Location: Remote
Commitment: 10–40 hours/week


What You'll Do


Evaluate and provide expert feedback on AI-generated responses related to clinical systems, healthcare IT, and hospital workflows
Review technical scenarios involving EHR configurations, system troubleshooting, and clinical application support for accuracy and realism
Identify gaps, errors, or unsafe recommendations in AI outputs and suggest improvements
Translate your clinical and technical experience into structured, actionable feedback that helps AI models reason more accurately
Work independently and asynchronously on your own schedule


Who You Are


Experienced in supporting or administering healthcare IT systems — EHRs, clinical applications, or hospital infrastructure
Strong technical troubleshooting and system optimization skills
Able to bridge the gap between clinical workflows and technical implementation
Detail-oriented with clear written communication skills
Comfortable working independently on task-based assignments


Nice to Have


Prior experience with data annotation, data quality, or AI evaluation workflows
Background in EHR platforms such as Epic, Cerner, or Meditech
Experience in clinical informatics, health IT consulting, or systems integration


Why Join Us


Work on cutting-edge AI projects alongside top research labs and teams
Fully remote and flexible — set your own hours and workload
Freelance perks: autonomy, variety, and global collaboration
Contribute meaningful domain expertise to AI systems that impact healthcare
Potential for ongoing work and contract extension
