Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
About Pacific Blue Cross

Pacific Blue Cross (PBC) has been British Columbia's leading benefits provider for over 80 years. We are an independent, not-for-profit society with strong roots in BC’s health care system. We provide health, dental, life, disability, and travel coverage to 1 in 3 British Columbians through employee group plans and individual plans.

We are fueled by a commitment to keep health care sustainable for all British Columbians. Through our PBC Health Foundation, we fund projects that improve health outcomes directly related to mental health and wellbeing as well as the prevention and/or management of chronic disease. We are interested in finding people who want to make a difference and who are looking to grow their career with us.

We offer an attractive compensation and benefits package. Our workplace culture values health and wellness, diversity and equality, continuing education, environmental sustainability and giving back to the community.

Perks


Flexible working hours of 7.5 hours per day, Monday to Friday (i.e., 37.5 hours per week).
Paid vacation starts at 4 weeks per year, and increases with years of service.
Hybrid work environment (i.e., a combination of work from office and work from home days).
Generous benefits, including extended health, dental, and life insurance; depending on the plan that you choose, these benefit premiums can be 100% paid by PBC.
PBC contributes the equivalent of 8% of your base salary to a Defined Contribution pension plan. No employee contribution or matching is required, giving you more take-home pay.
Retiree Benefits plan that is available to employees who have at least 10 years of service with PBC and have reached the age of 55.
Onsite gym, cafeteria, and access to virtual doctors/counsellors 24/7 via our Employee Family Assistance Program!


About The Position


We are searching for a permanent Senior Data Analyst to join our Strategy and Analytics team.
PBC’s compensation program is built on a pay-for-performance philosophy, rewarding both individual and organizational achievements. The typical hiring range for this position is $100,000 - $120,000 per year, with the starting salary determined based on a combination of skills, education, experience, and internal equity. As part of our total rewards package, you’ll be eligible for our annual bonus program.


Imagine stepping into a role where your work turns complex data into clarity that leaders can use to make decisions.

Here, you are not just building reports, you are helping shape how the business understands itself. You work closely with stakeholders to take messy, complex datasets and turn them into meaningful insights that drive strategy, improve performance, and support better decisions across the organization.

This role is ideal for someone with experience as a Data Analyst, Senior BI Analyst, Senior Business Intelligence Analyst, BI Developer, or Analytics Developer, with strong hands-on skills in Python, SQL, and Power BI.

You are naturally analytical, resourceful, and comfortable working in ambiguity. You enjoy solving problems without clear instructions and can work independently while staying connected to the bigger business picture. You don’t just analyze data you understand what the business is trying to achieve and translate information into something actionable.

You are also energized by innovation and emerging technologies, including AI and modern analytics tools, and you are always looking for better, smarter ways to solve problems. Most importantly, you know that insights only matter when they are understood and used so you focus on clear, simple, and impactful storytelling.

If this sounds like the kind of work you want to do, we would love to hear from you. Apply today and bring your expertise in data, analytics, and storytelling to a role where your insights will directly influence decisions, shape strategy, and help the organization see the business more clearly.

We offer an attractive compensation and benefits package. Our workplace culture values health and wellness, diversity and equality, continuing education, environmental sustainability and giving back to the community.

This role is currently working hybrid from home and from our head office in Burnaby, BC. Apply this week if you would like to take on this role.

Key Ways This Position Makes An Impact

In this role, you sit at the center of decision making, where data, business understanding, and strategy all come together.

You start with large, complex datasets and bring structure to them using Python and SQL cleaning, transforming, and exploring the data to uncover trends, risks, and opportunities that are not immediately visible.

From there, your work shifts from technical analysis to meaning. You translate complex findings into clear, business friendly insights, turning analysis into dashboards, reports, and narratives that help leaders quickly understand what is happening and what actions are needed.

At the same time, you play a key role in strengthening how data is used across the organization. Working with databases, Business Intelligence (Power BI) tools, and data integration systems, you help ensure information is reliable, consistent, and easy to access so teams can trust and rely on it.

Over time, your contributions help mature the organization’s analytics capability, reducing reliance on ad hoc reporting and enabling more standardized, scalable, and trusted insights that support better decision-making.

You also help move the organization forward by supporting predictive thinking and engaging with AI-enabled and modern analytics tools, helping leaders shift from reactive decisions to more proactive, forward-looking planning.

Ultimately, your work makes a simple but powerful difference: leaders see the business more clearly, understand it more deeply, and make better decisions with confidence, backed by trusted data.

Key Experiences You Bring To This Role


Bachelor’s degree in data science, statistics, mathematics, or a related quantitative field.
5+ years of related experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or data science.
Proficiency with SQL, Python, and/or R for data manipulation and statistical analysis.
Proficiency with modern business intelligence tools such as Power BI (preferred), Tableau, and/or Looker for data visualization.
Demonstrated ability to analyze large datasets and translate findings into meaningful recommendations.
Strong critical thinking, problem solving, and analytical skills.
Excellent time management and organizational abilities, with capacity to balance competing priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Strong interpersonal and communication skills with a proven ability to collaborate effectively across teams and build relationships with stakeholders.


If you’re excited about taking on this opportunity, we invite you to apply today and start the conversation with us about this position.

Please apply via our website this week at https://www.pac.bluecross.ca/careers

While we thank all applicants for their interest, only short-listed candidates will be contacted.

Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

PBC is an Indigenous Works employer of choice, an Insurance Business Canada Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion award winner, one of Canada’s Greenest employers, a Canadian Centre for Diversity and Inclusion (CCDI) employer partner, and a Pride at Work partner.

We strive to create a workplace where everyone feels valued and employees feel empowered to freely participate and contribute regardless of race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, religion, ability, education level, parental status, or socioeconomic status. We are an equal opportunity employer and welcome applications from all qualified candidates.

To request an accommodation in completing this job application, testing, interviewing, or otherwise participating in the employee selection process, please direct your inquiries to careers@pac.bluecross.ca. These accommodation requests will be reviewed confidentially and, on a case-by-case basis by Human Resources.

  • Onsite gym, cafeteria, and access to virtual doctors/counsellors 24/7 via our Employee Family Assistance Program!

About The Position

  • We are searching for a permanent Senior Data Analyst to join our Strategy and Analytics team.
  • PBC’s compensation program is built on a pay-for-performance philosophy, rewarding both individual and organizational achievements. The typical hiring range for this position is $100,000 - $120,000 per year, with the starting salary determined based on a combination of skills, education, experience, and internal equity. As part of our total rewards package, you’ll be eligible for our annual bonus program.

Imagine stepping into a role where your work turns complex data into clarity that leaders can use to make decisions.

Here, you are not just building reports, you are helping shape how the business understands itself. You work closely with stakeholders to take messy, complex datasets and turn them into meaningful insights that drive strategy, improve performance, and support better decisions across the organization.

This role is ideal for someone with experience as a Data Analyst, Senior BI Analyst, Senior Business Intelligence Analyst, BI Developer, or Analytics Developer, with strong hands-on skills in Python, SQL, and Power BI.

You are naturally analytical, resourceful, and comfortable working in ambiguity. You enjoy solving problems without clear instructions and can work independently while staying connected to the bigger business picture. You don’t just analyze data you understand what the business is trying to achieve and translate information into something actionable.

You are also energized by innovation and emerging technologies, including AI and modern analytics tools, and you are always looking for better, smarter ways to solve problems. Most importantly, you know that insights only matter when they are understood and used so you focus on clear, simple, and impactful storytelling.

If this sounds like the kind of work you want to do, we would love to hear from you. Apply today and bring your expertise in data, analytics, and storytelling to a role where your insights will directly influence decisions, shape strategy, and help the organization see the business more clearly.

We offer an attractive compensation and benefits package. Our workplace culture values health and wellness, diversity and equality, continuing education, environmental sustainability and giving back to the community.

This role is currently working hybrid from home and from our head office in Burnaby, BC. Apply this week if you would like to take on this role.

Key Ways This Position Makes An Impact

In this role, you sit at the center of decision making, where data, business understanding, and strategy all come together.

You start with large, complex datasets and bring structure to them using Python and SQL cleaning, transforming, and exploring the data to uncover trends, risks, and opportunities that are not immediately visible.

From there, your work shifts from technical analysis to meaning. You translate complex findings into clear, business friendly insights, turning analysis into dashboards, reports, and narratives that help leaders quickly understand what is happening and what actions are needed.

At the same time, you play a key role in strengthening how data is used across the organization. Working with databases, Business Intelligence (Power BI) tools, and data integration systems, you help ensure information is reliable, consistent, and easy to access so teams can trust and rely on it.

Over time, your contributions help mature the organization’s analytics capability, reducing reliance on ad hoc reporting and enabling more standardized, scalable, and trusted insights that support better decision-making.

You also help move the organization forward by supporting predictive thinking and engaging with AI-enabled and modern analytics tools, helping leaders shift from reactive decisions to more proactive, forward-looking planning.

Ultimately, your work makes a simple but powerful difference: leaders see the business more clearly, understand it more deeply, and make better decisions with confidence, backed by trusted data.

Key Experiences You Bring To This Role

  • Bachelor’s degree in data science, statistics, mathematics, or a related quantitative field.
  • 5+ years of related experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or data science.
  • Proficiency with SQL, Python, and/or R for data manipulation and statistical analysis.
  • Proficiency with modern business intelligence tools such as Power BI (preferred), Tableau, and/or Looker for data visualization.
  • Demonstrated ability to analyze large datasets and translate findings into meaningful recommendations.
  • Strong critical thinking, problem solving, and analytical skills.
  • Excellent time management and organizational abilities, with capacity to balance competing priorities in a fast-paced environment.
  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills with a proven ability to collaborate effectively across teams and build relationships with stakeholders.

If you’re excited about taking on this opportunity, we invite you to apply today and start the conversation with us about this position.

Please apply via our website this week at https://www.pac.bluecross.ca/careers

While we thank all applicants for their interest, only short-listed candidates will be contacted.

Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

PBC is an Indigenous Works employer of choice, an Insurance Business Canada Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion award winner, one of Canada’s Greenest employers, a Canadian Centre for Diversity and Inclusion (CCDI) employer partner, and a Pride at Work partner.

We strive to create a workplace where everyone feels valued and employees feel empowered to freely participate and contribute regardless of race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, religion, ability, education level, parental status, or socioeconomic status. We are an equal opportunity employer and welcome applications from all qualified candidates.

To request an accommodation in completing this job application, testing, interviewing, or otherwise participating in the employee selection process, please direct your inquiries to careers@pac.bluecross.ca. These accommodation requests will be reviewed confidentially and, on a case-by-case basis by Human Resources.

Title: Sr. Data and Reporting Analyst
Description:
Why You’ll Love Softchoice

We are a software-focused IT solutions and services provider that equips organizations to be agile and innovative, and for their people to be engaged, connected, and creative at work. That means moving them to the cloud, helping them build the workplace of tomorrow, and enabling them to make smarter decisions about their technology. By doing these things we help them create success for their customers and their people.

We stand proudly for our people and support their success through career development and advancement. We are recognized and respected for our culture of inclusion and belonging, continuously striving to do what’s good for our people and communities.

The Impact You Will Have

The Sr. Data and Reporting Analyst will be responsible for owning the design, delivery, and maintenance of People and Culture (P&C) reporting and business analysis. This role serves as the subject-matter expert on workforce data—driving reporting strategy, ensuring data integrity across P&C systems, and providing consultative guidance to senior leaders that shapes how the organization makes people decisions. As a senior team member, you will partner cross-functionally with business analysts and operational leaders across Softchoice to combine people data with business performance data, turning insight into action. You will lead end-to-end analytics initiatives with minimal direction, influence without authority, and bring executive presence to every stakeholder interaction.

What You’ll Do


Develop and own reporting dashboards using data mining analytics and reporting tools (SuccessFactors Story, ADP Vantage, PowerBI, Power Platform) to drive performance monitoring and executive decision-making.
Design, develop, and maintain data and statistical models, including complex data manipulation from multiple sources that generate actionable insights for leadership.
Serve as the P&C analytics subject-matter expert, proactively advising senior leaders on key metrics, trends, and data-driven recommendations that inform workforce strategy
Conduct a review of the intake and prioritization of reporting requests and ensure reporting data is presented in an understandable and user-friendly format.
Engage stakeholders at all levels in defining requirements, developing technical specifications, analyzing data flow, and completing testing for report execution.
Conduct audits on data extracted to ensure the highest quality is being used for reporting.
Perform quality assurance checks on system configurations including assessment of changes in SAP bi-annual releases to determine the impact.
Use HR system and process expertise to analyze and troubleshoot issues, identify root causes, and determine and execute resolution.
Support the implementation and maintenance of data and reporting strategy and governance within P&C and throughout the partnership with business areas.
Champion the empowerment of stakeholders at various levels in leveraging reporting tools to support strategic and operational needs.
Respond to benchmarking surveys, and prepare analysis on employee data across P&C Systems
Serve as the SME for P&C analytics, by owning the timely generation of recurring metrics.
Ensure reporting roles and access are defined and maintained in partnership with IT governance and SuccessFactors Administrators
Create and maintain Knowledge-Based Documentation (KBDs) related to new developments and enhancements in reports and dashboards for knowledge sharing and job shadowing.
Onboard new HR Technology team members on P&C reporting tools, including training and change management.
Works with other HR Technology team members to build awareness of data and process changes impacting business metrics.
Maintains current knowledge of reporting trends and best practices for long-term solutions.



What You’ll Bring To The Table


Bachelor’s degree in business analytics, management analytics, math, statistics, computer science or related field
A minimum of 5 years’ experience working with Analytics tools at an advanced level with 2 years in PowerBI and Power Platform tools.
Experience with data visualization tools with at least 1 full implementation of Planning, Forecasting BI, and Predictive Analytics
Enrolled or completing Data Analytics certification or programs is an asset (Microsoft Certified: Power BI Data Analyst or People Analytics programs)
Demonstrated experience in using queries and syntax to conduct data mining, transformation, cleansing, and visualization.
Intermediate to Advanced experience with Microsoft Office products and SQL for querying relational databases.
A broad understanding of HR business fundamentals with at least 4 years of HR operations experience
Experience in 1-2 full-cycle SAP SuccessFactors implementation projects is an asset.
Experience with owning governance, controls, processes, data modeling, master data management, data standardization
Should be able to model positive behavior in the engagement with others and display the desired values and culture of the organization.
Demonstrated ability to drive initiatives end-to-end with minimal direction, taking full ownership from problem identification through solution delivery.
Works collaboratively to provide services that are sought after and recognized for being of high quality.
Executive presence with the ability to distill complex technical information into clear, actionable narratives for senior leadership and non-technical audiences.
Must be able to handle multiple tasks and projects with deadlines and adaptable to changes in priority level that require a high level of organization and planning skills.
Must be able to work quickly and efficiently without sacrificing the quality of their work.
Detail-oriented with proven analytical and problem-solving skills
Strong consultative skillset—able to frame technology issues, plans, and solutions from both a business and technical perspective, providing both pros and cons for recommended solutions to peers, people leaders, and senior leadership.
Excellent documentation skills (Process and training manuals, workbook updates)



Compensation

A reasonable estimate of the current base pay range for this position is $76,500 to $102,000 annually + 10% Target Incentives.

Actual salary will be based on a variety of factors, including location, experience, skill set, education, and related certification. The range for this position in other geographic locations may differ.

Softchoice offers a comprehensive and competitive benefit plan to all full-time employees, which includes:


Health and Wellbeing: Medical and Dental Care, Employee & Family Assistance Program
Financial Benefits: RRSP/DPSP Retirement Savings Plan with Company Matching, Life and Disability Insurance
Paid Time Off: Vacation and Sick Leave, Holidays, Parental Leave, Volunteer Days, Bereavement Leave
Additional Perks: Employee Discount Program



Not sure if you qualify? Think about applying anyway:

We understand that not everyone brings 100% of the skills and experience for the role.

At Softchoice, we offer opportunities to a diverse group including those with a variety of workplace experiences and backgrounds. Whether you are new to corporate tech, returning to work after a gap in employment, or looking to transition and take the next step in your career, we are excited to learn more about you and encourage you to apply.

Why You’ll Love Working Here


The People: You’ll thrive in our collaborative environment, surrounded by incredible colleagues who foster support and innovation, driving our collective success
High-Performing Culture: At Softchoice, we are dedicated to achieving our goals and committed to success for our customers and each other
Flexibility: Plan your workdays in a way that suits you best
Award-Winning Workplace: Proudly recognized as a Great Place to Work for 20 consecutive years
Inclusive Culture: We are committed to an inclusive culture where every team member can be their authentic self
Competitive Benefits: Benefit from competitive perks that start on day one



Inclusion & Equal Opportunity Employment

We are an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity, inclusion & belonging. People seeking employment at Softchoice are considered without regard to any protected category including but not limited to, race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, disability, veteran status, gender identity, or sexual orientation.

Require accommodation? We are ready to help:

We are proud to provide interview & employment accommodation during the recruitment and hiring process. If you require any accommodation to apply or interview for a position, please reach out directly to asktalentacquisition@softchoice.com . We are committed to working with you to best meet your needs.

Our Commitment To Your Experience

We are committed to the safety of all applicants and team members. With that in mind, we have implemented digital interviewing for everyone. We understand that you may need to interview with distractions around you (such as children or furry friends) and we will be doing the same.

Before you start with us, we will conduct a criminal record check, verify your education, and check your references.

When you join Softchoice, we will onboard you remotely. Don't worry. It's quick, simple and you'll be connected with your new team in no time.

Title: Maintenance System Support Analyst
Description:
Marketing Statement

As the largest operating company in the integrated TransLink enterprise, Coast Mountain Bus Company (CMBC) operates a fleet of clean-fuel conventional and community shuttle buses, zero-emission trolley buses, and passenger ferry SeaBuses in Metro Vancouver, the largest single transit service area in Canada.

At CMBC, one of BC’s Top Employers, we're committed to providing an innovative, healthy, and engaging workforce. This is reflected in our workforce of over 5,500 employees performing over 400 unique jobs, who are committed and empowered to deliver service that attracts nearly 1.1 million passengers daily and connects people, businesses, and communities in the Metro Vancouver region.

We have the important job of helping our passengers get to work, appointments, visiting with family and friends, and back again every day. Metro Vancouver relies on us, and we take that as a point of pride.

Summary

Responsibilities

Represents the Maintenance Systems Support Department (MSS) facilitating technical support and expertise in Maintenance Systems (MS) applications and Data Analysis as it applies to Fleet Business and Engineering units. Work with clients to identify requirements, gather information, propose, and implement new procedures, applications, enhancements, configuration changes, procure hardware and software, and liaise with BTS to ensure standards and procedures comply within company policy. Develop business centric applications utilizing various programming languages as required by operating units. Provides guidance and support to Maintenance Systems Coordinators. Coordinates with external support teams to ensure organizational systems are functioning in accordance with business needs. Provides guidance, expertise, and recommendations to various stakeholders in CMBC and TransLink on various reporting systems, analytical tools, processes, and requirements. Conducts complex analysis, forecasting, reporting and documentation to improve effectiveness and to support continuous improvement of the various maintenance related systems for the division. Provides training to various stakeholders on MS, various reporting systems, analytical tools, processes, and requirements.

Duties And Responsibilities

Work with clients to identify requirements, gather information, propose, and implement new procedures, business centric applications, enhancements, configuration changes, procure hardware and software, and liaise with BTS to ensure standards and procedures comply within company policy by:

Identifying the requirements, assessing the impact of changes, maintaining, developing, and refining related Maintenance Systems including, but not limited to: Annual Workplan for Planning, Brake Reline Management, Maintenance Reporting, Fuel and KM systems, Power BI Dashboards, as well as new and developing Maintenance business processes.

Configuring, testing, improving, and troubleshooting new, or updated systems that have been implemented.

Liaising with BTS units in assessing recommendations and its impact on infrastructure.

Understanding the application technical infrastructure and the BTS Enterprise Architecture plans and future directions, for example: participating in the conceptual workshops and provide technical insights for MS in upgrading and advancement of Enterprise Resource Planning directly affecting the betterment of future versions.

Providing guidance and advice to maintenance system users to ensure information availability, quality, integrity, and reliability is maintained.

Reviewing and monitoring various maintenance information sources and systems to identify and resolve issues as well as verify their operation and data quality.

Driving to various CMBC locations to respond to issues and troubleshoot problems with systems.

Providing demos, training and knowledge transfer to end users, peers, and other Business units.

Develop business centric applications utilizing various programming languages as requested by internal and external customers:

Obtaining information, assessing requirements, proposing solutions, and coordinating hand-off for updates and support developing application files and reports using programs such as Cognos, Crystal Reports, Microsoft Excel, Access, Power Query, Power Pivot, Power BI

Utilizing programming languages such as, but not limited to: Structured Query Language (SQL), Visual Basic for Applications (VBA), Power Query M, and Data Analysis Expressions (DAX).

Liaising with BTS and Data solutions teams to collaborate and ensure applications adhere to development and support standards.

Maintaining the health of applications by providing continuous support, enhancements, and customizations throughout the application’s lifecycle.

Provides Guidance And Support To Maintenance Systems Coordinators By

Assigning day to day tasks, checking for accuracy and completion of work in a timely manner.

Verifying scheduled reports on vital business metrics avoiding service disruptions that can affect scheduled operational functions.

Monitoring and escalating change requests in the ticketing queue affecting the safety and security of revenue and non-revenue vehicles, for example: modifying inspection points and related parts avoiding critical failure costing unforeseen corrective maintenance.

Assisting the Assistant Manager in allocating tasks and providing guidance.

Responding to emergency callouts for immediate operation support as required, including off hours.

Coordinates with external support teams to ensure organizational systems are functioning in accordance with business needs by:

Liaising with external companies such as fuel vendors, software vendors, tank monitoring providers, and vendor for oil analysis to understand, assess and determine requirements relating to maintenance systems.

Keeping up to date on the application technical infrastructure and coordinating with TransLink support team ensuring requests submitted to them are in line with business processes and needs.

Identifying and assessing the impact of the changes requested by business users to ensure it meets organizational need/requirement and provides recommendation for improvement/adjustments.

Coordinate with external service provider in troubleshooting maintenance required systems to ensure functionality is up to organizational standards.

Ensure functionality of maintenance systems associated hardware, such as trouble shooting, diagnosing, repairs, and/or replacements.

Advancing knowledge and skills to become subject matter experts of the relevant supported applications.

Requirements

Provides guidance, expertise, and recommendations to various stakeholders in CMBC and TransLink on various reporting systems, analytical tools, processes, and requirements by:

Liaising with internal stakeholders such as Engineering, Training, Maintenance, Planning, Finance, Inventory, BTS, TransLink and CMBC Service Planning, HR, Performance Reporting, Infrastructure, and Bus Services.

Working with internal customers collaboratively to resolve issues and improve existing processes, including making recommendations for changes to reflect current conditions and ensure accurate results.

Providing recommendations to internal project teams as required, not limited to business processes and practices, detail design on systems, user requirements for efficiencies/functionalities.

Conducting and participating in maintenance cost/benefits analyses, derived from reporting or statistical solutions, typically from ad-hoc requests or abnormal behavior within data.

Providing guidance as needed in the use of best practices relating to Asset Management information, Work Management information and Workflow Management within MS and Maintenance Business Processes, to support change management.

Conducts Complex Analysis, Forecasting, Ad-hoc Reporting, And Documentation To Improve Effectiveness And To Support Continuous Improvements Of The Various Maintenance Related Systems For The Division By

Designs, develops, tests, implements, and maintains business solutions as required by the division.

Developing, maintaining, and refining the Fleet Maintenance Work Plan environment, modelling tools, and business processes.

Conducting in-depth analysis and studies involving advanced methods and techniques and statistical software, of current fleet MS and processes to identify problematic systems or procedures that need to be reworked, to improve efficiency, effectiveness and recover resources where possible.

Documenting all analysis, findings, and recommendations for improvement.

Developing, reviewing, and updating maintenance system processes, procedures, documentation, guidelines, and manuals, ensuring accuracy, current, and easily accessible to the maintenance team, to increase retention of processes within Fleet Maintenance.

Maintaining these documents to ensure information is current and up to date with ease of reference.

Liaising with other departments and external suppliers to determine requirements needed to create, test, and implement operational and statistical maintenance reports using various tools and maintenance systems.

Provides training to various stakeholders on MS, various reporting systems, analytical tools, processes, and requirements by:

Providing training on various maintenance systems for various user groups including but not limited to garage staff, other team members, and individuals that may need to use MS.

Developing training materials on emerging technologies and updates to current applications ensuring data quality and integrity are maintained.

Performs related duties as assigned, such being items of a minor nature that do not affect the value of the job.

Education

Qualifications

Completion of two-year diploma in computer science including courses in statistical analysis, operating system technologies. general programming report writing and the use of software development tools. Valid Class 5 BC Driver’s License required.

Experience

Requires three (3) years recent experience working in maintenance in a transit or similar environment, with demonstrated knowledge of system troubleshooting and maintenance information analysis using report-writing software, and training end users. A further period of up to one (1) year on the job is required to become familiar with the maintenance systems, policies, procedures, and user requirements; also, to become familiar with the work leader responsibilities including resolution of escalated problems and providing work leader direction to employees.

Other Information

Recruitment Process: An applicant will be required to demonstrate their suitability for this position by meeting the minimum level of qualifications and experience in order to be invited into the selection process. A standard interview format will be used including general, scenario and behavioural descriptive interview questions.

Work Schedule

37.5 hours per week.

Work Designation

Hybrid

This position offers the flexibility of working both on-site and remotely within B.C.

Rate of Pay

Salary $8,236 - $9,896 per month (Actual salary offered will be commensurate with education, experience and internal parity).

The Total Compensation Package includes Extended Health, Dental, Transit Pass and enrollment in the Public Service Pension Plan. Focus on your development through tuition reimbursement, training, and mentorship programs. Enjoy a variety of health and wellness programs, including access to gym facilities. Speak to us to know more about what we offer.

How To Apply

Please click the 'Apply' button at the top right corner or go to https://www.translink.ca/About-Us/Careers.aspx to apply for this position and view instructions on the process.

INSTRUCTIONS: Please save your (1) cover letter, and your (2) resume as one pdf document prior to uploading your application on-line.

Posting Date: April 22, 2025

Closing Date: Open until filled

Please note that only those short listed will be contacted.

Having trouble applying? Please view the System Requirements & FAQ's by going to http://www.translink.ca/careers.

Equal Employment Opportunity

Coast Mountain Bus Company is committed to employment equity and building a diverse workforce, representative of the customers we serve and the many communities in the Metro Vancouver region. We welcome and encourage Indigenous applicants, people of colour, all genders, 2SLGBTQ+ and persons with disabilities to apply. Learn more about TransLink's commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion.

Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. For a confidential inquiry, simply email us at jobs@translink.ca

Work with clients to identify requirements, gather information, propose, and implement new procedures, business centric applications, enhancements, configuration changes, procure hardware and software, and liaise with BTS to ensure standards and procedures comply within company policy by:

Identifying the requirements, assessing the impact of changes, maintaining, developing, and refining related Maintenance Systems including, but not limited to: Annual Workplan for Planning, Brake Reline Management, Maintenance Reporting, Fuel and KM systems, Power BI Dashboards, as well as new and developing Maintenance business processes.

Configuring, testing, improving, and troubleshooting new, or updated systems that have been implemented.

Liaising with BTS units in assessing recommendations and its impact on infrastructure.

Understanding the application technical infrastructure and the BTS Enterprise Architecture plans and future directions, for example: participating in the conceptual workshops and provide technical insights for MS in upgrading and advancement of Enterprise Resource Planning directly affecting the betterment of future versions.

Providing guidance and advice to maintenance system users to ensure information availability, quality, integrity, and reliability is maintained.

Reviewing and monitoring various maintenance information sources and systems to identify and resolve issues as well as verify their operation and data quality.

Driving to various CMBC locations to respond to issues and troubleshoot problems with systems.

Providing demos, training and knowledge transfer to end users, peers, and other Business units.

Develop business centric applications utilizing various programming languages as requested by internal and external customers:

Obtaining information, assessing requirements, proposing solutions, and coordinating hand-off for updates and support developing application files and reports using programs such as Cognos, Crystal Reports, Microsoft Excel, Access, Power Query, Power Pivot, Power BI

Utilizing programming languages such as, but not limited to: Structured Query Language (SQL), Visual Basic for Applications (VBA), Power Query M, and Data Analysis Expressions (DAX).

Liaising with BTS and Data solutions teams to collaborate and ensure applications adhere to development and support standards.

Maintaining the health of applications by providing continuous support, enhancements, and customizations throughout the application’s lifecycle.

Provides Guidance And Support To Maintenance Systems Coordinators By

Assigning day to day tasks, checking for accuracy and completion of work in a timely manner.

Verifying scheduled reports on vital business metrics avoiding service disruptions that can affect scheduled operational functions.

Monitoring and escalating change requests in the ticketing queue affecting the safety and security of revenue and non-revenue vehicles, for example: modifying inspection points and related parts avoiding critical failure costing unforeseen corrective maintenance.

Assisting the Assistant Manager in allocating tasks and providing guidance.

Responding to emergency callouts for immediate operation support as required, including off hours.

Coordinates with external support teams to ensure organizational systems are functioning in accordance with business needs by:

Liaising with external companies such as fuel vendors, software vendors, tank monitoring providers, and vendor for oil analysis to understand, assess and determine requirements relating to maintenance systems.

Keeping up to date on the application technical infrastructure and coordinating with TransLink support team ensuring requests submitted to them are in line with business processes and needs.

Identifying and assessing the impact of the changes requested by business users to ensure it meets organizational need/requirement and provides recommendation for improvement/adjustments.

Coordinate with external service provider in troubleshooting maintenance required systems to ensure functionality is up to organizational standards.

Ensure functionality of maintenance systems associated hardware, such as trouble shooting, diagnosing, repairs, and/or replacements.

Advancing knowledge and skills to become subject matter experts of the relevant supported applications.

Requirements

Provides guidance, expertise, and recommendations to various stakeholders in CMBC and TransLink on various reporting systems, analytical tools, processes, and requirements by:

Liaising with internal stakeholders such as Engineering, Training, Maintenance, Planning, Finance, Inventory, BTS, TransLink and CMBC Service Planning, HR, Performance Reporting, Infrastructure, and Bus Services.

Working with internal customers collaboratively to resolve issues and improve existing processes, including making recommendations for changes to reflect current conditions and ensure accurate results.

Providing recommendations to internal project teams as required, not limited to business processes and practices, detail design on systems, user requirements for efficiencies/functionalities.

Conducting and participating in maintenance cost/benefits analyses, derived from reporting or statistical solutions, typically from ad-hoc requests or abnormal behavior within data.

Providing guidance as needed in the use of best practices relating to Asset Management information, Work Management information and Workflow Management within MS and Maintenance Business Processes, to support change management.

Conducts Complex Analysis, Forecasting, Ad-hoc Reporting, And Documentation To Improve Effectiveness And To Support Continuous Improvements Of The Various Maintenance Related Systems For The Division By

Designs, develops, tests, implements, and maintains business solutions as required by the division.

Developing, maintaining, and refining the Fleet Maintenance Work Plan environment, modelling tools, and business processes.

Conducting in-depth analysis and studies involving advanced methods and techniques and statistical software, of current fleet MS and processes to identify problematic systems or procedures that need to be reworked, to improve efficiency, effectiveness and recover resources where possible.

Documenting all analysis, findings, and recommendations for improvement.

Developing, reviewing, and updating maintenance system processes, procedures, documentation, guidelines, and manuals, ensuring accuracy, current, and easily accessible to the maintenance team, to increase retention of processes within Fleet Maintenance.

Maintaining these documents to ensure information is current and up to date with ease of reference.

Liaising with other departments and external suppliers to determine requirements needed to create, test, and implement operational and statistical maintenance reports using various tools and maintenance systems.

Provides training to various stakeholders on MS, various reporting systems, analytical tools, processes, and requirements by:

Providing training on various maintenance systems for various user groups including but not limited to garage staff, other team members, and individuals that may need to use MS.

Developing training materials on emerging technologies and updates to current applications ensuring data quality and integrity are maintained.

Performs related duties as assigned, such being items of a minor nature that do not affect the value of the job.

Education

 Qualifications

Completion of two-year diploma in computer science including courses in statistical analysis, operating system technologies. general programming report writing and the use of software development tools. Valid Class 5 BC Driver’s License required.

Experience

Requires three (3) years recent experience working in maintenance in a transit or similar environment, with demonstrated knowledge of system troubleshooting and maintenance information analysis using report-writing software, and training end users. A further period of up to one (1) year on the job is required to become familiar with the maintenance systems, policies, procedures, and user requirements; also, to become familiar with the work leader responsibilities including resolution of escalated problems and providing work leader direction to employees.

Other Information

Recruitment Process: An applicant will be required to demonstrate their suitability for this position by meeting the minimum level of qualifications and experience in order to be invited into the selection process. A standard interview format will be used including general, scenario and behavioural descriptive interview questions.

Work Schedule

37.5 hours per week.

Work Designation

Hybrid

This position offers the flexibility of working both on-site and remotely within B.C.

Rate of Pay

Salary $8,236 - $9,896 per month (Actual salary offered will be commensurate with education, experience and internal parity).

The Total Compensation Package includes Extended Health, Dental, Transit Pass and enrollment in the Public Service Pension Plan. Focus on your development through tuition reimbursement, training, and mentorship programs. Enjoy a variety of health and wellness programs, including access to gym facilities. Speak to us to know more about what we offer.

How To Apply

Please click the 'Apply' button at the top right corner or go to https://www.translink.ca/About-Us/Careers.aspx to apply for this position and view instructions on the process.

INSTRUCTIONS: Please save your (1) cover letter, and your (2) resume as one pdf document prior to uploading your application on-line.

Posting Date: April 22, 2025

Closing Date: Open until filled

Please note that only those short listed will be contacted.

Having trouble applying? Please view the System Requirements & FAQ's by going to http://www.translink.ca/careers.

Equal Employment Opportunity

Coast Mountain Bus Company is committed to employment equity and building a diverse workforce, representative of the customers we serve and the many communities in the Metro Vancouver region. We welcome and encourage Indigenous applicants, people of colour, all genders, 2SLGBTQ+ and persons with disabilities to apply. Learn more about TransLink's commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion.

Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. For a confidential inquiry, simply email us at jobs@translink.ca

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Reporting to the Manager, Business Intelligence & Analytics, the Senior Data Analyst will have a deep understanding of Data, AI, and Analytics solutions. You will be responsible for designing, developing, and delivering effective storytelling solutions using Power BI and Bird’s modern analytics platform.

We are seeking an individual who is proactive, curious, and driven—someone who understands not just how to build dashboards, but how to enable a culture of data analytics.

This role is ideal for an individual with a strong sense of the big picture: how data governance, storytelling, tools, and people come together to enable better decisions. You will play a key role in advancing Bird’s analytics maturity by delivering trusted insights, promoting effective data storytelling, and contributing to the Data & AI Center of Excellence (CoE) through standards, enablement, and data fluency.

The ideal candidate is comfortable navigating complexity, managing multiple priorities, and working across both Agile and Waterfall delivery models—partnering effectively with business, engineering, and leadership teams.

This position follows a hybrid work model with regular in office presence.

What You Will Be Working On


Design, develop, and deliver high‑quality analytics solutions using Power BI, ensuring accuracy, reliability, and consistency of data and metrics.
Apply effective data storytelling principles to transform complex information into clear, compelling, and decision‑ready insights.
Partner with stakeholders to understand business context, ask the right questions, and translate analytical needs into meaningful dashboards and reports.
Contribute to the evolution of the Data & AI Center of Excellence (CoE), helping shape standards, best practices, templates, and shared ways of working.
Promote data governance concepts by reinforcing trusted metrics, clear definitions, consistent logic, and proper documentation.
Support and advance data fluency and literacy across the organization by helping stakeholders better interpret, question, and use analytics effectively.
Perform analytical investigations and root‑cause analysis to explain trends, variances, and anomalies in business performance.
Collaborate with Data Engineering and IT teams to ensure analytics solutions align with data Lakehouse architecture and enterprise platforms.
Demonstrate a strong understanding of Data Lakehouse concepts, with exposure to tools such as Databricks, Microsoft Fabric, or Microsoft Synapse Analytics, sufficient to collaborate effectively with engineering teams.
Navigate and contribute within both Agile and Waterfall project environments, adapting communication and delivery style as needed.
Manage multiple priorities simultaneously while maintaining quality, structure, and stakeholder confidence.
Maintain strong, proactive communication with stakeholders and contribute to successful project outcomes.
Stay current with analytics trends and Microsoft’s analytics ecosystem (Power BI, Lakehouse platforms, and related technologies), applying learnings pragmatically.



What We Are Looking For


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Business Analytics, or a related field.
5+ years of experience in a Senior Data Analyst or Business Intelligence role.
Strong hands‑on experience with Power BI, with an emphasis on insight, clarity, and usability rather than just visual complexity.
A proactive, self‑driven mindset — comfortable taking ownership, identifying gaps, and helping move analytics forward.
Demonstrated understanding of what building a data‑driven culture actually means, beyond tools alone.
Experience contributing to or working within a Center of Excellence–style model for Data, AI, Analytics, or BI.
Solid grasp of data governance fundamentals, including trusted metrics, standard definitions, and consistency of logic.
Strong communication and organizational skills, with the ability to work effectively across both technical and non‑technical audiences.
Experience working in both Agile and Waterfall delivery environments.
Ability to manage competing priorities with structure, judgment, and professionalism.
General understanding of the Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps / Power Automate) without requiring deep specialization.



Preferred Qualifications


Experience delivering insights from structured, semi‑structured, and unstructured data sources.
Working knowledge of SQL and structured data querying.
Familiarity with modern data lakehouse architectures and exposure to platforms such as Databricks, Microsoft Fabric, or Synapse Analytics.
Power BI or relevant Microsoft analytics certification.
Experience enabling or mentoring others through data education, documentation, or informal



For Those Who Seek To Redefine

The greatest achievements in history are borne from the greatness within people – where human potential meets vision, and passion fuels evolution. Unlocking this potential is the most important thing we do at Bird.

As a leader in Canadian construction for over 100 years, the impact of our team is etched deeply within the core of our legacy. Beyond Bird, this impact is felt in the fundamental aspects of our everyday lives. From the critical infrastructure we depend on, to the energy and resources that keep us moving - we are powering our communities and shaping Canada’s skylines coast-to-coast-to-coast. Entrenched in the foundation of a culture built more than a century ago is an enduring quest to reimagine what is possible. Our impact is greater than ever, and we are looking for those who seek to redefine their story.

What We Believe In

We Put Safety First

A healthy and safe work environment is non-negotiable. We build a culture of operational and psychological safety through engagement, learning and leadership.

We Lead With Honesty

We speak and act with integrity, clarity and care so people can trust our word and our work. Being honest means we can deliver the best outcomes and consistent results.

We Are Stronger Together

Success is a team effort. Our inclusive workplace enables our combined expertise, humility and creativity to unlock our greater potential.

We Are Driven To Do Great Work

We built our name on quality. We have a passion for excellence in our work and relationships that honours our businesses and our industry.

We Create Opportunity

Rooted in a solid foundation, we adapt and grow to face the future. We are committed to elevating each other to chart the best path forward in an evolving world.

At Bird, we value Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DE&I) and believe it is essential to our success. We will continue to listen, learn, and take action in our commitment to building progress in our organization, and the industry as a whole. Cultivating an environment where all employees feel included, seen, and have a sense of belonging is core to Bird’s culture. We commit to proactively employing a workforce that reflects the communities in which we work, fostering an environment of continuous learning, and never compromising on our values.

Title: Application Analyst
Description:
Position: Application Analyst

Location: Vancouver, Canada

Department: Information Technology

Reports To: Manager, Applications

Position Summary

The Application Analyst is part of the global IT team responsible for the delivery and operations of Seapeak’s business systems. The Application Analyst will apply critical thinking in supporting and delivering services to a constantly evolving business and technical landscape. Strong relationships will be established with IT and process stakeholders to understand support requirements and how the supported systems are used.

Major Responsibilities


Establish strong relationships with process stakeholders to understand and prioritize work items; create specifications, process/data maps, and use cases, with a specific lens on identifying opportunities for process automation and low-code solutions
Identify, track, and resolve application problems; leverage automation tools to streamline day-to-day troubleshooting and performance monitoring
Manage application environments for testing; collaborate with other Analysts to design and operate data interfaces and APIs, focusing on low-code integration strategies
Champion data integrity and reliability through education and monitoring; mentor junior IT members on both system support and emerging automation practices
Support the new vessel onboarding process and provide routine/non-routine administration for application use and security access
Create or update standardized application support documentation, incorporating automated workflow guides where applicable
Coordinate with the global Vessel Systems Support group to test, implement, and support solutions on the vessels
Work with the Analytics team to improve data quality; utilize low-code tools or agentic automation concepts to optimize data warehouse integrations and identify table relationships.
Develop reports and dashboards to assist in identifying KPIs and trends, utilizing automated reporting features where possible


Requirements (Knowledge, Skills & Abilities)


3 - 5 years working with process/functional owners and providing 2nd level operational support for large business applications
Experience with RFPs and a full application lifecycle
Demonstrated ability to learn and adapt; experience or high interest in low-code environments (preferably OutSystems) and automation platforms (preferably UiPath) to improve business systems
Strong knowledge of continuous quality improvement, management information systems techniques, and change control procedures
Self-starter and results-driven; able to prioritize and complete tasks in a timely manner with minimum guidance
Proactive, dependable, and positive attitude with experience coordinating deployments across multiple stakeholder groups
Solid SQL querying experience, and experience with report/dashboard writing software such as PowerBI
Outgoing personality with excellent communication skills; comfortable delivering presentations and leading meetings
Flexibility to provide after-hours support; experience supporting remote locations in multiple time zones is an asset
Available to travel to regional offices and to vessels to service software, if required


Compensation

The typical hiring range for this position is $75,000 – $90,000 CAD per year. The final agreed upon salary may vary based on factors such as job-related knowledge, skills and experience.

Benefits Package


Target Annual Bonus Plan
17 vacation days, growing to 30 days with service, plus statutory holidays
100% company paid benefits for employee and direct dependents includes extended health, dental, vision, group life insurance, AD&D, STD and LTD
Health Care Spending Account of CAD750 / year
Global Medical Assistance
Employee Assistance Program
Flexible Spending Allowance CAD 1,500/year
Hybrid working. Although you are welcome to work in our Downtown Vancouver office full time, you are able to work from home up to 2 days per week after completing the probationary period.


About Us

With combined assets of over USD 10 billon, Seapeak is one of the world’s largest independent owner and operators of Liquefied Natural Gas carriers, with ownership interests in over 50 LNG vessels. In addition, Seapeak has ownership interests in 40 Natural Gas Liquids vessels. Our sponsor, leading infrastructure investor Stonepeak, has helped to position our company with a focus on growth in our global gas transportation markets. Since early-2022, we have invested over USD 2.2 billion, adding 22 ships to our fleet.

Our business is important – we are powering the day-to-day lives of so many around the world – and we are looking to build our Team to support our growth through acquisition and newbuild orders.

At Seapeak, we are committed to building successful careers and future leaders. Join us! For the chance to be empowered, to make decisions and be part of a supportive, high-achieving Team.

We set the standard for industry. We are solution driven, accountable for results and measured by success.

Title: Reporting Analyst
Description:
Workday HR Reporting & Systems Analyst




– HYBRID 1/day a week










We are partnering with a well-established Canadian organization seeking a Senior Workday HR Reporting & Systems Analyst to join their People & Culture team.

This role is ideal for someone who has deep expertise in Workday reporting, workforce analytics, and HR data management, and who has worked within a small to mid-sized HRIS team environment where they owned reporting solutions end-to-end.

What We're Looking For

The client is specifically seeking a senior-level Workday Reporting Analyst, not an HRIS administrator or implementation consultant.

Successful candidates will have:

Extensive experience building and maintaining complex Workday reports and analytics solutions.
Experience working within smaller HRIS teams where responsibilities are broad and less siloed.
Strong ownership of reporting, workforce analytics, stakeholder engagement, and business-facing reporting solutions.
The ability to work directly with business leaders and HR stakeholders to translate reporting requirements into actionable insights.

Please Note: Candidates from large enterprise HRIS teams where reporting responsibilities are highly specialized or segmented may not be the best fit for this opportunity.

Key Responsibilities

Design, develop, and maintain complex Workday reports, dashboards, scorecards, and workforce analytics.
Deliver ad hoc reporting and self-service reporting solutions for business stakeholders.
Partner with HR, Finance, Technology, and leadership teams to provide meaningful workforce insights.
Perform data validation, auditing, reconciliation, and reporting quality assurance.
Support HRIS enhancements, system optimization, and continuous improvement initiatives.
Maintain reporting documentation, data definitions, and governance processes.
Participate in HR technology upgrades, enhancements, and transformation initiatives.

Qualifications

3+ years of Workday reporting, HRIS reporting, or workforce analytics experience.
Strong hands-on experience with Workday Reporting, Calculated Fields, Composite Reports, Matrix Reports, and Workforce Analytics.
Experience supporting HR data management, reporting processes, and HR systems.
Advanced Excel skills including Pivot Tables, XLOOKUP/VLOOKUP, INDEX/MATCH, and data analysis.
Strong analytical, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities.

Nice to Have

Experience with Power BI, Tableau, or other business intelligence tools.
Experience supporting Workday HCM, Recruiting, Talent, Compensation, or Learning modules.
Exposure to HR technology implementations, upgrades, or transformation projects.
HRIS, Workday Reporting, or Analytics certifications.

  • Advanced Excel skills including Pivot Tables, XLOOKUP/VLOOKUP, INDEX/MATCH, and data analysis.
  • Strong analytical, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
  • Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities.

Nice to Have

  • Experience with Power BI, Tableau, or other business intelligence tools.
  • Experience supporting Workday HCM, Recruiting, Talent, Compensation, or Learning modules.
  • Exposure to HR technology implementations, upgrades, or transformation projects.
  • HRIS, Workday Reporting, or Analytics certifications.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
We are seeking a Senior Data Analyst to drive data-driven insights and support product analytics for large-scale mobile applications and cloud-based services. This role will work cross-functionally to analyze user behavior, improve product performance, and help shape strategic decisions through advanced analytics and visualization.




Key Responsibilities

Design and develop data dashboards, reports, and data pipelines to support business insights and product analytics
Analyze large datasets to understand user behavior, product performance, and trends
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities to enhance customer experience and product innovation
Translate complex data into actionable insights for stakeholders
Work on complex analytical problems requiring in-depth evaluation of multiple variables
Apply appropriate analytical techniques and methodologies to deliver high-quality results
Leverage ML/AI tools to generate insights and improve reporting capabilities




Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Data Science, or related)
5+ years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or related field (preferably within a product-focused environment)
Advanced proficiency in SQL and working knowledge of Python
Strong experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau or similar)
Solid understanding of data warehousing concepts and data modeling
Experience building and managing ETL processes and data pipelines (e.g., Airflow, Apache Spark, or similar)
Hands-on experience with cloud platforms such as AWS or GCP
Strong communication skills with the ability to work effectively across teams




Nice to Have

Experience working with mobile or health-related data
Certifications in AWS, GCP, or data analytics
Exposure to or experience with machine learning or AI technologies

Title: Senior Business Analytics & Insights
Description:
Marketing Statement

As the largest operating company in the integrated TransLink enterprise, Coast Mountain Bus Company (CMBC) operates a fleet of clean-fuel conventional and community shuttle buses, zero-emission trolley buses, and passenger ferry SeaBuses in Metro Vancouver, the largest single transit service area in Canada.

At CMBC, one of BC’s Top Employers, we're committed to providing an innovative, healthy, and engaging workforce. This is reflected in our workforce of over 5,500 employees performing over 400 unique jobs, who are committed and empowered to deliver service that attracts nearly 1.1 million passengers daily and connects people, businesses, and communities in the Metro Vancouver region.

We have the important job of helping our passengers get to work, appointments, visiting with family and friends, and back again every day. Metro Vancouver relies on us, and we take that as a point of pride.

Responsibilities

PRIMARY PURPOSE

Provides technical expertise to the development of a data governance framework for CMBC, including leading stakeholder engagement, developing, and monitoring progress of long-term roadmap, establishing requisite business processes, and ensuring the Data Warehouse, Business Intelligence and Analytic (DW/BI&A) solutions align with the established framework. Provides technical leadership and supervision on the design, development, and quality assurance of monthly dashboard reports to the Executive Management Team (EMT), to ensure timely and accurate delivery. Supports the Manager, Business Analytics & Insights with leading the annual Corporate Business Planning and Reporting process through stakeholder engagement with leaders from across the organization, ensuring alignment to Corporate and Enterprise-level strategic plans.

Key Accountabilities

Provides technical leadership on planning and implementation of DW/BI&A solutions. Designs, implements, and maintains customized data warehouse, business intelligence and analytics (DW/BI&A) technical solutions. Ensures DW/BI&A solutions function effectively and align with standards, policies and supports ongoing Data Governance Program within CMBC. Ensures solutions adhere to best practices and Enterprise standards.

Designs, implements, and maintains the Corporate Business Plan BI reporting solution, data collection process and KPI integration to ensure the alignment of planned annual works to strategic long-range workplans, Enterprise goals, as well as ties together corporate-level KPIs to the annual initiatives to ensure alignment to planned works and performance measures assessed.

Provides technical expertise in the development and sustainment of a data governance framework for CMBC, including standardized documentation. Leads stakeholder engagement and ensures the DW/BI&A solutions developed align with the governance framework established. Leads discussions with those identified as part of the governance framework within the organization and looks for alignment opportunities from across the Enterprise.

Stays abreast of new techniques, technologies, and industry trends, and participates in relevant industry events related to Data Governance. Leads the team towards modern practices and techniques; establishes and participates in design reviews, regular or ad-hoc data quality reviews ensuring data quality throughout the data pipeline.

Manages and develops projects of moderate to high impact, risk, and complexity. Designs and develops project scope, schedules, and work plans; defining and prioritizing deliverables; ensuring compliance with Enterprise standards, policies and procedures. Alerts management to critical issues and risks. Identifies and resolves project issues, including scheduling overruns, conflicting agendas, and technical issues.

Provides technical support to the maintenance of custom Extract Transform Load (ETL) solution, as well as continual assessment of metadata reporting to ensure data integrity and quality assurance.

Consults and collaborates with business and technology stakeholders on data management and data architecture strategies, governance, practices, and policies.

Supports the Executive Management Team by providing KPIs to report on monthly performance; leads requirements gathering, target setting, report design and delivery and documentation. Carries out analysis related to variances in corporate performance measures, recommending best practices to the Senior Management team for problem areas.

Prepares quarterly reports for the CMBC Board of Directors and TransLink Statutory Reporting requirements.

Provides support to the Manager in the development and maintenance of the Team’s Strategic Plan Roadmap, planning and decisions making. Provides input on the dependency and sequencing of projects as well as highlights any potential project or compliance risks to ensure project priorities align to annual objectives.

Participates in the development and implementation of a continuous improvement strategy by offering Business Process advisory services to other departments. Supports the delivery of better value and greater efficiency through the identification and elimination of unnecessary complexity within business processes and identification of better ways of working, applying Lean methodology.

Acts as a key liaison with International Benchmarking Groups to conduct detailed surveys and analysis. Determines international best practices related to specific Key Performance Indicators and completes analysis of moderate to high impact, recommending business changes with financial/budgetary impacts. Completes follow-up analysis to confirm impacts and ROI. Presents findings to Executive Team, and at conferences as requested by Executive leadership.

Develops business cases to support KPIs with strategic objectives and leverages available benchmarking resources for best practices; provides comparable performance measures from other transit systems and business enterprises to stakeholders towards informed decision making. Forecasts the implications of various alternatives to maximize the effectiveness of KPIs for organizational success.

Represents the Business Analytics & Insights Team on inter-departmental committees and as a technical input for larger scale capital projects as requested.

Provides technical support and guidance to other Analysts and supports Managers within the organization by connecting them to relevant data sources for their reporting needs, including developing custom SQL views of required data.

Qualifications

EDUCATION & EXPERIENCE:

The requirements for this job are acquired through completion of a university degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Analytics, or other relevant business-related fields plus seven (7) years of progressive experience in performance reporting functions in a multifaceted organization.

Other Requirements

Advanced knowledge of concepts, principles, practices and techniques pertaining to all aspects of performance measures, benchmarking, balanced scorecards and dashboards

Excellent knowledge of Microsoft Data Warehouse and BI technologies, both Cloud and on-prem (SQL Server, SSIS, SSAS, SSRS, Power BI Desktop, PowerBI Report Builder, PowerBI Service, Tableau etc.)

Strong knowledge of Microsoft PowerPlatform solutions including PowerApps, PowerAutomate and Power Platform Admin Centre.

Strong knowledge and understanding of the relevant concepts of data mining and data warehousing

Demonstrated ability to communicate the complexities of technical programs (e.g. Kimball methodology, modern data warehousing).

Advanced experience of data modeling and business process design software, such as Visio.

Advanced knowledge of coaching and mentoring teams on Data Warehouse & Business Intelligence Architect concepts and methodology.

Advanced analytical and problem-solving skills to handle conflict resolution, facilitating discussion and alternatives of different approaches.

Solid leadership skills with ability to manage and influence direct or indirect reporting staff, and to provide specialized guidance and expertise to all levels of the organization.

Excellent planning, organization, and time management skills with strong ability to organize competing priorities.

Experience maintaining ETL process using software such as SSIS

Prior experience in design, deployment, or sustainment of Data Governance Program

Advanced knowledge of concepts, principles, practices, and techniques pertaining to all aspects of performance measures, benchmarking, balanced scorecards, and dashboards

Advanced interpersonal and communication skills to provide specialized guidance and expertise to managers, staff and customers; and to develop and manage critical relationships at all levels in the enterprise.

Experience in leading and facilitating Lean/process improvements event is an asset.

Other Information

Recruitment Process: An applicant will be required to demonstrate their suitability for this position by meeting the minimum level of qualifications and experience in order to be invited into the selection process. A standard interview format will be used including general, scenario and behavioural descriptive interview questions.

Work Schedule

37.5 hours per week.

Work Designation

Hybrid

This position offers the flexibility of working both on-site and remotely within B.C.

Rate of Pay

The salary for this position ranges from $84,480 - $126,720 per annum. Actual salary offered will be based on education, experience, skills, and qualifications as they relate to the role.

We aim to provide competitive pay that reflects your professional background and expertise. We want to ensure our offer will align with your strengths considering experience, skills, and internal equity to offer a fair and equitable salary. The Total Compensation Package, which includes Extended Health, Dental, Transit Pass and enrollment in the Public Service Pension Plan as well as other employment offerings will be discussed in detail as your application progresses.

How To Apply

Please click the 'Apply' button at the top right corner or go to https://www.translink.ca/About-Us/Careers.aspx to apply for this position and view instructions on the process.

INSTRUCTIONS: Please save your (1) cover letter, and your (2) resume as one pdf document prior to uploading your application on-line.

Posting Date: May 27, 2026

Closing Date: Open until filled

Please note that only those short listed will be contacted.

Having trouble applying? Please view the System Requirements & FAQ's by going to http://www.translink.ca/careers.

Coast Mountain Bus Company is committed to employment equity and building a diverse workforce, representative of the customers we serve and the many communities in the Metro Vancouver region. We welcome and encourage Indigenous applicants, people of colour, all genders, 2SLGBTQ+ and persons with disabilities to apply. Learn more about TransLink's commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion.

Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. For a confidential inquiry, simply email us at jobs@translink.ca
