Title: Application Analyst
Description:
Position: Application Analyst

Location: Vancouver, Canada

Department: Information Technology

Reports To: Manager, Applications

Position Summary

The Application Analyst is part of the global IT team responsible for the delivery and operations of Seapeak’s business systems. The Application Analyst will apply critical thinking in supporting and delivering services to a constantly evolving business and technical landscape. Strong relationships will be established with IT and process stakeholders to understand support requirements and how the supported systems are used.

Major Responsibilities


Establish strong relationships with process stakeholders to understand and prioritize work items; create specifications, process/data maps, and use cases, with a specific lens on identifying opportunities for process automation and low-code solutions
Identify, track, and resolve application problems; leverage automation tools to streamline day-to-day troubleshooting and performance monitoring
Manage application environments for testing; collaborate with other Analysts to design and operate data interfaces and APIs, focusing on low-code integration strategies
Champion data integrity and reliability through education and monitoring; mentor junior IT members on both system support and emerging automation practices
Support the new vessel onboarding process and provide routine/non-routine administration for application use and security access
Create or update standardized application support documentation, incorporating automated workflow guides where applicable
Coordinate with the global Vessel Systems Support group to test, implement, and support solutions on the vessels
Work with the Analytics team to improve data quality; utilize low-code tools or agentic automation concepts to optimize data warehouse integrations and identify table relationships.
Develop reports and dashboards to assist in identifying KPIs and trends, utilizing automated reporting features where possible


Requirements (Knowledge, Skills & Abilities)


3 - 5 years working with process/functional owners and providing 2nd level operational support for large business applications
Experience with RFPs and a full application lifecycle
Demonstrated ability to learn and adapt; experience or high interest in low-code environments (preferably OutSystems) and automation platforms (preferably UiPath) to improve business systems
Strong knowledge of continuous quality improvement, management information systems techniques, and change control procedures
Self-starter and results-driven; able to prioritize and complete tasks in a timely manner with minimum guidance
Proactive, dependable, and positive attitude with experience coordinating deployments across multiple stakeholder groups
Solid SQL querying experience, and experience with report/dashboard writing software such as PowerBI
Outgoing personality with excellent communication skills; comfortable delivering presentations and leading meetings
Flexibility to provide after-hours support; experience supporting remote locations in multiple time zones is an asset
Available to travel to regional offices and to vessels to service software, if required


Compensation

The typical hiring range for this position is $75,000 – $90,000 CAD per year. The final agreed upon salary may vary based on factors such as job-related knowledge, skills and experience.

Benefits Package


Target Annual Bonus Plan
17 vacation days, growing to 30 days with service, plus statutory holidays
100% company paid benefits for employee and direct dependents includes extended health, dental, vision, group life insurance, AD&D, STD and LTD
Health Care Spending Account of CAD750 / year
Global Medical Assistance
Employee Assistance Program
Flexible Spending Allowance CAD 1,500/year
Hybrid working. Although you are welcome to work in our Downtown Vancouver office full time, you are able to work from home up to 2 days per week after completing the probationary period.


About Us

With combined assets of over USD 10 billon, Seapeak is one of the world’s largest independent owner and operators of Liquefied Natural Gas carriers, with ownership interests in over 50 LNG vessels. In addition, Seapeak has ownership interests in 40 Natural Gas Liquids vessels. Our sponsor, leading infrastructure investor Stonepeak, has helped to position our company with a focus on growth in our global gas transportation markets. Since early-2022, we have invested over USD 2.2 billion, adding 22 ships to our fleet.

Our business is important – we are powering the day-to-day lives of so many around the world – and we are looking to build our Team to support our growth through acquisition and newbuild orders.

At Seapeak, we are committed to building successful careers and future leaders. Join us! For the chance to be empowered, to make decisions and be part of a supportive, high-achieving Team.

We set the standard for industry. We are solution driven, accountable for results and measured by success.

Title: Commercial Pricing & Profitability Analyst
Description:
Commercial Pricing & Profitability Analyst

Origin Sustainables — Vancouver, BC

Role Type: Full-time, in-office

Reports to: CEO




About Origin Sustainables

Origin Sustainables is a Canadian supplier and importer of sustainable foodservice packaging serving multi-unit restaurant groups, QSR brands, and enterprise customers across Canada.




We are more than a packaging distributor. Our customers rely on us for tailored packaging solutions, direct sourcing, quality control, inventory planning, logistics coordination, implementation support, and reliable supply chain execution across complex packaging programs.




We work directly with end users while often fulfilling through broadline distributors and other channel partners. That makes pricing and profitability more complex than simple product markup. Good commercial decisions require clear assumptions around landed cost, freight, warehousing, inventory exposure, customer commitments, distributor requirements, and cost-to-serve.




About the Role

We are looking for a commercially minded, analytical, and detail-oriented Commercial Pricing & Profitability Analyst to support pricing, margin analysis, opportunity economics, account profitability, inventory economics, working-capital exposure, and commercial risk analysis.




This role sits at the intersection of Sales/Account Management, Sourcing, Operations, Supply Chain, Finance, and leadership. You will gather inputs from across the business, validate assumptions, maintain pricing and margin tools, run scenarios, and prepare clear analysis before customer opportunities move into proposal, approval, or execution.




This is not a sales role and not a traditional accounting or FP&A role. It is a hands-on commercial decision-support role for someone who enjoys pricing, profitability, cost analysis, scenario modeling, and practical business problem-solving.




What You’ll Do

Support pricing, margin, cost, inventory, and profitability analysis for quotes, proposals, RFQs/RFPs, renewals, pricing schedules, and commercial decisions.
Use pricing models to calculate landed cost, delivered cost, gross margin, contribution margin, CMROI, and opportunity economics.
Gather, develop, and validate cost assumptions related to product cost, freight, warehousing, inventory, fulfillment, service-level requirements, and cost-to-serve.
Conduct scenario modeling and commercial trade-off analysis to inform pricing decisions, account planning, customer opportunities, renewals, and negotiation preparation.
Identify commercial risks, margin issues, pricing inconsistencies, cost changes, and questionable assumptions before customer commitments are made.
Maintain pricing models, customer pricing records, pricing templates, margin reports, and commercial support tools.
Track cost fluctuations, freight changes, pricing deviations, margin erosion, and variances between pricing assumptions and actual results.
Analyze margin and profitability by customer, SKU, opportunity, account, and portfolio, and prepare clear recommendations for leadership review.
Support reporting and analysis on aged inventory, slow-moving SKUs, customer-specific inventory exposure, inventory turns, and working-capital impact.
Help evaluate whether inventory commitments are justified by customer volume, margin, reliability requirements, strategic value, and commercial risk.




This role supports commercial analysis and recommendations; final decisions on pricing, inventory, contracts, and customer-facing commitments remain with leadership.




What We’re Looking For

3–5 years of experience in pricing, commercial analysis, sales operations, business analysis, finance, supply chain analysis, proposal support, or a related role.
Strong Excel/spreadsheet skills, including modeling, formulas, lookups, pivot tables, pricing tables, and data analysis.
Strong analytical and problem-solving ability.
Strong commercial judgment, including the ability to identify pricing, margin, cost, inventory, and risk issues that require further review.
High attention to detail and accuracy.
Strong teamwork and collaboration skills, with the ability to gather inputs, validate assumptions, and turn cross-functional information into clear, useful analysis.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Comfortable working with imperfect data, evolving processes, and hands-on analysis.
Comfortable working full-time in office in Vancouver.




Strong Assets

Experience in packaging, foodservice, CPG, distribution, wholesale, import/export, manufacturing, logistics, or supply chain.
Bachelor’s degree or diploma in business, finance, economics, accounting, supply chain, operations, or a related field.
Experience with NetSuite, Salesforce, HubSpot, Power BI, SAP, Cognos, or similar ERP/CRM/reporting systems.
Experience supporting pricing, quotes, RFQs/RFPs, customer profitability, margin analysis, or contract renewals.
Familiarity with landed costs, freight, warehousing, inventory, working capital, contribution margin, CMROI, or cost-to-serve analysis.
Experience analyzing customer-specific inventory, distributor fulfillment economics, or complex B2B supply programs.




Why Join Origin?

This is an opportunity to join a growing Canadian company at an important stage of its evolution. You will work directly with the CEO and cross-functionally with Sales/Account Management, Sourcing, Operations, Supply Chain, and Finance.




You will gain exposure to how enterprise customer programs are priced, planned, evaluated, launched, and managed — and help build the commercial decision-support discipline that enhances competitiveness and protects profitability as Origin grows.




How to Apply

Please apply with your resume and a brief note explaining why this role interests you and how your experience would help Origin improve pricing, profitability, commercial planning, and decision-making.

Origin Sustainables — Vancouver, BC

Reports to: CEO


About Origin Sustainables

Origin Sustainables is a Canadian supplier and importer of sustainable foodservice packaging serving multi-unit restaurant groups, QSR brands, and enterprise customers across Canada.


We are more than a packaging distributor. Our customers rely on us for tailored packaging solutions, direct sourcing, quality control, inventory planning, logistics coordination, implementation support, and reliable supply chain execution across complex packaging programs.


We work directly with end users while often fulfilling through broadline distributors and other channel partners. That makes pricing and profitability more complex than simple product markup. Good commercial decisions require clear assumptions around landed cost, freight, warehousing, inventory exposure, customer commitments, distributor requirements, and cost-to-serve.


About the Role

We are looking for a commercially minded, analytical, and detail-oriented Commercial Pricing & Profitability Analyst to support pricing, margin analysis, opportunity economics, account profitability, inventory economics, working-capital exposure, and commercial risk analysis.


This role sits at the intersection of Sales/Account Management, Sourcing, Operations, Supply Chain, Finance, and leadership. You will gather inputs from across the business, validate assumptions, maintain pricing and margin tools, run scenarios, and prepare clear analysis before customer opportunities move into proposal, approval, or execution.


This is not a sales role and not a traditional accounting or FP&A role. It is a hands-on commercial decision-support role for someone who enjoys pricing, profitability, cost analysis, scenario modeling, and practical business problem-solving.


What You’ll Do

  • Support pricing, margin, cost, inventory, and profitability analysis for quotes, proposals, RFQs/RFPs, renewals, pricing schedules, and commercial decisions.
  • Use pricing models to calculate landed cost, delivered cost, gross margin, contribution margin, CMROI, and opportunity economics.
  • Gather, develop, and validate cost assumptions related to product cost, freight, warehousing, inventory, fulfillment, service-level requirements, and cost-to-serve.
  • Conduct scenario modeling and commercial trade-off analysis to inform pricing decisions, account planning, customer opportunities, renewals, and negotiation preparation.
  • Identify commercial risks, margin issues, pricing inconsistencies, cost changes, and questionable assumptions before customer commitments are made.
  • Maintain pricing models, customer pricing records, pricing templates, margin reports, and commercial support tools.
  • Track cost fluctuations, freight changes, pricing deviations, margin erosion, and variances between pricing assumptions and actual results.
  • Analyze margin and profitability by customer, SKU, opportunity, account, and portfolio, and prepare clear recommendations for leadership review.
  • Support reporting and analysis on aged inventory, slow-moving SKUs, customer-specific inventory exposure, inventory turns, and working-capital impact.
  • Help evaluate whether inventory commitments are justified by customer volume, margin, reliability requirements, strategic value, and commercial risk.


This role supports commercial analysis and recommendations; final decisions on pricing, inventory, contracts, and customer-facing commitments remain with leadership.


What We’re Looking For

  • 3–5 years of experience in pricing, commercial analysis, sales operations, business analysis, finance, supply chain analysis, proposal support, or a related role.
  • Strong Excel/spreadsheet skills, including modeling, formulas, lookups, pivot tables, pricing tables, and data analysis.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving ability.
  • Strong commercial judgment, including the ability to identify pricing, margin, cost, inventory, and risk issues that require further review.
  • High attention to detail and accuracy.
  • Strong teamwork and collaboration skills, with the ability to gather inputs, validate assumptions, and turn cross-functional information into clear, useful analysis.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.
  • Comfortable working with imperfect data, evolving processes, and hands-on analysis.
  • Comfortable working full-time in office in Vancouver.


Strong Assets

  • Experience in packaging, foodservice, CPG, distribution, wholesale, import/export, manufacturing, logistics, or supply chain.
  • Bachelor’s degree or diploma in business, finance, economics, accounting, supply chain, operations, or a related field.
  • Experience with NetSuite, Salesforce, HubSpot, Power BI, SAP, Cognos, or similar ERP/CRM/reporting systems.
  • Experience supporting pricing, quotes, RFQs/RFPs, customer profitability, margin analysis, or contract renewals.
  • Familiarity with landed costs, freight, warehousing, inventory, working capital, contribution margin, CMROI, or cost-to-serve analysis.
  • Experience analyzing customer-specific inventory, distributor fulfillment economics, or complex B2B supply programs.


Why Join Origin?

This is an opportunity to join a growing Canadian company at an important stage of its evolution. You will work directly with the CEO and cross-functionally with Sales/Account Management, Sourcing, Operations, Supply Chain, and Finance.


You will gain exposure to how enterprise customer programs are priced, planned, evaluated, launched, and managed — and help build the commercial decision-support discipline that enhances competitiveness and protects profitability as Origin grows.


How to Apply

Please apply with your resume and a brief note explaining why this role interests you and how your experience would help Origin improve pricing, profitability, commercial planning, and decision-making.

Title: Resource Strategy & Management Advisor – Supply Chain Business Intelligence Solutions Analyst
Description:
Job Location: Burnaby, BC

Employment type: Permanent

Hours of work: Full-time (37.5 hrs/week)

Annual Salary: $ 109,700.00 - 138,700.00

No. of positions: 1

Closing date: 2026-06-12




What you’ll do -




In this role, you will work in a dynamic, fast‑moving environment where priorities evolve, and high‑quality insights are critical. Your day will include maintaining, and continuously improving, existing Supply Chain reports, while working closely with business partners across varied business groups and levels of management to gather requirements for new reporting and respond to ad‑hoc information requests from executive leadership and government stakeholders.




You will regularly work with very large and complex data sets, applying a deep understanding of Procure‑to‑Pay (P2P) business processes and SAP system data to translate raw information into accurate, clear, actionable insights that support operational, financial, and compliance‑related decision‑making. You will also partner with the Procurement Policy team to develop data‑driven compliance insights that support policy interpretation, monitoring, and continuous improvement.




You will collaborate with a team of reporting professionals, contributing as a senior technical resource while fostering a culture of service excellence, continuous improvement, and knowledge sharing.




Through this work, you will play a key role in supporting BC Hydro’s Supply Chain reporting needs during a period of unprecedented growth, ensuring leaders have timely, reliable, and decision‑ready information to support the organization’s evolving priorities.




Responsibilities:




• Design and apply efficient, repeatable data processes to extract, model, transform, and analyze large, complex datasets from SAP and related systems, reducing manual effort and ensuring accurate, reliable, and auditable reporting.




• Manage and validate complex supply chain data from SAP Business Warehouse and other source systems, applying data management and statistical best practices to ensure data integrity, quality, and enterprise reliability.




• Design, develop, and maintain automated reports and dashboards using SAP Analytics Cloud, Power BI, and related tools, exercising professional judgment to replace manual reporting with scalable, user‑friendly solutions that deliver decision‑ready insights for leaders at all levels.




• Plan and execute User Acceptance Testing (UAT) for SAP BW report enhancements and Datasphere data model development, designing, documenting, and applying test scripts to validate large, complex data models and ensure data quality, governance compliance, and readiness for enterprise use.




• Develop a strong understanding of organizational objectives, priorities, and end‑to‑end business and system processes to define meaningful, decision‑supporting metrics aligned with operational and strategic outcomes.




• Independently partner with Supply Chain stakeholders to uncover, define, document, and prioritize new and evolving reporting requirements, translating business needs into clear, user‑focused reporting solutions and formal requirements artifacts.




• Apply advanced analytical, statistical, and business process judgment to respond to time‑sensitive ad hoc analysis and reporting requests, including those from executive and senior leadership, by validating outputs and clearly communicating findings.




• Support reporting initiatives end‑to‑end from intake through deployment and adoption by leading intake, prioritization, and scoping of reporting requests; developing clear technical and end‑user documentation; delivering training; supporting change management; and driving continuous improvement.




• Contribute as a senior technical professional by exercising sound judgment, collaborating across functions, and supporting reporting governance, standards, and adoption.




• Ensure the sustainability and governance of reporting solutions by maintaining trusted reporting assets and documentation, supporting enhancements and knowledge transfer, and ensuring alignment with organizational standards, policies, and audit requirements.




What you bring –




• Education: Undergraduate degree in Business, Supply Chain, Finance, Information Systems, Data Science, Engineering, or a related field; graduate degree and/or relevant technical certifications (e.g., Lean Six Sigma, PMI, DAMA, CDMP, CBAP, CSCP) considered an asset.




• Experience: 7+years of progressive experience in analytics, performance measurement, or data‑enabled supply chain environments, including the applied use Procure‑to‑Pay (P2P) process knowledge to deliver enterprise‑level analytical and reporting outcomes and a demonstrated ability to design, standardize, and improve reporting processes that enhance consistency, reliability, and decision support.




• Advanced Analytics & Platforms: Hands‑on expertise with enterprise BI tools—particularly Power BI—including the ability to interpret, audit, and troubleshoot complex DAX, M‑code, and data‑model logic. Experience with SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) is a strong asset; ability to learn and validate SAC calculation logic is required.




• Enterprise Data & Governance Leadership: Strong experience applying data governance practices, including data quality management, reporting standards, documentation, and stewardship and formal testing practices within ERP and BI environments supporting data integrity, auditability, and defensible reporting.




• User Acceptance Testing & Data Validation: Demonstrated experience planning and executing User Acceptance Testing (UAT) for SAP Business Warehouse report enhancements and Datasphere data model development, including designing, documenting, and applying test scripts to validate large, complex data models and ensure accuracy, integrity, and business readiness.




• Data Modeling & Technical Collaboration: Strong understanding of dimensional data modeling and experience partnering with IT and data engineering teams to design, validate, and sustain scalable, governed supply‑chain data models. Proficiency in SQL is required; familiarity with scripting languages (e.g., Python or R) for data validation or automation is an asset.




• Continuous Improvement & Innovation: Proven ability to identify and implement opportunities to improve analytics capability, efficiency, and data maturity through best practices, automation, and emerging analytical techniques.




• Stakeholder Engagement & Business Partnership: Ability to independently build strong partnerships with business stakeholders, understand evolving information needs, and co‑create analytics solutions that support enterprise‑wide decision‑making and measurable outcomes.




• Communication & Influence: Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to clearly frame complex analytical concepts and insights into accessible, compelling narratives for technical and non‑technical audiences.




What we offer –




- A comprehensive benefits package

- A minimum of 15 paid vacation days

- An annual 8% flexible benefits credit to allocate toward vacation, benefits, or income.

- A lifetime pension

- Flexible work model, depending on your role type

- Training and development courses




For more information on the benefits we offer, visit bchydro.com/benefits.




What else you should know –




Don't forget to update your Candidate Profile with your current resume and copies of your certifications. This will ensure we have all the necessary information to assess your application without any delays.




How to apply –




Interested candidates should submit their applications online at https://app.bchydro.com/careers/current_opp.html or click ‘Apply’

You must use a supported browser, such as Firefox, Internet Explorer, Google Chrome or Safari. Your pop up blocker will also need to be disabled for the BC Hydro Careers site.

On the BC Hydro Careers site, click on the Apply button in order to complete the steps to apply for this job. Please be sure to update your Candidate Profile with your current resume and include copies of your certifications, if applicable.

Title: Analytics Architect
Description:
We are hiring an Analytics Architect on behalf of a well-established organization in Vancouver.




The role will be apart of the Finance team and will help build and improve company’s analytics foundation. The Analytics Architect will design, build, and maintain reliable and scalable datasets that support self-service reporting, decision-making, automation, and future AI/ML initiatives. In this role, you will work closely with the business and take ownership of key financial and operational metrics, will build dependable data pipelines and curated datasets/scalable analytics data products that serve as a single, trusted source of data across the organization, play a key role in moving existing SQL Server data mart to the cloud, bringing in new data sources, and making sure analysts and business users have access to high-quality, well-structured data to support decision-making.




Your responsibilities:




Partner with AI Solutions, FP&A, and business stakeholders to deliver reliable, business-ready data products
Actively participate in business requirements discussions
Build and maintain data pipelines that integrate information from multiple business systems
Integrate data from different business systems into clean and trusted analytics-ready datasets
Create and maintain curated datasets that serve as the trusted source for company-wide KPIs
Implement automated data quality checks and resolve data issues to ensure accuracy and reliability
Use modern engineering practices—including CI/CD, automation, testing, and version control— to build and deliver reliable analytics solutions
Support self-service reporting and semantic models in Power BI
Partner with IT to ensure appropriate access controls in place and aligned with enterprise standards




Your experience and skills:




Post-secondary degree in a relevant field (Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, Business, Economics) or equivalent practical experience
5+ years of experience in data and analytics engineering and exposure to data architecture design
Strong SQL and Python skills, with experience in Microsoft SQL Server
Experience building data pipelines and integrating data from multiple systems
Experience designing and maintaining analytics or semantic models (e.g., Power BI)
Experience with cloud data platforms (Databricks or Microsoft Fabric preferred)
Experience with CI/CD, source control, and deployment practices (e.g., Azure DevOps)
Strong ability to translate technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders

Title: Asset Information System Analyst 2 (FTT)
Description:
Job Location: Burnaby, British Columbia, Canada V3N 4X8

Employment type: Temporary

Hours of work: Full-time (37.5 hrs/wk)

Annual Salary: $ $ 70,800.00 - 76,300.00

No. of positions: 1

Closing date: June 24, 2026




About the job:

Job Location: Edmonds B03

Region: Lower Mainland

Flexible Work Role: Hybrid




What you’ll do

• Performs the duties of the Asset Information System Analyst 1 job : (a) Prepares training materials and delivers training courses to end-users; (b) Delivers training and mentors team members; (c) Analyzes business requirements, prepares reports, analyzes the results, and makes recommendations for improvements; (d) Deals directly with first and third tier service providers such as, Fujitsu to ensure end-user problems have been resolved in a satisfactory and timely manner; (e) Prepares and delivers end-user communication as required by business owners and technical owners; (f) Directs and performs complex testing for application fault analysis, analyzes the results, and makes recommendations for changes; (g) Implements complex system administration activities under the general supervision of the System Administrator using a high security clearance; (h) Manages departmental records and documentation; (i) Manages complex business reporting requirements using advanced reporting applications; (j) Co-ordinates the publishing of information to the intranet / SharePoint/databases; and (k) Ensures applications, data and processes meet compliance requirements (i.e. NERC and CIP, PCBs, etc.).

• Technical owner for Stations/Lines work management data (project costs, estimate costs, hours, etc.) and asset management data (equipment records, equipment failure records, etc.). Researches sources of data, analyzes the plausibility and quality of the data and where there are errors or anomalies, investigates root cause of problem, change sources and advises IT of correct data field to be used. Ensures reports are extracting right data and report information is correct and valid. Develops work processes and business rules to enable service providers and internal users to correctly apply asset management support systems. Prepares and maintains various training materials and helpdesk manuals. 3. Coordinates maintenance policies and standards within the asset information system by: (a) Implementing and revising maintenance programs/instructions within the system for all Stations/Lines equipment; and (b) Coordinating policy and standard structure.

• Ensures applications adhere to business process requirements, meets compliance, functional and technical requirements by: a) Pro-actively identifying and resolving business issues and, where required, providing recommendations for resolution to management; b) Dealing directly with various business offices and staff to gather and review recommendations and implement appropriate solutions to ensure the success of business systems.

• Is considered a Subject Matter Expert (SME) when working with a team to develop and implement new or revised business information system applications and as such: (a) May act as a "task manager" or participate on a project team to coordinate and facilitate changes and enhancements to existing or new applications; (b) Ensures second tier application support personnel deliver solutions that meet agreed-to business requirements; and (c) Represents the interests of Stations/Lines to ensure business needs are met.

• Assists in development and implementation of pilot and new computer systems for a variety of Asset Management Services applications by liaising with other departments and outside consultants, controlling project scheduling, analyzing and controlling project costs, resolving conflicts and approving work estimates within assigned limits. Tasks include researching and documenting user requirements; devising alternate solutions and recommend best approach to address issue; providing and/or collecting estimate for proposed solutions; provide assistance in business case preparation; coordinating with system developers and users; testing new system before actual implementation; assisting in system roll- out.

• Provides technical advice and guidance to incumbents in the Asset Information System Analyst 1 job, other specialists temporarily assigned to projects, and to lower-level support staff.

• Performs duties of a minor nature related to the above duties that do not affect the rating of the job.




What you bring

• Diploma of Technology from an Institute of Technology in Computer Systems and: either four (4) years of experience as an Asset Information System Analyst 1 or four (4) years of experience analyzing large and complex datasets. OR

• Six (6) years of equivalent work experience includes experience working with asset information and work management systems, budgeting, processes and reporting systems including providing front line support, user training and participation in system development projects. Of the six (6) years of experience, three (3) years of experience must include working with work management systems.




What we offer

• A comprehensive benefits package - A minimum of 15 paid vacation days - A lifetime pension - Flexible work model, depending on your role type - Training and development courses For more information on the benefits we offer, visit bchydro.com/benefits.




For more information on the benefits we offer, visit bchydro.com/benefits.




What else you should know

• This position is affiliated with the Movement of United Professionals union (MoveUP/COPE). http://moveuptogether.ca

• In-office presence required a minimum of 2 days per week

• This is a 2-year full-time temporary role based out of our Edmonds location; however, other locations may be considered, subject to seating availability

• Experience with SAP FIORI and SAP GUI is preferred

• Experience with Asset Hierarchy and Master Data maintenance in SAP EAM is an asset

• Experience with Jira Service Management is an asset

Title: CRM & Data Manager
Description:
Join a Mission-Driven Organization Creating Real Impact Across British Columbia!




Our client is a rapidly growing BC-based nonprofit organization focused on addressing childhood food insecurity across the province.




As the organization enters an exciting new phase of operational and fundraising growth, they are seeking a collaborative, systems-minded, and data-driven Data & CRM Manager to lead and evolve their fundraising systems and donor data environment.




This is a highly visible role with genuine ownership, influence, and long-term growth potential.







Why This Role Matters




This position will serve as the primary owner and strategic lead for the organization’s CRM ecosystem, donor data infrastructure, reporting environment, and fundraising operations workflows.




The successful candidate will play a foundational role in:

Supporting a major CRM migration initiative
Improving fundraising operations and reporting
Building scalable systems and workflows
Enhancing automation and operational efficiency
Helping the organization become more data-informed as it scales




This is far more than a database administration role - it is a strategic infrastructure hire for the organization.







What You’ll Be Doing




CRM & Systems Leadership

Own and administer the organization’s CRM platform
Manage workflows, integrations, permissions, and system improvements
Support CRM migration and optimization initiatives
Build scalable processes and documentation




Data Governance & Reporting

Maintain donor database integrity and accuracy
Oversee imports, exports, deduplication, and cleanup
Build reports, dashboards, and fundraising analytics
Translate data into actionable insights for leadership and fundraising teams




Fundraising Operations & Collaboration

Support campaign tracking, stewardship, and donation workflows
Improve automation and operational efficiencies
Train and support non-technical CRM users
Collaborate closely with fundraising, finance, and leadership teams







Who We’re Looking For




The ideal candidate likely brings:

3–7+ years of CRM, donor database, or fundraising operations experience
Experience working with nonprofit fundraising systems
Strong reporting, analytics, and systems-thinking capability
Advanced Excel and data management skills
Experience with CRM migrations, implementations, or workflow optimization
Strong collaboration, communication, and problem-solving skills
High initiative and ownership mentality




Experience with the following platforms is considered highly valuable:

Virtuous
Keela
Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud
Raiser’s Edge
DonorPerfect
HubSpot
Power BI
API integrations and workflow automation tools




Nonprofit experience is strongly preferred, though technically strong candidates from adjacent sectors with strong mission alignment will also be considered.







Culture & Work Environment

Our client offers a collaborative, flexible, and highly team-oriented culture where people genuinely care about both the mission and each other. The organization values initiative, autonomy, teamwork, transparency, and continuous improvement.




Highlights include:

Hybrid work flexibility (WFH Mondays & Fridays)
3 weeks paid vacation
Additional paid holiday shutdown periods
Strong work/life balance
Long-term growth opportunities
Meaningful, mission-driven work
Dog friendly office







Total Rewards

Target base salary range: $70,000 – $90,000 CAD
Comprehensive health and dental benefits package
Flexible hybrid work environment
3 weeks paid vacation PLUS 2 weeks of additional paid holiday during office shutdown periods
Opportunity to help shape infrastructure within a rapidly scaling organization
Strong long-term growth opportunity as the organization continues to expand







The Opportunity

This is a rare opportunity to join a purpose-driven organization at a pivotal stage of growth and help build the systems, infrastructure, and operational maturity required to scale long term.




For someone who enjoys ownership, systems thinking, collaboration, and mission-driven work, this role offers the chance to make a meaningful and measurable impact across communities throughout British Columbia!

Title: Manager, RBT2 Performance Analytics and Information Management
Description:
Detailed Job Description

JOIN US IN ENABLING TRADE THROUGH CANADA'S LARGEST PORT.

The Vancouver Fraser Port Authority is an arm’s-length federal agency responsible for the shared stewardship of the lands and waters that make up the Port of Vancouver, Canada’s largest port. Our mandate, as outlined in the Canada Marine Act, is to enable Canada’s trade objectives on behalf of all Canadians, ensuring the safe movement of goods through the Port of Vancouver while protecting the environment and considering local communities.

As an organization with a significant impact on communities and businesses across Canada, the Vancouver Fraser Port Authority is dedicated to diversity, inclusion, and sustainability. We strive to create a workplace that mirrors the richness of our community, empowering all employees to achieve their full potential.

The Manager, RBT2 Performance Analytics & Information Management is accountable for leading best-in-class information capture, management, and performance analytics functions across a multi-billion-dollar, multi-project capital infrastructure program.

The role ensures that high-quality, integrated, and timely information is collected, structured, and made available across all workstreams, delivery partners, and contractors to support rapid, evidence-based decision-making. This position enables program leadership to make informed decisions at pace, supporting the successful delivery of the program on schedule, within budget, and in alignment with safety, quality, environmental and other project objectives.

Working within a matrixed, multi-party delivery environment, the role establishes a robust end-to-end information and analytics ecosystem—from field and contractor data capture through to executive-level reporting—ensuring a single source of truth, proactive issue identification, and a “no surprises” culture across the program lifecycle.

The RBT2 team have established an environment where there is a focus on delivering at pace, being forward-thinking and ensuring alignment and a positive culture in order to bring the world more Canada, together.

What You Do


Set direction and establish and enforce program-wide standards for information capture, ensuring data from contractors, consultants, and internal teams is timely, complete, consistent and structured for downstream analytics and reporting
Ensure clear definition of what data is required, who provides it, how often, and in what format (aligned to contractual obligations and program needs) to support project success
Establish and regulate disciplined information governance frameworks for data ownership and accountability, data quality assurance and validation, and auditability and traceability
Ensure all program information supports decision-making requirements, regulatory compliance, assurance, claims, and dispute processes.
Provide oversight of the integration of field data, contractor systems, design data, cost, schedule, risk, and commercial systems into a unified information ecosystem (leveraging a Connected Data Environment)
Set the approach for controlled information exchange across delivery partners, ensuring the right information reaches the right stakeholders at the right time
Oversee and monitor compliance with information requirements and continuously improve workflows to reduce friction, duplication, and rework
Drive a culture of information discipline and accountability across all delivery participants
Establish and lead a program-wide performance analytics function that transforms raw data into actionable insight
Provide leadership and direction in translating complex data into clear, concise insights that highlight key risks and deviations from plan, root causes and systemic issues and required decisions and actions
Ensure executive, program leadership and governance forums have access to real-time, evidence-based analytics and reporting to support rapid, traceable and confident decision-making
Oversee the development and maintenance of integrated dashboards and reporting that highlight leading indicators and provide clear visibility of:
schedule performance and critical path risks
cost performance and forecast trends
safety performance indicators
quality and non-conformance trends
environmental compliance and performance
First Nations commitments compliance and performance
Accountable for ensuring available information is timely, reliable, and provides decision-focused insights, with traceability to source data and clear link to implications and recommended actions
Enable forward-looking analytics, including trend analysis, early warning indicators and scenario and sensitivity insights
Drive alignment with Program Controls and functional teams to ensure analytics are consistent, integrated, and trusted across the program
Accountable for the integrity, availability, structure, traceability and effective use of program data to enable analytics and reporting; provides insight and decision support but does not own cost, schedule, risk performance outcomes or forecasts
Responsible for the program-wide systems strategy covering project management systems, document management, collaboration platforms, and data repositories. Critically evaluate expert / SME recommendations and ensure the systems are fit for use for the needs of the diverse delivery environment of RBT2
Ensure governance frameworks for system access, data ownership, data quality controls, and auditability (including clear RACI across internal teams and delivery partners) are maintained and effective
Ensure effective integration across program systems and tools, including, but not limited to:
document management
cost tracking and forecasting
scheduling and planning
risk and commercial
safety, quality and environmental compliance
Indigenous consultation
Oversee and drive automation of data workflows to reduce manual intervention, improve timeliness of reporting and enhance data consistency and integrity
Partner with IS and vendors to ensure system reliability, compliance with applicable policies (e.g. cybersecurity and records management), and scalability
Accountable for system readiness for major program gates (financial close, procurement milestones, regulatory submissions, substantial completion)
Lead continuous improvement initiatives to enhance usability, reporting capability, and data quality
Oversee the program’s Connected Data Environment (CDE), ensuring it supports:
Appropriate access and permissions for consultants and contractors
Secure and controlled information exchange
Accurate and timely upload of information from contractors’ systems
Scalability over long program durations
Oversee onboarding, training, and ongoing support for contractors, consultants, and internal teams using the CDE; monitor adoption and compliance, and continuously improve workflows based on user feedback
Ensure all systems align with:
Contractual requirements
Applicable VFPA policies and standards including cybersecurity and records management
Applicable regulatory obligations (including audit and Freedom of Information considerations).
Ensure a best-in-class document management framework is maintained with complete and auditable records to support claims management, dispute resolution, and third-party reviews are available
Accountable for program-wide information management and performance analytics, ensuring consistent, responsive, and service-oriented delivery
Facilitate integration and drive alignment across functional teams (Program Controls, Engineering, Construction, Environmental, First Nations, Commercial) to ensure integrated reporting and decision-making



What You Bring


Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (civil, mechanical, electrical, or systems), Construction Management, Infrastructure Project Management, Information Systems, Data Analytics, or Computer Science, or a related discipline combining technical, analytical and delivery expertise, plus 5+ years of progressively senior experience in major/mega project capital project delivery (mega project delivery >$1B, preferred) and in overseeing or implementing capital infrastructure project information management and/or project performance analytics and reporting; or an equivalent combination of education and experience .
Must demonstrate a proven track record of enabling data-driven decision-making, improving project performance outcomes (cost, schedule, safety, etc.) and delivering integrated reporting and analytics capability.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office applications.
Experience with InEight and Primavera P6 is an asset.



What We Offer


Market-competitive base salaries and variable incentive plan
Generous and inclusive benefits offering including retirement benefits plan
Flexible remote work program
Earned time off program
Paid vacation and personal care leave
Learning, development, and advancement opportunities
Onsite gym with towel service in a national landmark in the heart of Vancouver’s waterfront
Friendly, unique, and respectful workplace



Location: Vancouver, Canada

Job type: Exempt, Full-Time, Permanent, Hybrid

Working environment: Normal office conditions.

MARINE TRANSPORTATION SECURITY CLEARANCE

The incumbent must obtain and maintain a valid Marine Transportation Security Clearance as this position is required to access restricted areas/information as defined by the Marine Transportation Security Regulations.

This is Canadian-based employment, and it is expected that all employees maintain legal entitlement to work in Canada. Applicants selected to move forward in the hiring process may be subject to background checks, including but not limited to criminal record, credit, and/or reference checks.

At the Vancouver Fraser Port Authority, we are dedicated to employment equity and a workplace reflective of the diversity of our port community. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates including women, persons with disabilities, Indigenous peoples, and members of visible minorities. Reasonable accommodation can be provided in the selection process and while working with the port authority. If you require support applying to our openings, please contact us at careers@portvancouver.com.

We recognize and acknowledge that we are located on the traditional territories of the xʷməθkʷəy̓əm (Musqueam), Sḵwx̱wú7mesh (Squamish), and səlilwətaɬ (Tsleil-Waututh) Nations. To learn more about our indigenous relations and commitment to reconciliation, please visit our website.

Title: Senior Technical Analyst - Data Warehouse Developer
Description:
YVR is a place of connection, bringing together people, places and cargo while ensuring a safe and efficient experience for everyone. As part of our Innovation and Technology Group, your work will help deliver industry-leading travel technology to transform the passenger experience. From strategic planning to project execution, the work you do matters and will help improve our processes to make our airport work better for our communities.

Position Overview

We have a full-time opportunity for an innovative Senior Technical Analyst – Data Warehouse Developer. This role will be part of the Advanced Analytics team, seeking a skilled Data Warehouse Developer with expertise in Azure Databricks technologies. If you are passionate about data, enjoy hands-on experience in data modelling, and have a successful track record of migrating from on-premises data warehouses to the cloud, we want to hear from you.

Key Responsibilities Include


Lead the design and development of scalable, high-performance data warehouse and Lakehouse solutions using Azure Databricks, Microsoft Fabric, and hybrid/on-premises environments, leveraging best practices in data modeling and architecture.
Design and implement end-to-end data pipelines (batch and streaming) using modern ELT frameworks, including medallion architecture (bronze/silver/gold), ensuring data quality, reliability, and scalability.
Develop and maintain data architecture roadmaps, align platform capabilities with evolving enterprise architecture, governance standards, and business objectives.
Collaborate with business and technical stakeholders to gather requirements and translate them into robust, maintainable data solutions, effectively communicating complex concepts to diverse audiences.
Establish and enforce data governance, security, and quality standards, including data lineage, access controls, and compliance with organizational policies.
Optimize data processing and storage performance, including cost management and query tuning across cloud and on-prem platforms.
Implement and support CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices for data engineering workflows, including version control, automated testing, and deployment.
Prepare and maintain comprehensive technical documentation, including architecture diagrams, data models, and operational procedures.


Key Qualifications Include


Minimum bachelor’s degree in computer science, or equivalent, or comparable education with minimum 10 years’ post degree experience working with data warehouse and developing business intelligence solutions.
Knowledge and expertise with virtual server platforms, storage devices, cloud computing, relational databases and Data and Analytics.
Good experience with data prep, data integration (ETL), data modelling, along with proficiency in working with Azure Databricks, experience with SQL and Python
Experience in project, vendor and relationship management including mentoring junior resources.
Excellent English verbal and written communication skills, including ability to communicate at all levels of the organization, from end users to senior leaders.
Expert level knowledge of technologies underlying assigned systems as well as expert level of critical thinking, analytical skills and troubleshooting capability.


Although not required, French language skills are an asset as we are committed to providing bilingual services to our visitors and passengers. This helps us meet federal language obligations while ensuring a elcoming experience. Candidates who do not speak French are still strongly encouraged to apply.

This position is open to both Vancouver Airport Authority employees and external applicants. Previous job performance will be taken into consideration for all internal candidates that apply for this position.

As part of our recruitment process, short-listed candidates may be required to participate in an assessment process.

Salary Range : $102,693 to $134,213 per annum, based on 37.5-hour work week. This is individually tailored to reflect your unique experience, qualifications, and internal equity. At YVR, your work experience goes beyond the paycheck. We support your personal and professional development, well-being, and a thriving work culture with generous vacation days, extensive health benefits, retirement savings matching, wellness programs, community engagement, commuting support, and continuous learning opportunities. Learn more at https://careers.yvr.ca/benefits

Who We Are

YVR is more than just an airport. We connect our beautiful province and all it has to offer to the world. We are all leaders and trailblazers for change and innovation, so no matter the department or team you’re a part of, the work you do matters.

At YVR, we are flexible in everything we do. We will work together to find ways to deliver customer excellence that helps us all thrive.

Whatever your background and wherever you’re from, you belong at YVR. If you have any questions about accessibility or require any assistance applying, please reach out at careers@yvr.ca.

  • Excellent English verbal and written communication skills, including ability to communicate at all levels of the organization, from end users to senior leaders.
  • Expert level knowledge of technologies underlying assigned systems as well as expert level of critical thinking, analytical skills and troubleshooting capability.

Although not required, French language skills are an asset as we are committed to providing bilingual services to our visitors and passengers. This helps us meet federal language obligations while ensuring a elcoming experience. Candidates who do not speak French are still strongly encouraged to apply.

This position is open to both Vancouver Airport Authority employees and external applicants. Previous job performance will be taken into consideration for all internal candidates that apply for this position.

As part of our recruitment process, short-listed candidates may be required to participate in an assessment process.

Salary Range : $102,693 to $134,213 per annum, based on 37.5-hour work week. This is individually tailored to reflect your unique experience, qualifications, and internal equity. At YVR, your work experience goes beyond the paycheck. We support your personal and professional development, well-being, and a thriving work culture with generous vacation days, extensive health benefits, retirement savings matching, wellness programs, community engagement, commuting support, and continuous learning opportunities. Learn more at https://careers.yvr.ca/benefits

Who We Are

YVR is more than just an airport. We connect our beautiful province and all it has to offer to the world. We are all leaders and trailblazers for change and innovation, so no matter the department or team you’re a part of, the work you do matters.

At YVR, we are flexible in everything we do. We will work together to find ways to deliver customer excellence that helps us all thrive.

Whatever your background and wherever you’re from, you belong at YVR. If you have any questions about accessibility or require any assistance applying, please reach out at careers@yvr.ca.

Title: Senior Analyst, Location Intelligence
Description:
Job Summary

We are seeking a highly skilled and detail-oriented Senior GIS Analyst to support and advance the production of sophisticated geographic, demographic, and market intelligence that informs commercial real estate strategy and decision making.

In this senior role, you will work independently and collaboratively with other GIS professionals to analyze, process, and manage large, complex geospatial datasets. You will play a key role in shaping best practices for spatial data management, automation, QA/QC, and analytical workflows. The position requires strong logical thinking, organization, and technical depth, with the ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights.This role is ideal for a GIS professional who stays current with emerging GIS trends, AI applications, data automation, and big data technologies, and who enjoys improving systems, workflows, and outcomes - not just producing maps.

Essential Duties And Responsibilities


Lead the creation of high-quality cartographic and analytical deliverables using the ESRI platform, including advanced static maps, interactive web maps, dashboards, and applications.
Analyze and manage large-scale geospatial and demographic datasets, ensuring data accuracy, consistency, and performance across workflows.
Design, implement, and maintain ETL workflows to extract, transform, and integrate data from multiple internal and external sources using tools such as FME and Python.
Apply and enforce best practices for spatial data governance, including metadata standards, data models, version control, update strategies, and QA/QC protocols.
Proactively research and evaluate new datasets, tools, and technologies (including AI/ML in GIS) to improve analytical capability and efficiency.
Develop and maintain reusable data products, templates, scripts, and standardized deliverables to support consistent output across regions and projects.
Support the ongoing maintenance and optimization of enterprise geospatial databases used in commercial real estate analysis.
Provide technical guidance, knowledge sharing, and mentorship to junior or intermediate GIS team members as needed.
Collaborate with cross‑functional partners to identify analytical needs and deliver solutions that support client and business objectives.
Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives by identifying inefficiencies and proposing scalable, automated solutions.
Perform additional responsibilities as assigned in support of the overall success of the GIS and Location Intelligence team.


Qualifications

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

Education and/or Experience


Bachelor’s degree in Geography, GIS, Geomatics, Spatial Data Science, or a related field.
5+ years of progressive professional GIS experience, including work with complex datasets and production environments.


Technical Expertise


Advanced proficiency with the ESRI platform (ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Online, web applications, services, and data management).
Strong experience with spatial data analysis, data modeling, and geospatial workflows.
Demonstrated experience working with large, multi-source datasets and enterprise data environments.


Core Competencies


Exceptional attention to detail, organizational skills, and commitment to data quality.
Strong logical reasoning and analytical problem-solving abilities.
Ability to work independently, prioritize effectively, and manage multiple concurrent initiatives.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts clearly.
Conducts basic financial analysis.


HIGHLY DESIRED/KEY ASSETS


Strong hands-on experience with FME for enterprise-level data transformation and automation.
Experience using ArcGIS API for Python to automate workflows, manage content, and support web GIS operations.
Solid understanding of big data concepts, scalable data processing, and performance considerations.
Demonstrated awareness or application of AI/ML in GIS, spatial analytics, or advanced data processing techniques.
Experience integrating GIS with enterprise databases, data warehouses, or cloud-based data platforms.
Exposure to 3D data, spatial modeling, or advanced analytical methods is an asset.
Knowledge of commercial real estate (CRE) data, metrics, and market analysis strongly preferred.


Other Skills & Abilities


Strong cartographic design sense, spatial awareness, and understanding of visual best practices.
Ability to interpret and synthesize complex data, charts, and analytical outputs.
Proficiency with spreadsheet, presentation, and documentation tools.
Ability to apply mathematical and statistical concepts to analytical tasks.


SCOPE OF RESPONSIBILITY

Works with a high degree of independence while aligning with departmental standards and objectives.

Responsible for planning and executing assigned analytical and production tasks, including setting timelines and priorities.

Decisions and outputs may have moderate to significant impact on team deliverables, data quality, and client outcomes.

CBRE carefully considers multiple factors to determine compensation, including a candidate’s education, training, and experience. The salary being offered for this position is $74,000-$82,000 annually. This role will provide the following benefits: generous paid time off, Group Retirement Program, Medical, Dental and Vision coverage, Life Insurance and more.

Equal Employment Opportunity Statement: CBRE is an equal opportunity employer who welcomes and encourages application from all persons regardless of race, creed, ancestry, national or ethnic origin, colour, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, family status, gender identity or expression, disability or any other status or condition protected by applicable federal, provincial or territorial law.

Candidate Accommodations: CBRE values the differences of all current and prospective employees and recognizes how every employee contributes to our company’s success. CBRE provides reasonable accommodations in job application procedures for individual with disabilities. If you require assistance due to a disability in the application or recruitment process, please submit a request via email at recruitingaccommodations@cbre.com or via telephone at Via telephone at +1 866 388 4346 (Canada) and +1 866 225 3099 (U.S.)

Title: Database & GIS Administrator
Description:
Reporting to the Director, Exploration, the Exploration Database and GIS Administrator and dotted line to the Manager, GIS & Database based in Vancouver, is responsible for the management, integrity, security, and effective use of geological, drilling, sampling, spatial, and exploration datasets across the Company’s Ontario assets. The role provides technical leadership and oversight in database administration, GIS systems, data governance, and digital workflows to support exploration decision-making, resource growth, and efficient program execution.

The position works closely with exploration, technical services, consultants, and external service providers to ensure timely delivery of accurate and accessible data products, mapping, and reporting tools. This position is based in Vancouver, BC or Ontario, Canada, with occasional travel to project sites.




Lead the management and continuous improvement of exploration database, GIS, and digital information systems across the Company’s Ontario assets
Ensure integrity, security, governance, and accessibility of geological, drilling, sampling, assay, and spatial data
Establish and maintain data standards, validation controls, and best practices for technical data management and reporting
Oversee delivery of accurate and timely datasets, maps, dashboards, and reporting tools to support exploration planning and decision-making
Support exploration program execution through effective field data capture, sample tracking, and integration with modelling and technical workflows
Coordinate compilation, validation, and delivery of data for internal stakeholders, consultants, auditors, due diligence reviews, and technical studies
Lead GIS management of spatial datasets including tenure, geology, infrastructure, environmental, and planning layers
Maintain technical records, document control systems, and historical exploration archives
Ensure compliance with corporate standards, regulatory requirements, and industry best practices
Support preparation of regular management and technical reporting packages
Identify opportunities to strengthen the Company’s digital exploration capability and data-driven culture
Perform other duties as assigned




Required Experience and Skills




Strong understanding of drilling, sampling, geological logging, survey control, and assay workflow
3+ years of experience in mineral exploration, mining, database management, GIS, or related technical role
Proficiency with industry software including Leapfrog, ArcGIS Pro, QGI
Advanced proficiency with Microsoft 365, Excel, and data reporting tool
Experience with SQL, scripting, automation, or Power BI is an asset
Strong organizational skills with high attention to detail and data accuracy
Strong communication skills and ability to work across technical and operational team
Experience in remote or operating mine environments is an asset

Title: Business Systems Support Analyst
Description:
Business System Support Analyst

Vancouver BC(Hybrid)

Job Summary

Join a global mining technology leader where you'll play a pivotal role in improving the business systems that power operations worldwide, while partnering with stakeholders across the business to drive meaningful change and continuous improvement.

Key Selling Points


Work across enterprise systems including NetSuite, CRM and Business Intelligence platforms in a global technology environment
Influence business process improvement and digital transformation initiatives with direct exposure to senior stakeholders
Grow your career within a global organisation backed by Weir, supporting mining operations around the world


About Us

We are the world’s leading mining software business bringing together the expertise of Micromine, Motion Metrics and Fast2Mine.

Our solutions help mining companies explore, design, plan and operate more effectively through industry-leading software, AI, machine vision and automation technologies. From geology and mine planning to fleet performance and operational optimization, we help customers improve productivity, efficiency and sustainability across the mining value chain.

Backed by Weir's global reach and resources, we combine deep mining expertise with a strong innovation roadmap, delivering trusted technology solutions used by mining operations around the world.

The Opportunity

As our Business System Support Analyst, you'll become a trusted partner in enhancing the systems and processes that support a growing global business. This role offers the opportunity to combine technical problem-solving, business analysis and stakeholder engagement while contributing to projects that improve operational efficiency, user experience and business performance across multiple regions.

This will be part of Micromine.

The Impact you'll have


Help shape the effectiveness of critical business systems used across a global organisation.
Partner with stakeholders to identify opportunities that improve processes and business outcomes
Influence user adoption and system optimization through effective support and training
Contribute to business transformation initiatives that drive operational excellence
Build strong relationships across technical and non-technical teams globally
Develop expertise across enterprise applications while expanding your business analysis capability


What You Need To Succeed


3+ years of NetSuite or similar ERP systems supporting enterprise business systems
Strong business process analysis, process mapping and documentation skills
Proven ability to troubleshoot issues and translate technical concepts for diverse audiences
3+ years' experience in technical support, business systems support or a similar role
Experience gathering requirements and collaborating with stakeholders to improve business outcomes
A proactive, customer-focused mindset with strong communication and problem-solving capabilities


What You Can Expect From Us


A base salary range of CAD $90,000 to 100,000 CAD DoE
Extended health benefits, including allowances for family and wellness expenses
4 weeks paid Vacation and 2 weeks paid Personal Leave (i.e. Sick / Carer's Leave)
RRSP matching
Subsidized gym membership
Hybrid working arrangements to balance work and home life
Opportunities for professional development and progression
A team first culture and the chance to work within the mining technology industry


Apply Now

Are you ready to influence the systems, processes and user experience that support a global technology business? If you're passionate about solving complex problems, driving continuous improvement and creating meaningful business impact, we'd love to hear from you.

To learn more, visit our website at www.micromine.com or LinkedIn Life Page.

Weir Software Solutions is dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment, and we welcome applicants from a variety of backgrounds to apply.

#Hybrid

#Micromine

Title: Change Analyst
Description:
About Delta Intelligent Building Technologies (Canada) Inc. (formerly known as Delta Controls)

Delta Intelligent Building Technologies (Canada) Inc. is a leading building controls manufacturer. For over three decades, we have provided innovative building automation solutions for commercial, healthcare, education, and leisure facilities.

Job Summary

We are looking for a Change Analyst to support our product data and change management processes. You will help create and maintain product information, track design changes, and make sure all updates follow proper procedures. You will work closely with the Configuration Manager and cross-functional teams to keep our products accurate and up to date.

Responsibilities


Create, manage, and maintain product master data.
Identify and coordinate the daily management of configuration items, including creation of parts and bills of materials (BOMs).
Organize, control, and assist in the processing and implementation of changes.
Ensure version control processes, policies, and procedures are followed consistently.
Perform impact analyses and review design changes.
Assign approvers according to change type and ensure proper controls for data accuracy.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams and business partners to communicate and align on Design Change Requests and notifications.


Required Skills / Qualifications


1–2 years of experience as a Change Analyst.
Bachelor’s degree in a technical or business field.
Strong understanding of CMII principles and Fit, Form, and Function (FFF).
Experience using PLM/ERP tools.
Experience creating parts and bills of materials (BOMs) and managing changes.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent attention to detail, interpersonal, and written communication skills
Ability to work independently and meet deadlines with minimal supervision.
Preferred: Experience managing complex design changes and knowledge of hardware, software development, and manufacturing processes.


Work Location: Hybrid (In-office 3 days per week, subject to change)

City: Surrey

Type: Full-time, Permanent

Salary: $ 68,000 - $78,000 CAD

This range reflects the minimum and maximum target payable for the base salary of new hires across all Canadian locations. Actual compensation may vary outside this range and is dependent on various factors.

Why work for us?

We provide fantastic opportunities to individuals passionate about business and technology. Delta Electronics products are the most energy efficient power products in the industry and our company are perfect for those who want to create, design and build sustainable solutions using massively scalable, big-data architecture, with a strong focus on building automation. Here are some of the Delta perks you’ll receive from day 1!


Employee & safety centric culture with the possibility of remote work
Flexible hours and scheduling available
Competitive total compensation along with generous vacation allowances
Three weeks of vacation
In-house & external training provided along with education support and opportunities for growth
Company funded annual personal development budget
Financial benefits & planning such as group RRSP & company match
Employer paid premiums for extended healthcare and family-focused benefits (health, vision and dental)
Travel insurance coverage and access to the Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Wellness - onsite fitness center & company reimbursement for personal gym memberships
Internal Recognition & Service Awards
Employee Referral bonuses
Transit accessible with free onsite parking
Regular company events such as "Food Truck Fridays", Lunch & Learn and other planned activities

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
Saltworks Technologies is a leading Canadian cleantech company solving two of the world's most pressing industrial challenges: water sustainability and critical minerals supply. We design and build advanced industrial water treatment and lithium refining systems for some of the world's most recognized companies — in semiconductors, mining, automotive, and industrial processing. Saltworks is a 2025 Fast 50 Company and EY Entrepreneur of the Year award winner.

The Role

Saltworks is seeking a Business Analyst to support the continued growth of the organization. As the company scales, this role helps ensure that processes, systems, and ways of working evolve in a structured and practical way to support day‑to‑day operations and long‑term objectives.

Working closely with teams across the business, the Business Analyst will help bridge gaps between business needs and system capabilities, improving how work is planned, tracked, and executed. This role is suited to someone who enjoys working across functions, understands how systems and processes connect, and can help turn evolving requirements into solutions that drive consistency, visibility, and effective execution.

This is an on-site role at our office in Richmond, BC, with limited opportunity for remote work. You must be located in or be willing to relocate to the Lower Mainland.

Key Responsibilities


Work at the intersection of people, processes, and technology, balancing competing priorities while driving work that moves the business forward.
Lead business analysis efforts by identifying inefficiencies, bottlenecks, and manual workarounds, and translating findings into clear, actionable recommendations.
Analyze data and workflows with a focus on optimization, simplification, and measurable improvement.
Partner with stakeholders across the organization to gather requirements and feedback, translating them into well-defined functional specifications.
Identify and communicate priorities, risks, and resource needs to keep initiatives on track.
Design, test, and implement solutions, primarily within our project management platform (Wrike), ensuring changes are ready before go‑live.
Sustain and refine solutions after implementation through validation, user feedback, and lessons‑learned reviews.
Create and maintain clear process documentation to enable training, continuity, and compliance.


You Will Be a Great Fit If You Have


3–5 years of experience as a Business Analyst or in a similar role.
A degree in Engineering, Information Systems, Computer Science, Business, or a related field.
Strong experience translating complex business operations into structured, well-definedrequirements.
Solid experience with business process mapping and workflow documentation.
Experience delivering and implementing solutions across systems and processes.
The ability to think at a high level while remaining comfortable working through details.
Strong communication skills and the ability to work with both technical teams and business stakeholders.

Saltworks Technologies is a leading Canadian cleantech company solving two of the world's most pressing industrial challenges: water sustainability and critical minerals supply. Saltworks is a 2025 Fast 50 Company and EY Entrepreneur of the Year award winner.

Saltworks is seeking a Business Analyst to support the continued growth of the organization. As the company scales, this role helps ensure that processes, systems, and ways of working evolve in a structured and practical way to support day‑to‑day operations and long‑term objectives.

Working closely with teams across the business, the Business Analyst will help bridge gaps between business needs and system capabilities, improving how work is planned, tracked, and executed. This role is suited to someone who enjoys working across functions, understands how systems and processes connect, and can help turn evolving requirements into solutions that drive consistency, visibility, and effective execution.

This is an on-site role at our office in Richmond, BC, with limited opportunity for remote work. You must be located in or be willing to relocate to the Lower Mainland.
